## PAGE 00077: (2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `272741228`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741228/2022x+Refresh1+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

In the 2022x Refresh1 release, theimport of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF 1.2 projectand the element mapping are now completed. Moreover, the UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator was adapted for UAF 1.2. Finally, new parametric diagrams, notification improvements, and more reception property creation options were implemented.

As for SysML features, this new version introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update, so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.

The 2022x Refresh1 release brings improvements to the Data Markings and Classification Plugin. The plugin is now enhanced with the ability to display Containment Markings, perform Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation, and add Data Markings recursively.

The client-side performance improvements include the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality that will help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance caused by the execution of expressions. Additionally, the 2022x Refresh1 version provides a separate installation file for ARM architecture-based macOS (i.e., Apple M1)that allows the modeling tool to run up to 2-3 times faster than with the x86 build.

For general modeling, this release presents an improved error notification display, fully featuredStructured Expressions dialogs without the separate Standard/Expert UI modes,a new 'UI Font' environment option for changing the default application font, and an ability to create custom project disclaimer configurations.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is now improved with an ability to show custom centralized announcements in the modeling tool informing users on planned server-wide or project-specific events.

Last but not least, the 2022x Refresh1 release introduces the initial Power'by solution – an alternative collaborative modeling environment that is native to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform and provides an additional set of platform-specific capabilities such as advanced configuration management, traceability across disciplines, and others.

To download the latest version, see [Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2022xR2/Downloading+installation+files). For further information, check the product documentation.

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. Itincludes Collaboration Powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform improvements, an improved launcher for MAC OS Ventura, and several bug fixes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### UAF 1.2 Features

##### ArchiMate Import

Theimport of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF 1.2 projectand the element mapping are now completed.The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### UAF Reports Publishing with Cameo Collaborator

In this version, the UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator was adapted for UAF 1.2.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents' space='CC4TWC2022xR1']>>]]>

##### Other Enhancements

- The creation of the reception property for Operational, Resource, and Service signals is now available:
  - From the Operation Performer, Resource Performer, Service, and their specializations Specification Window.
  - From the element symbols' smart manipulator on the diagram pane.
  - By dragging and dropping the reception property from the Containment tree on the element symbols on the diagram pane.
- New parametric diagrams are introduced:
  - UAF 1.2: Strategic Parametric diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>
  - NAF 4.0: C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>
- Notifications were improved to show background action information in the following diagrams:
  - Strategic Phasing diagram
  - Strategic Actual Deployment diagram
  - Strategic Typical Deployment diagram

#### SysML Features

##### Improved Item Flow Management Experience

SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 brings several improvements to Item Flow management, which include:

- Selecting diagrams to update flows. From now on, a new Item Flow realization does not end up with unexpected results in existing diagrams (previously, the flow may have been updated or not), as a simple diagram update control mechanism is introduced across the project. The Item Flow Manager dialog and the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard are now enriched with the ability for you to choose which diagrams with the realizing elements to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting_diagrams_to_update_flows.png']

- A quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram. Display/hide the flowing items quickly using the dedicated action in the smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

- Element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection. Selecting the direction for the Item Flow in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard is now easier as the icons indicating element types are displayed next to the element names, allowing you to identify the sender and receiver more easily. [ATTACHMENT filename='Element_type_representation_in__Item_Flow_Direction_selection.png']

- Auto-remove not realized Item Flows. You can now specify the newly introduced Remove Not Realized Item Flows SysML project option to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. Additionally, you can run the validation to check whether there are any Item Flows in the model that are not realized.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space='SYSMLP2022xR1']>]]>

##### Requirements Verification in Instance Tables

In addition to performing requirements verification in Requirement tables, now you can also carry it out in Instance tables. While the verification in a Requirement table allows you to perform the analysis for a single instance set as the context, the verification in an Instance table allows you to perform the analysis for multiple instances at once via the **Enable Patterns-Based Verification** option. It allows you to analyze multiple instances conveniently and efficiently.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement table vs. the Instance table.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[Learn more about requirements verification in Instance Tables >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2022xR1/Requirements+verification#Requirementsverification-PerformingautomatedRequirementsverificationinInstanceTable)

##### Other Improvements

- When you create an Action directly inside the Structured Activity Node, the Allocated To property is specified automatically according to the allocation mode.

#### Data Markings and Classification Plugin Improvements

The 2022x Refresh1 version introduces new and improved functionality in the **Data Markings and Classification Plugin**, including:

- Display Containment Markings . You can now choose to have Containment Markings that are displayed on the owner element, indicating the owned elements' Data Markings. They are displayed in parentheses as postfixes next to the element name. Use the newly introduced Element Types with Containment Markings project option to specify which element types can have Containment Markings displayed on them. This feature allows you to quickly see the owned elements' Data Markings by simply glancing at the owner element. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Containment Markings' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Containment_Markings.png'] The Containment Markings are updated recursively for the package *Sensitive Information*and its contained elements.

- Sensitive Element Redaction . The Sensitive Element Redaction model transformation allows you to narrow down the full model by removing elements with the selected Data Markings. Controlling what elements should constitute the model based on their Data Markings allows you to provide a redacted model to suppliers who can only have access to some model elements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sensitive Element Redaction' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A model transformed via Sensitive Element Redaction by removing elements with *Secret (S)* and *Top Secret (TS)* Data Markings.

- Add Data Markings recursively . From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced Sensitive Element Types project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Markings Recursively' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png'] Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block *Radar*and its Value and Part Properties.

- New validation rules . The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.
  - The Invalid Containment Markings rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.
  - The Missing Data Marking checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.
  - When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings validation rules' space='DMP2022xR1']>]]>

#### Client-side Performance Improvements

##### Evaluate Operation Performance

If you are tired of seeing progress bars while performing various operations in the tool, you can now use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality, which allows you to view performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performance if it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. You can also analyze the building of matrixes and tables.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adorning diagram symbols operation performance evaluation.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

Additionally, you can now see the language recommendations based on their performance. The recommendations are visible via the 'recommended' indication and the performance indicator icon in the language selection menu. The icon color reflects the performance: green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium, and red for slow. It allows you to see the languages' performance when choosing one for your operations and make an informed decision.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Evaluating operation performance' space='MD2022xR1']>]]>

##### Other Improvements

- **Installation file for Apple M1/M2**. 2022x Refresh1 release provides two different installation files for macOS based on its processor architecture: in addition to the x86 one, now there is also a separate installation file for the one based on ARM (i.e., Apple M1). It is bundled with ARM architecture Java. The modeling tool runs up to 2-3 times faster with ARM architecture Java on Apple M1.

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

- A new UI Font environment option is now available, allowing you to change the default font the application should be displayed in. This change also supports the correct display of non-Latin scripts.
- The Standard/Expert UI modes are now removed from the Structured Expressions dialogs. Instead, the dialogs are now fully featured by default without the need to switch between modes.
- From now on, you can create project disclaimer configurations to display custom disclaimers upon opening a project. The disclaimers can contain any information you want project users to be aware of before opening the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project disclaimer configuration' space='MD2022xR1']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='disclaimer_configuration.png']
- Instead of displaying individual error notifications of the same type, they are now grouped based on their type (expression creation failure, expression evaluation failure, model change and diagram load in expressions), allowing a more compact display of notifications (see the image below). [ATTACHMENT filename='Grouping_Multiple_Instances_of_Same_Type_Notifications.png']

#### Collaboration

##### Teamwork Cloud Announcements

The 2022x Refresh1 version of MagicDraw can show custom announcements to users. [Managed through Teamwork Cloud Admin](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022xR1/Managing+announcements), these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in MagicDraw UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.

##### Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We introducePower'by – anew way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation' space='MSOSA2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation' space='CEA2022xR1']

From now on, in addition to adding Data Markings to individual elements, you can add them recursively to multiple project elements via the owner element. Use the newly introduced <strong>Sensitive Element Types </strong>project option to specify which element types can have Data Markings added to them recursively. This feature allows you to add Data Markings to multiple elements conveniently and efficiently. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Adding Data Markings Recursively" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about adding Data Markings recursively >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Adding_Data_Markings_Recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding Data Markings recursively to the Block <em>Radar </em>and its Value and Part Properties.</h6></li></ul><p><br /></p><ul><li data-uuid="c35a0cdd-0ef4-43c9-90ed-75a52c972855"><strong>New validation rules</strong>. The new features listed above are supported with additional validation rules.<ul><li>The <strong>Invalid Containment Markings</strong> rule checks if the Containment Markings match the Data Markings of owned elements.</li><li>The <strong>Missing Data Marking </strong>checks if there are elements that are specified to have Data Markings in the Sensitive Element Types project option but do not have any Data Markings added.</li><li>When a property is typed by a sensitive element, the <strong>Inconsistent Data Markings (Property) </strong>validation rule checks whether the property has a matching Data Marking or if the sensitivity level of the added Data Marking is not lower than the sensitivity level of the Data Marking added to the type. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Data Markings validation rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about validation rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><h3><span>Evaluate Operation Performance</span></h3><p>If you are tired of seeing progress bars while performing various operations in the tool, you can now use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality, which allows you to view performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind <span>degraded tool performance</span> if it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. You can also analyze the building of matrixes and tables. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adorning diagram symbols operation performance evaluation.</h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="237a232f-ac0a-4183-b938-5a335e5453ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rBAj6JfYlqg&amp;ab_channel=NoMagic" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />Additionally, you can now see the language recommendations based on their performance. The recommendations are visible via the 'recommended' indication and the performance indicator icon in the language selection menu. The icon color reflects the performance: green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium, and red for slow. It allows you to see the languages' performance when choosing one for your operations and make an informed decision. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="language_performance_indicators.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Evaluating operation performance" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about operation performance evaluation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span>Other Improvements</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="924e64f2-dbc3-4506-be6e-cfe7da3fc06f"><span><strong>Installation file for Apple M1/M2</strong>. 2022x Refresh1 release provides two different installation files for macOS based on its processor architecture: in addition to the x86 one, now there is also a separate installation file for the one based on ARM (i.e., Apple M1). It is bundled with ARM architecture Java. The modeling tool runs up to 2-3 times faster with ARM architecture Java on Apple M1.</span></li></ul><h2>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><ul><li data-uuid="812027ad-9fce-437c-aa29-b6447c4fe3ee">A new <strong>UI Font</strong> environment option is now available, allowing you to change the default font the application should be displayed in. This change also supports the correct display of non-Latin scripts. </li><li data-uuid="08f2e1a2-ec0f-4f09-966e-9211ae4bace5">The Standard/Expert UI modes are now removed from the Structured Expressions dialogs. Instead, the dialogs are now fully featured by default without the need to switch between modes.  </li><li data-uuid="74386b8e-ee13-47fc-bf97-00028d9b2a29">From now on, you can create project disclaimer configurations to display custom disclaimers upon opening a project. The disclaimers can contain any information you want project users to be aware of before opening the project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR1" ri:content-title="Project disclaimer configuration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about project disclaimer configurations >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disclaimer_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="701b7fe8-0815-4827-b17f-6325c2e42a27">Instead of displaying individual error notifications of the same type, they are now grouped based on their type (expression creation failure, expression evaluation failure, model change and diagram load in expressions), allowing a more compact display of notifications (see the image below).<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Grouping_Multiple_Instances_of_Same_Type_Notifications.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h2>Collaboration</h2><h3>Teamwork Cloud Announcements</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The 2022x Refresh1 version of MagicDraw can show custom announcements to users. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022xR1/Managing+announcements">Managed through Teamwork Cloud Admin</a>, these announcements can contain information on server-wide upgrades, project-specific deadlines, or any other important news. An announcement is displayed in MagicDraw UI until a user acknowledges it by closing.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="announcements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Teamwork Cloud announcement displayed on the Welcome screen of a modeling tool and a specific project.</span></h6><h3>Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We introduce</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Power'by – a</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> new way to collaborate on models within the 3DEXPERIENCE platform environment. For programs and projects requiring models to span across multiple engineering disciplines, Power'by allows leveraging native 3DEXPERIENCE platform capabilities, such as access control, advanced configuration management, traceability through the digital thread, and many others. The initial release aims to provide core collaboration features: storing and loading projects, working on them simultaneously, and committing and updating changes as part of configuration management control.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_through_3DEXPERIENCE.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="7150de3c-2e77-4334-b246-1ef399a22f7d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSOSA2022xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Systems of Systems Architect 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1c34cd23-ebcf-4fa4-b4ef-113c9197b6a1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2022xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741790 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: (2022x Refresh1) Product Line Engineering

- page_id: `272741790`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741790/2022x+Refresh1+Product+Line+Engineering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:32.165+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

##### Multiple Independent Feature Model Support

Beginning with this version, you can use multiple independent feature models in one project. An example of a common use case would be using one feature model for expressing PLE variability, and another one for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMP2022xR1']. Another example would be using one feature model for expressing global enterprise-wide PLE variability and a smaller, local one that is project-specific.

When there are multiple independent feature models in a single project, defined variation points can refer to features in any of those feature models. This also means that the100% model of the system is defined by a combination of the configurations. This combination needs to be selected during variant highlight and/or realization.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Line Engineering' space='PLE2022xR1']

An example of a common use case would be using one feature model for expressing PLE variability, and another one for <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Markings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Another example would be using one feature model for expressing global enterprise-wide PLE variability and a smaller, local one that is project-specific.</p><p>When there are multiple independent feature models in a single project, defined variation points can refer to features in any of those feature models. This also means that the100% model of the system is defined by a combination of the configurations. This combination needs to be selected during variant highlight and/or realization.</p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PLE2022xR1" ri:content-title="Product Line Engineering" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Product Line Engineering 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741701 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2022x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `272741701`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741701/2022x+Refresh1+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-26T19:33:26.052+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.

##### Improved Item Flow Management Experience

SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 brings several improvements to Item Flow management, which include:

- Selecting diagrams to update flows. From now on, a new Item Flow realization does not end up with unexpected results in existing diagrams (previously, the flow may have been updated or not), as a simple diagram update control mechanism is introduced across the project. The Item Flow Manager dialog and the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard are now enriched with the ability for you to choose which diagrams with the realizing elements to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting_diagrams_to_update_flows.png']

- A quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram. Display/hide the flowing items quickly using the dedicated action in the smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

- Element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection. Selecting the direction for the Item Flow in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard is now easier as the icons indicating element types are displayed next to the element names, allowing you to identify the sender and receiver more easily. [ATTACHMENT filename='Element_type_representation_in__Item_Flow_Direction_selection.png']

- Auto-remove not realized Item Flows. You can now specify the newly introduced Remove Not Realized Item Flows SysML project option to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. Additionally, you can run the validation to check whether there are any Item Flows in the model that are not realized.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space='SYSMLP2022xR1']>]]>

##### Requirements Verification in Instance Tables

In addition to performing requirements verification in Requirement tables, now you can also carry it out in Instance tables. While the verification in a Requirement table allows you to perform the analysis for a single instance set as the context, the verification in an Instance table allows you to perform the analysis for multiple instances at once via the **Enable Patterns-Based Verification** option. It allows you to analyze multiple instances conveniently and efficiently.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement table vs. the Instance table.

To learn more, watch the following video:

[Learn more about requirements verification in Instance Tables >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2022xR1/Requirements+verification#Requirementsverification-PerformingautomatedRequirementsverificationinInstanceTable)

##### Other Improvements

- When you create an Action directly inside the Structured Activity Node, the Allocated To property is specified automatically according to the allocation mode.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2022xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Improved Item Flow Management Experience</h3><p>SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 brings several improvements to Item Flow management, which include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="39f4d107-076a-4bfa-a8ab-b8a96bde8569"><strong>Selecting diagrams to update flows. </strong>From now on, a new Item Flow realization does not end up with unexpected results in existing diagrams (previously, the flow may have been updated or not), as a simple diagram update control mechanism is introduced across the project. Additionally, you can run the validation to check whether there are any Item Flows in the model that are not realized.</li></ul><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR1" ri:content-title="Item Flow management" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Item Flow management >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Requirements Verification in Instance Tables</h3><p>In addition to performing requirements verification in Requirement tables, now you can also carry it out in Instance tables. While the verification in a Requirement table allows you to perform the analysis for a single instance set as the context, the verification in an Instance table allows you to perform the analysis for multiple instances at once via the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> option. It allows you to analyze multiple instances conveniently and efficiently.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Requirements_verification_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Performing the Requirements verification analysis in the Requirement table vs. the Instance table. </h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2022xR1/Requirements+verification#Requirementsverification-PerformingautomatedRequirementsverificationinInstanceTable">Learn more about requirements verification in Instance Tables &gt;&gt;</a></p><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="4fad2834-e7e9-44df-acfd-5d6d74865f01">When you create an Action directly inside the Structured Activity Node, the <strong>Allocated To</strong> property is specified automatically according to the allocation mode.</li></ul><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="1c180d69-cebe-490e-b7e5-663cfdea3b06"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR1" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741802 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2022x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 Plugin

- page_id: `272741802`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272741802/2022x+Refresh1+UAF+1.2+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-27T12:42:27.388+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh1) 2022x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 18, 2022

In the 2022x Refresh1 release, theimport of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF 1.2 projectand the element mapping are now completed. Moreover, the UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator was adapted for UAF 1.2. Finally, new parametric diagrams, notification improvements, and more reception property creation options were implemented.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].For further information, check the.

##### ArchiMate Import

Theimport of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF 1.2 projectand the element mapping are now completed.The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### UAF Reports Publishing with Cameo Collaborator

In this version, the UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator was adapted for UAF 1.2.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents' space='CC4TWC2022xR1']>>]]>

##### Other Enhancements

- The creation of the reception property for Operational, Resource, and Service signals is now available:
  - From the Operation Performer, Resource Performer, Service, and their specializations Specification Window.
  - From the element symbols' smart manipulator on the diagram pane.
  - By dragging and dropping the reception property from the Containment tree on the element symbols on the diagram pane.
- New parametric diagrams are introduced:
  - UAF 1.2: Strategic Parametric diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>
  - NAF 4.0: C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='UAF12P2022xR1']>>]]>
- Notifications were improved to show background action information in the following diagrams:
  - Strategic Phasing diagram
  - Strategic Actual Deployment diagram
  - Strategic Typical Deployment diagram

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12P2022xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

more about document publishing with Cameo Collaborator >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="70e57aaf-3e0c-4322-98de-5a850f75d771">The creation of the<span> </span><em>reception</em><span> </span>property for Operational, Resource, and Service signals is now available:<ul><li>From the Operation Performer, Resource Performer, Service, and their element symbols on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="72b51622-8015-4913-ba64-328b653cd436">New parametric diagrams are introduced:<ul><li>UAF 1.2: Strategic Parametric diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Parametric diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>NAF 4.0: C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR1" ri:content-title="C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="3f0a8b71-1da9-422e-9f91-9ee939259481">Notifications were improved to show background action information in the following diagrams:<ul><li>Strategic Phasing diagram</li><li>Strategic Actual Deployment diagram</li><li>Strategic Typical Deployment diagram</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="ee569ad8-2ef2-4406-baea-555701bab907"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR1" ri:content-title="UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.2 Plugin 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742818 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `272742818`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742818/2022x+Refresh2+2022x+Refresh2+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:32.474+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News > (2022x Refresh2) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: February 16, 2024

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- Failure to initialize the DSL engine due to the incorrect usage of the «metaProperty» stereotype is fixed.
- The modeling tool no longer freezes when using a module in Model Transformation.
- Once you click the ‘Down’ key on a cell after editing it in the Generic Table, the cursor no longer jumps to the consecutive row of the last column but to the consecutive row of the same column.
- The issue where a project cannot be saved if the modeling tool’s installation location is the disk root is fixed.
- Internal errors no longer occur when the 'Message to Self' action from the Smart Manipulator of a lifeline is executed.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML

- Multiple selected variant values in 3DX Variant Management or Model Definition widget models are now accurately represented in slot values after model import to the modeling tool.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- Simulation no longer freezes when hitting a timing event or duration constraint if timing properties are used.
- The issue where simulation produces different results after reopening a project has been fixed.
- The problem causing a Do Activity to be skipped after execution of an Entry Activity in a State Machine Diagram has been resolved.
- The issue where State regions do not execute in parallel due to a duration constraint has been fixed.
- The issue where nested State Machines are executed with the wrong context has been fixed.
- The problem where a sent signal does not reach nested State Machines during a State Machine Diagram simulation has been resolved.
- Now when executing the FMUs, the step endpoint is called only when the value of the 'simulationTime' key is increased.
- The problem where the simulation suddenly stops working after exceeding a certain log file size has been eliminated.
- The Time Unit of a Step Size is no longer misinterpreted as milliseconds when running FMUs.

##### Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2

- The issue regarding the COR2165 validation rule has been fixed. Now w hile creating Function Object Flow between Function Actions correct parameters will be created.
- The issue where the Select in Containment tree did not work correctly if Item Flows are filtered or when there are inherited/aggregated flows turned on in Item Flow Manager has been fixed.
- The Affects relationship was adjusted to enable the selection of UAF Activities as its target.

##### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Fixed an authentication issue where it was not possible to use identical LDAP and SAML users in combination.
- Fixed an issue where an incorrect result was displayed after changing the role scope to Global.
- Fixed an issue where the login page was unavailable on JxBrowser in some cases.
- P roject update is now requested before locking project usages in certain scenarios.
- Categories can no longer be deleted in server from the Offline tab.
- The images imported from DOORS Next Generation are now displayed in Cameo Collaborator documents and can be exported to PDF.

##### Cameo DataHub

- Images inserted with the help of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space='MD2022xR2'] are now available for synchronization from the modeling tool to the ENOVIA Requirements application. To learn how to enable HTML image support , refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML images, RTF, and OLE support' space='CDH2022xR2'] .
- The synchronization issue occurring while moving the elements in the modeling tool is now fixed for the ENOVIA Requirements application.

##### Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

- Improved performance of project opening and project creation in 3D EXPERIENCE Platform.
- Fixed an issue where merge-specific commit type was not reported among the usual project commit types.
- Fixed an issue where it was impossible to connect to the platform in certain cases.
- Fixed an error that may have occurred with some projects while adding them to the 3D EXPERIENCE Platform.

##### Other

- Errors no longer appear after using the ‘Generate’ action and switching to another diagram while using a modeling tool with the CATIA Software Producer plugin.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742922 space=VN version=3 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2

- page_id: `272742922`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742922/2022x+Refresh2+2022x+Refresh2+Hot+Fix+2
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:28:21.532+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News > (2022x Refresh2) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on July 26, 2024

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) Addressed vulnerabilities' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where all element representations are removed from a diagram in a server project instead of only the selected one(s) has been resolved.
- The removal of shortcut keys via Environment Options is no longer impersistent.
- The issue where the spell checker was not working due to the inability to extract dictionaries provided in the modeling tool installation files has been fixed.
- Comparator exceptions are no longer thrown, and table diagrams are no longer empty when working with derived properties in DSL.
- An anchor between a Note symbol and a Legend Item no longer disappears from a diagram after reopening a project.
- The issue where the ID and Text Tagged Values are not displayed in a Note on a diagram is fixed.
- The issue where a validation failure is not reported if the error message has an incorrect script or HTML text is fixed.
- The performance of selecting an element with a large number of ports has been improved and the issue of the modeling tool freezing is fixed.
- The issue where a lot of memory is used when updating Text Index has been fixed.
- The issue where a screen recording dialog is displayed upon launching the modeling tool on macOS has been fixed.
- The modeling tool installation issue on Linux where the recommended Java version could not be selected has been fixed.

###### Collaboration

- 
  - The issues where the UPDM Profile for BPMN is duplicated after profile migration has been fixed.
  - The problem where in certain cases saving projects locally creates unexpected project usages has been resolved.
  - The errors no longer appear while loading Teamwork Cloud projects previously migrated with the 2022x R1 version.
  - Large model texts no longer break the index and are added to Teamwork Cloud without exceptions.

###### Collaboration Powered by**3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

- 
  - The issue preventing users from connecting to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform with a custom port has been resolved.
  - The issue where an error occurs while trying to edit project branches after a project merge has been fixed.
  - The problem causing an error when trying to open a project after a project merge, if the source branch is hidden, has been resolved.
  - The issue of the project branch history not being displayed after a project merge, when the source branch is hidden, has been resolved.
  - The issues where you could not log in to the 2024xFD03 platfor from the 2022x Refresh2 or earlier modeling tool version has been fixed.
  - The issue where you could not log in to the R2024x FD02 platform from the 2022x modeling tool version has been fixed.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue where in some cases signals are not sent during simulation has been fixed.
- The problem when a signal with generalization and redefined attribute does not work with Accept Event Actions has been resolved.
- A simplified Set Simulation Variable method has been introduced for setting a single simulation variable. The new method does not require constructing a JSON.
- Redefined array values are no longer doubled when redefined property is an opaque expression.
- The issue where during simulation a Constraint Parameter is not passed to another Constraint Parameter has been fixed.
- The issue related to the forked Activity execution has been resolved.
- The problem where an Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action does not take into account context selection has been fixed.

##### Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2

- The PES export functionality is no longer available.

##### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- The CVE-2023-3589 vulnerability is addressed. The remediation is as follows:

To enable CSRF protection, you need to uncomment *[esi.dm](http://esi.dm).csrf.allowed-addresses*property in the Teamwork Cloud application.conf file.

*[esi.dm](http://esi.dm) {*

*# Enable to turn on CSRF protection. This will block all incoming REST API requests, except those, coming from specified*

*# IP addresses. List of strings, allowed IP must begin with specified string.*

*# csrf.allowed-addresses = ["127.0.0.1", "0.0.0"]*

*}*

##### Cameo DataHub

- The Ddatahub.trm.not_use_Folders argument reduces the time required to retrieve the list of Requirement Specifications available for the user on Single Specification Selection GUI. Setting the argument to *true*disables the support for specification folders on 3DS platform.
- The TWC OSLC API URL could be provided manually on the Ddatahub.oslc.url.backLink argument. The argument replaces the automatic retrieval of OSLC API link disabled in 2022x version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

data-uuid="2247aff1-4fbe-41f1-9870-8040e8135465">The issue where in some cases signals are not sent during simulation has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="47594b96-dd6a-4195-873f-62ce23f9cded">The problem when a signal with generalization and redefined attribute does not work with Accept Event Actions has been resolved. </li><li data-uuid="5ff6b082-f8a0-4215-bac5-7b45ee275042"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A simplified </span>Set Simulation Variable<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>method has been introduced for setting a single simulation variable. The new method does not require constructing a JSON. </span></li><li data-uuid="245cc738-75bd-490a-a974-2aeb33fd5f2e">Redefined array values are no longer doubled when redefined property is an opaque expression. </li><li data-uuid="ab9e972a-eafa-4acd-89ae-8733c96c377c">The issue where during simulation a Constraint Parameter is not passed to another Constraint Parameter has been fixed. </li><li data-uuid="c7520f04-03a0-4ff5-917c-9f90b707e561">The issue related to the forked Activity execution has been resolved. </li><li data-uuid="c5974bc5-c1ed-4d15-8651-90429bab99e9"> The problem where an Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action does not take into account context selection has been fixed. </li></ul><h3>Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2</h3><ul><li data-uuid="e649b696-baa0-4481-bf60-3d0a1e1d6700"><span>The PES export functionality is no longer available.</span></li></ul><h3>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator</h3><ul><li data-uuid="86956682-2a7b-402a-bfb8-8d2e58a3ecce">The CVE-2023-3589 vulnerability is addressed. The remediation is as follows:</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">To enable CSRF protection, you need to uncomment <em><a href="http://esi.dm">esi.dm</a>.csrf.allowed-addresses </em>property in the Teamwork Cloud application.conf file.</p><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><em><a href="http://esi.dm">esi.dm</a> {</em></p><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><em>    # Enable to turn on CSRF protection. This will block all incoming REST API requests, except those, coming from specified</em></p><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><em>    # IP addresses. List of strings, allowed IP must begin with specified string.</em></p><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><em>    # csrf.allowed-addresses = [&quot;127.0.0.1&quot;, &quot;0.0.0&quot;]</em></p><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><em>}</em></p><h3><span>Cameo DataHub</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="50dbe486-73ed-4335-9e5b-46467040f7ba"><span>The Ddatahub.trm.not_use_Folders argument reduces the time required to retrieve the list of Requirement Specifications available for the user on Single Specification Selection GUI. Setting the argument to <em>true </em>disables the support for specification folders on 3DS platform.</span></li><li data-uuid="00b34cb4-8f2a-497c-b52e-7c6cb1f228ba">The TWC OSLC API URL could be provided manually on the Ddatahub.oslc.url.backLink argument. The argument replaces the automatic retrieval of OSLC API link disabled in 2022x version. </li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742989 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3

- page_id: `272742989`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742989/2022x+Refresh2+2022x+Refresh2+Hot+Fix+3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:48.041+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News > (2022x Refresh2) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: February 28, 2025

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of bug fixes and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2']. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where a multi-valued enumeration property is not exported to a Requirements Interchange Format File has been fixed.
- The issue of an incorrect default value for the 'Legend Location' option in a matrix has been fixed.
- The issue where the URL specified for the 'Active Hyperlink' property is truncated in the Specification window and table diagrams has been fixed.
- The issue where an element is pasted to the current owner if the containment path on a diagram is pasted too has been resolved. Now, you can copy-paste an element to a different owner, allowing you to update the containment path.

###### Report Wizard

- The issue where a local template does not open when attached template is extracted has been fixed.
- The issuewhere the imported template shows up only when Report Wizard dialog is reopened has been fixed.

###### Cameo Data Modeler

- The issue when using ER to DDL transformation and model contained identifying relationships has been fixed.

###### Collaboration

- The issue where an incorrect latest version is displayed in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog has been fixed.
- The issue where an incorrect ancestor version is selected during project merge has been fixed. Additionally, the Accept Recursively and Reject Recursively commands in the Change Details panel now work as expected.

##### Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2

- The issue regarding wrong UAF customization profile version in compatibility range has been fixed.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML

- The issue where the Autowire Parts command takes too much time to execute has been fixed.
- The issue where importing Simulink blocks with complex calculations does not work properly has been fixed.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue in rare cases preventing you to add a classifier as a value in the Variables pane of the Simulation console has been resolved.
- The issue where in the initialization phase, default values are not passed from one constraint parameter to another has been fixed.
- The problem where the Dymola 2024x Refresh1 integration does not work with Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit has been resolved.

##### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Improved Web Application Platform performance of API methods retrieving users information.
- The cross-cluster resource synchronization feature has been improved by fixing issues related to token expiration.
- Fixed a minor bug where navigating to Cameo Collaborator graphical comments would cause the modeling tool to blink.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open Source components have been updated, as listed below, to address known software vulnerabilities. Legal Notices will be updated to reflect these, and other changes, at the next scheduled regular release.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742764 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: (2022x Refresh2) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `272742764`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742764/2022x+Refresh2+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:28.414+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces powerful enhancements that provide greater flexibility when contextualizing documents. With the ability to create and manage document sections, you can now easily add explanatory texts and images anywhere in your document. Furthermore, the document export capability has been improved, allowing you to customize the appearance of exported documents by adding page breaks and disabling automatic section numbering. The release also includes other improvements, such as enhanced rich text editing, improved comment filtering, and more.

##### Document Contextualization Enhancements

Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2 presents a powerful functionality that will give you even more flexibility when contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents. It allows you to create new document sections, which means that you can add new content, like explanatory texts and images, anywhere in a document. With this game-changing capability, enriching the published data so it could be understood by stakeholders or customers who are not familiar with modeling languages will be easier than ever. In addition, managing new sections will not be a problem: you can rename and reorder them with just a few clicks.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating document sections.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Reordering document sections.

##### Exporting Documents to PDF/HTML

The latest release of Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio introduces several improvements to the document export capability to give you more control over the appearance of exported documents:

- When exporting Cameo Collaborator documents to PDF, you can add page breaks after all sections with a specified heading level.
- When exporting documents to PDF or HTML, you can disable the automatic section numbering in the generated file. This functionality could be useful if document sections have custom numbering that you want to keep.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The second step of the document export wizard allows you to choose the section numbering and page break preferences.

##### Other Improvements

- The rich text editing window for textual properties has been updated and now includes the ability to use the Superscript, Subscript, Clear Formatting, and Strikethrough features. [ATTACHMENT filename='rich_text_editing.png']
- When you filter Cameo Collaborator comments by user, the user filter icon is highlighted to indicate that the filter is active. Also, the filter remains active after replying to comments.
- The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio allows you to simulate a model from any place in a document where the execution element is displayed. In addition, you can run multiple simulations at once, and they will be placed in a queue.

##### Deployment Changes

Now the access to ZooKeeper nodes can be based on Access Control Levels (ACL) for improved security. For more information about ZooKeeper access control using ACLs, refer to [https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl).

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='TWCloud2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742820 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: (2022x Refresh2) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272742820`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742820/2022x+Refresh2+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:33.028+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

In version 2022x Refresh2, Cameo DataHub introduces many new features and improvements. You can now add a single requirement specification in the ENOVIA Requirements on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform. Also, you can open the module view without opening the module in IBM Doors.

#### ENOVIA Requirements

##### Single specification selection

A new feature to select a single specification is introduced to improve the connection to the ENOVIA Requirement application. In earlier versions, when you connected to the ENOVIA Requirements application, all available specifications were displayed in the Cameo DataHub Explorer panel. Now, when you connect to the ENOVIA Requirements application, a new dialog appears, listing all the specifications available to you. Then you select one specification at a time and connect to it. The specification added is available when you reconnect to the same data source from the same project. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification selection dialog.

You can also add other specifications one at a time by selecting the **Add single requirement specification** option from the ENOVIA data source shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Add single requirement specification command in the shortcut menu.

##### Other Improvements

- ENOVIA Requirements now supports 3DEXPERIENCE platform versions up to 2023x FD03.
- The issue related to the connection to the distributed platform and the platform on the cloud is resolved.
- Operations related to copying data, expanding data source nodes, and data synchronization between the platform and the modeling application are improved. The following table shows the comparison between the time taken to perform various activities. The test set contains 1000 requirement specifications with 1000 requirements under each one, and the synchronization direction is from ENOVIA Requirements to the modeling tool. Sr. No.Action2022xR22022xR1Improvement1Connecting to ENOVIA Requirements data source01:16 Min02:15 Min~45%2Adding Requirement Specification from Single Selection UI03:22 MinNANew feature3Expanding ENOVIA DS node00:01 Min00:01 Min0%4Expanding added Requirement Specification node /1000 items00:01 Min00:49 Min~100%5Using Copy data with sync operation02:28 Min44:54 Min~95%

#### IBM Doors

##### Data source creation from view

The new command **Create data source from view** is added to the IBM DOORS. You can now open the module view without opening the module.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Create data source from View command in the shortcut menu.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo DataHub Documentation' space='CDH2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00090: (2022x Refresh2) Cameo Safety and Reliability Analyzer

- page_id: `272742803`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742803/2022x+Refresh2+Cameo+Safety+and+Reliability+Analyzer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:30.855+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

##### Fault Tree Analysis

A new plugin has been introduced which is used in safety and reliability engineering to understand how systems can fail or what is the cause of system failure. The Fault Tree Analysis plugin lets you create a Fault Tree Analysis diagram with the help of different types of events and gates. The Fault Tree Analysis diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram and follows the Risk Analysis and Assessment Modeling Language (RAAML) methods. You can also interconnect the Fault Tree Analysis diagram with the System Model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fault Tree Analysis diagram.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Safety and Reliability Analyzer' space='CSRA2022xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742674 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2022x Refresh2) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272742674`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742674/2022x+Refresh2+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:24.502+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

Version 2022x Refresh2 allows users to easily create and manage element-level webhooks, strengthen password security with lifecycle options, and synchronize resourcesacrossrepositories in more flexible ways. These updates enhance the usability, security, and collaboration aspects of Magic Collaboration Studio.

In addition, Cameo Collaborator introduces powerful enhancements that provide greater flexibility when contextualizing documents. With the ability to create and manage document sections, you can now easily add explanatory texts and images anywhere in your document. Furthermore, the document export capability has been improved, allowing you to customize the appearance of exported documents by adding page breaks and disabling automatic section numbering. The release also includes other improvements, such as enhanced rich text editing, improved comment filtering, and more.

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin

##### Webhooks' Improvements

In 2022x Refresh2 we implemented the ability to conveniently [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a webhook' space='MCS2022xR2'] in Teamwork Cloud Admin. A simple UI wizard will guide you through the creation of resource or element-level (NEW!) webhooks to listen to changes made in the Magic Collaboration Studio repository.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting a webhook in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

##### Password Lifecycle Management

Password requirements management in the Teamwork Cloud Admin Settings application was enhanced with added password lifecycle management options. In addition to the complexity requirements, you can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing password complexity and lifecycle' space='MCS2022xR2'] in the Lifecycle tab.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Password complexity & lifecycle requirements with the new Lifecycle tab.

##### Multi-Actions in Resources Application

We implemented a multi-action capability in the Resources application. Once enabled through the Actions menu [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the App bar, this capability allows you to select multiple resources or categories to delete or move.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling multiselect and selecting multiple categories to move or delete in the Resources app.

##### Cross-Cluster Synchronization Improvements

Starting with 2022x Refresh2, we allow further synchronization of previously synchronized resources. This change was inspired by certain business cases where third parties willing to interchange Magic Collaboration Studio projects often deny connections to each other’s networks (as represented by the first topology in the image). With the improvement, it is possible to set up a Magic Collaboration Studio instance in a somewhat neutral environment (such as AWS Cloud), where all involved parties can synchronize the resources they want to share. To check out a resource, it is simply necessary to synchronize it further from a neutral environment to a company’s specific network.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Other fixes/improvements

- Directly assigned and inherited access levels are now displayed in reports .
- Data markings are now displayed in the Resources application. You can also see a preview of the data marking you are creating.
- The UI of the task manager was improved and now shows why a process or an activity had errors.
- A new role Reports Manager was added, which allows access to the Reports application.
- Search results in the Resources application are now sorted in a more user-friendly way.

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### Document Contextualization Enhancements

Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2 presents a powerful functionality that will give you even more flexibility when contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents. It allows you to create new document sections, which means that you can add new content, like explanatory texts and images, anywhere in a document. With this game-changing capability, enriching the published data so it could be understood by stakeholders or customers who are not familiar with modeling languages will be easier than ever. In addition, managing new sections will not be a problem: you can rename and reorder them with just a few clicks.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating document sections.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Reordering document sections.

##### Exporting Documents to PDF/HTML

The latest release of Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio introduces several improvements to the document export capability to give you more control over the appearance of exported documents:

- When exporting Cameo Collaborator documents to PDF, you can add page breaks after all sections with a specified heading level.
- When exporting documents to PDF or HTML, you can disable the automatic section numbering in the generated file. This functionality could be useful if document sections have custom numbering that you want to keep.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The second step of the document export wizard allows you to choose the section numbering and page break preferences.

##### Other Improvements

- The rich text editing window for textual properties has been updated and now includes the ability to use the Superscript, Subscript, Clear Formatting, and Strikethrough features. [ATTACHMENT filename='rich_text_editing.png']
- When you filter Cameo Collaborator comments by user, the user filter icon is highlighted to indicate that the filter is active. Also, the filter remains active after replying to comments.
- The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio allows you to simulate a model from any place in a document where the execution element is displayed. In addition, you can run multiple simulations at once, and they will be placed in a queue.

##### Deployment Changes

Now the access to ZooKeeper nodes can be based on Access Control Levels (ACL) for improved security. For more information about ZooKeeper access control using ACLs, refer to [https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl).

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud and Services' space='TWCloud2022xR2']

Also, the filter remains active after replying to comments.</li><li data-uuid="5f09bf8e-da07-4e7e-bbd1-4ec3c20b783f">The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio allows you to simulate a model from any place in a document where the execution element is displayed. In addition, you can run multiple simulations at once, and they will be placed in a queue.</li></ul><h3><br />Deployment Changes</h3><p>Now the access to ZooKeeper nodes can be based on Access Control Levels (ACL) for improved security. For more information about ZooKeeper access control using ACLs, refer to <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl">https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl</a>.</p><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="0685a30a-6ad2-4a7d-866f-034403023049"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2022xR2" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud and Services" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
