<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742477 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: (2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `272742477`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742477/2022x+Refresh2+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:17.020+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

2022x Refresh2 introduces improvements to MagicGrid projects, as now you can easily create additional MagicGrid package structures for components and subsystems in MagicGrid projects.

2022x Refresh2 presents various enhancements to server-side simulation, including the ability to generate Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs) for external co-simulation, live Timeline charts allowing real-time monitoring and analysis of simulation data, and improved server-simulation with UI. Additionally, this release introduces triangular distribution support for random input generation, support for Behavior Ports, enhancements to the Watch Pane, including the ability to export Watch items to a SelectPropertiesConfig, and more.

The 2022x Refresh2 release introduces performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side, as well as general modeling and collaborative modeling enhancements.

The client-side performance enhancements include improved validation performance and decreased table opening time.

For general modeling, this release presents new syncing with Excel options, the ability to temporarily activate or deactivate legends without the diagram lock, and automated package structure creation.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is improved with a new file exchange format .szip, allowing faster transfer of models between OEMs and suppliers, the ability to view Teamwork Cloud data markings in MagicDraw, and other enhancements. Also, Power'By has been updated with standard profiles migration and the ability to cut used project references if the used project is not found.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### SysML Features

##### Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid ProjectsAutomated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects

2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the **Create Package Structure** command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the **Create Package Structure**command.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using packages' space='SYSMLP2022xR2']>]]>

#### Simulation Features

##### Server-Side Simulation

2022x Refresh2 presents multiple server-side simulation improvements, making model analysis more effective and providing a better user experience.

**Building Server-Side Simulation FMUBuilding Server-Side Simulation FMU**

The latest version introduces a powerful new feature: the ability to generate a Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU) for external co-simulation of dynamic models. This functionality allows you to exchange simulation results between different simulation tools, enabling a more collaborative and integrated approach to modeling and simulation.

You can easily create an FMU which exposes the model's input and output parameters defined in a Simulation Configuration. This FMU can then be used to run server-side simulations of this Simulation Configuration, utilizing the provided inputs and returning outputs after each time step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating an FMU and using it in another simulation tool.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating FMU - Technology preview' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

Live Timeline Charts**Live Timeline Charts**

Server-side simulation now supports live Timeline charts. With this latest update, you can view Activity and State plots in real-time, making monitoring and analyzing your simulation data easier and more efficient.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A live Timeline Chart.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation with UI' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

Improvement to Server-Simulation with UI**Improvement to Server-Simulation with UI**

When simulating a Simulation Configuration with a specified UI, you can now open an index UI window that displays all UI elements in separate boxes. An index UI window allows you to easily reorder and resize each individual UI, giving you more control and flexibility in customizing your simulation experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A UI index window where you can reorder and resize all existing UIs.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation with UI' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Triangular Distribution SupportTriangular Distribution Support

In addition to the widely-used uniform and normal distributions, the new version nowsupports triangular distribution for generating random input values. This new feature offers greater flexibility in model simulation and can help you achieve more accurate simulation results. After applying the «triangular» stereotype, you can easily generate random input values based on the minimum, maximum, and peak values specified for a given Value Property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A triangular distribution chart.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distribution Extensions' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Behavior Port SupportBehavior Port Support

The new release includes support for Behavior Ports during simulation. If a port is designated as a behavior, all incoming requests will be immediately passed to the owning classifier while ignoring all delegation connectors.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Internal Block Diagram with a Behavior Port.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Watch Pane ImprovementsWatch Pane Improvements

2022x Refresh2 introduces several improvements to the Watch Pane:

- You can now export the list of Watch items to a new or existing SelectPropertiesConfig by using the Watch Pane toolbar.
- The Watch Pane can now display the Requirement and Margin columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting the list of Watch items to a SelectPropertiesConfig.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Watch pane' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Other Improvements-simOther Improvements

- The new version allows you to create an Internal Transition and set its trigger or effect by simply using the drag-and-drop functionality.
- The Select Port dialog now displays only the Ports that are associated with the context. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

- Validation Performance Improvements**Validation Performance Improvements.** Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the **Validate Elements Recursively** action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.

- Table Performance Improvements**Table** Performance Improvements. Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. Now it is 5 times faster.

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the Select Columns dialog's Properties tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png']

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

##### New Sync with Excel OptionsNew Syncing with Excel File Options

Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:

- **Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.** If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool.

- **Color marking modified data upon export.** You can now color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes in the Excel file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.

- **Mapping elements by ID upon export.** From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### *Check user permissions* row is added to the synced Excel file *Constraints in Generic Table* without overwriting the data already existing in the file.

- **Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.** You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the **Sync Plain Text** and **Import Plain Text** check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export without affecting the styling specified in the file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Sync Plain Text**check box in the **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog and the **Import Plain Text** check box in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Manipulate Legends without Diagram LockManipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button, allowingyou to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Temporarily suspending the applied Legend *Status* via **Suspend/Active** toggle button.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Automated Package Structure CreationAutomated Package Structure Creation

You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the**Create Package Structure**action.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The package structure configuration element *SubsystemPackages*defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. 
This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element *Subsystem 3 Design*via the **Create Package Structure**action.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated Package Structure Creation' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

#### CollaborationCollaboration

##### TWCTeamwork Cloud

###### New FileNew File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)

Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!

###### TWC Data MarkingsTeamwork Cloud Data Markings in MagicDraw

Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in **Open Server Project / Manage Project** and **Use Server Project** dialogs in MagicDraw. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.

###### Set as latestEnhanced Set as Latest Functionality

The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.

###### Pre-merge checkPre-merge Check of Project Permissions

We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible. 
**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.

###### Recursive searchRecursive Global Element Usage Search

Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the **Used By in Server Recursively** option for the **Search in Server**action.**Used By in Server Recursively** works similarly to **Used by in Server**, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Used By in Server Recursively action.

###### Other fixesOther fixes/improvements

- Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the **Conflicting Changes** panel.
- **Remove, keep References** operation has been improved to work faster.

##### Power3DEXPERIENCE Platform – Power’By

###### **Standard Profiles**Standard Profiles Migration

To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.

###### Cut referencesCutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found

If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.

#### Upcoming UpdatesUpcoming Updates

Important updates coming with the next major version release:

- OpenJDK version change. Desktop applications ( MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect ) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK 17.0.7+7 version.
- JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. The JavaScript Nashorn language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript*Rhino*instead. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>
- Record Macro functionality discontinuation. The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Cyber Systems Engineer Documentation' space='MCSE2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Systems Modeler Documentation' space='CSM2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Creation in MagicGrid Projects </h3><p>2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the <strong>Create Package Structure</strong> command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_Package_Structure.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>command.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR2" ri:content-title="Using packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Simulation Features</h2><h3>Server-Side Simulation</h3><p>2022x Refresh2 presents multiple server-side simulation improvements, making model analysis more effective and providing a better user experience.</p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41cf132a-8464-4fe0-9c19-9f5f087c81e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Building Server-Side Simulation FMU</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Building Server-Side Simulation FMU</strong></p><p>The latest version introduces a powerful new feature: the ability to generate a Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU) for external co-simulation of dynamic models. This functionality allows you to exchange simulation results between different simulation tools, enabling a more collaborative and integrated approach to modeling and simulation.</p><p>You can easily create an FMU which exposes the model's input and output parameters defined in a Simulation Configuration. This FMU can then be used to run server-side simulations of this Simulation Configuration, utilizing the provided inputs and returning outputs after each time step.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generating_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Generating an FMU and using it in another simulation tool.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Generating FMU - Technology preview" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about generating FMU >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10d5b2a3-9288-4342-b788-4798ad0c40f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Live Timeline Charts</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Live Timeline Charts</strong></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Server-side simulation now supports live Timeline charts. With this latest update, you can view Activity and State plots in real-time, making monitoring and analyzing your simulation data easier and more efficient.</span><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1100"><ri:attachment ri:filename="live_timeline_chart.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A live Timeline Chart.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Simulation with UI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about server-side simulation with UI >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5ca47cd-6938-4bc7-bd43-10a0b3869a36"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Improvement to Server-Simulation with UI</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Improvement to Server-Simulation with UI</strong></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">W</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">hen simulating a Simulation Configuration with a specified UI, you can now open an index UI window that displays all UI elements in separate boxes. An index UI window allows you to easily reorder and resize each individual UI, giving you more control and flexibility in customizing your simulation experience.<br /><br /></span></span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ui_index_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A UI index window where you can reorder and resize all existing UIs.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Simulation with UI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about server-side simulation with UI >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fc2cb48-2b65-495e-b0ca-b36c9b46b7dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Triangular Distribution Support</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Triangular Distribution Support</h3><p>In addition to the widely-used uniform and normal distributions, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the new version now </span>supports triangular distribution for generating random input values. This new feature offers greater flexibility in model simulation and can help you achieve more accurate simulation results. After applying the «triangular» stereotype, you can easily generate random input values based on the minimum, maximum, and peak values specified for a given Value Property.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Triangular_Distribution.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A triangular distribution chart.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Distribution Extensions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about applying probability distributions >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4fc04b8-fc2c-4e9c-bfc0-ef6ff910df41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Behavior Port Support</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Behavior Port Support</h3><p>The new release <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">includes support for Behavior Ports during simulation</span>. I<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">f a port is designated as a behavior, all incoming requests will be immediately passed to the owning classifier</span> while ignoring all delegation connectors.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_port_support.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Internal Block Diagram with a Behavior Port.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about supported Ports >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dfecaa9-1e77-4513-85a6-fdca22e5c976"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Watch Pane Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Watch Pane Improvements</h3><p>2022x Refresh2 introduces several improvements to the Watch Pane:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2ddfd956-c2e6-407b-ad83-ac07bc38795c">You can now export the list of Watch items to a new or existing SelectPropertiesConfig by using the Watch Pane toolbar.</li><li data-uuid="b3bcf473-2313-4a31-a27d-67991c19db3a">The Watch Pane can now display the Requirement and Margin columns.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_watch_list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exporting the list of Watch items to a SelectPropertiesConfig.</h6><p><br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2022xR2" ri:content-title="Watch pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the Watch Pane >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="184560d5-cb3c-4ece-82a7-be73c6867c33"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements-sim</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="88be9714-1e26-485e-bcad-68dce0470a0e">The new version allows you to create an Internal Transition and set its trigger or effect by simply using the drag-and-drop functionality.</li><li data-uuid="16a9b332-6ec1-4c6d-af86-db0881818a78">The <strong>Select Port</strong> dialog now displays only the Ports that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">are associated with the context.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_port_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ul><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5cb0ce0-ea4d-49e5-942e-eb94c9eb9e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client side performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="99ccc299-339a-4a83-a49e-b4d73cf6da7c"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2f4fd47-9036-4122-915c-2221c5ede03f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Validation Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Validation Performance Improvements.</strong> Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.<br /><br /></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Performance Improvements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the <strong>Validate Elements Recursively</strong> action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.<br /><br /></h6><ul><li data-uuid="c649ca50-71b0-409d-a03d-357ef7db5aca"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee1f30b4-276e-465b-8d3a-73b6104f41d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Table Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Table </strong></span><strong>Performance Improvements. </strong>Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Table Performance Improvements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. <span>Now it is 5 times faster.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d616641-bea0-4f1b-afb4-fb3996706237"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="77346d25-a328-49c8-8316-dc12d2f22f8e">From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog's <strong>Properties </strong>tab.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88793ec1-a4a9-4dbc-9b84-53543c984962"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New Sync with Excel Options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New Syncing with Excel File Options</span></h3><p><span>Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="a453690b-1ec6-40f1-8c99-0f3e7a2cdd3b"><span> <strong>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.</strong> </span><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.</span> </span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="skipping_rows.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="dad9f437-345d-47a2-98d5-dc0feb0e3620"><span> <strong>Color marking modified data upon export. </strong></span>You can now color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes in the Excel file.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="9f5d16fe-456b-4941-ba58-130f7a90c3f8"><span> <strong>Mapping elements by ID upon export.</strong> </span>From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mapping_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><em>Check user permissions</em> row is added to the synced Excel file <em>Constraints in Generic Table</em> without overwriting the data already existing in the file. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="8948ec81-51ae-4c3d-a4dc-8cce485696ff"><span><strong>Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.</strong> You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the <strong>Sync Plain Text</strong> and <strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export without affecting the styling specified in the file.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_plain_text_check_boxes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The <strong>Sync Plain Text </strong>check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog and the <strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog. </span></h6><p><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Syncing with Excel files >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24ac508c-de55-4bdf-98cb-c4dca6796adc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button, allowing</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> you to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Suspend_Mode_for_Applied_Legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Temporarily suspending the applied Legend <em>Status</em> via <strong>Suspend/Active</strong> toggle button.</h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a4ef1a8-8dc6-4b20-9037-a586f2e92ec2"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FKfl9rDorec&amp;t=7s" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Manipulating Legends without Diagram Lock >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f15b693c-102b-4d83-859e-88d806a1a6b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Automated Package Structure Creation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Automated Package Structure Creation</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the<span> </span></span><strong>Create Package Structure</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>action.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Automated Package Structure Creation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The package structure configuration element <em>SubsystemPackages </em>defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. <br />This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element <em>Subsystem 3 Design </em>via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>action. </span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Automated Package Structure Creation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2a3e277-67ff-49eb-8666-39ed7c6d17c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82578d71-581b-42b4-bfdc-d5dcd49ff7cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec7378b7-8301-4eb8-be02-cf9ec9d79654"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New File</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)</span></h4><p>Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!</p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75a73823-35c3-4fd0-a007-d714741b92b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC Data Markings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud Data Markings in MagicDraw</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in <strong>Open Server Project / Manage Project</strong> and <strong>Use Server Project</strong> dialogs in MagicDraw. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_data_markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.</h6><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b246181-664e-4309-91cd-f43601f70e3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Set as latest</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Enhanced Set as Latest Functionality</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_as_latest.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b023512-1cc9-4276-8798-333024967e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Pre-merge check</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Pre-merge Check of Project Permissions</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible.</span><br /><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="per_merge_check.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f4c9a7-e74c-43a8-9ca8-23beb9ac1ee1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Recursive search</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Recursive Global Element Usage Search</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the <strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> option for the <strong>Search in Server </strong>action. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> works similarly to <strong>Used by in Server</strong>, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_by_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Used By in Server Recursively action.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d720d13a-83e8-4b09-9474-2a04e7796ba9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other fixes</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other fixes/improvements</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="d40e76f2-2a55-4569-b317-0aeb8766125f"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the <strong>Conflicting Changes</strong> panel.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="d5c6ba6e-050a-4144-880a-697092cde9fc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong style="text-align: left;">Remove, keep References</strong> operation has been improved to work faster.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfdd0694-20ca-468f-858e-b8118a0d2206"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Power</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>3DEXPERIENCE <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Platform – Power’By</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11315306-8328-4395-bd48-880be3dc24e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Standard Profiles</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Standard Profiles Migration</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="236"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standard_profiles_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.<br /></span></h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de2f374-10a4-46da-90f5-293c5c454815"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Cut references</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Cutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcccb683-4eb4-41b1-8bde-5ad16b34b792"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Upcoming Updates</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Upcoming Updates</h2><p>Important updates coming with the next major version release:</p><ul><li data-uuid="cd9e397e-170f-48b0-9e6b-62fa1517af3c"><strong>OpenJDK version change. </strong>Desktop applications (<span>MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect</span>) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK <span>17.0.7+7</span> version. </li><li data-uuid="9b6c978c-b1fa-44aa-a31c-c939ede712bd"><strong>JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The JavaScript<span> </span></span><em>Nashorn</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript<span> </span><em>Rhino</em><span> </span>instead. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the deprecated JavaScript Nashorn >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="83692289-5550-47be-a1bf-75c4c62b71c1"><strong>Record Macro functionality discontinuation. </strong>The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.<strong> </strong></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="e46fe60a-b1de-4a37-86d8-bdcbb9fb5573"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCSE2022xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Cyber Systems Engineer Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Cyber Systems Engineer 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="17a9df73-3bfd-4b5e-9c51-c147e255edd9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CSM2022xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo Systems Modeler Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Systems Modeler 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742926 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: (2022x Refresh2) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- page_id: `272742926`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742926/2022x+Refresh2+Magic+Model+Analyst+Cameo+Simulation+Toolkit
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:41.842+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit2022x Refresh2 presents various enhancements to server-side simulation, including the ability to generate Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs) for external co-simulation, live Timeline charts allowing real-time monitoring and analysis of simulation data, and improved server-simulation with UI. Additionally, this release introduces triangular distribution support for random input generation, support for Behavior Ports, enhancements to the Watch Pane, including the ability to export Watch items to a SelectPropertiesConfig, and more.

#### Server-Side Simulation

Magic Model Analyst /Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh2 presents multiple server-side simulation improvements, making model analysis more effective and providing a better user experience.

##### Building Server-Side Simulation FMU

The latest version of Magic Model Analyst /Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces a powerful new feature: the ability to generate a Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU) for external co-simulation of dynamic models. This functionality allows you to exchange simulation results between different simulation tools, enabling a more collaborative and integrated approach to modeling and simulation.

You can easily create an FMU which exposes the model's input and output parameters defined in a Simulation Configuration. This FMU can then be used to run server-side simulations of this Simulation Configuration, utilizing the provided inputs and returning outputs after each time step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating an FMU and using it in another simulation tool.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating FMU - Technology preview' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Live Timeline Charts

Server-side simulation now supports live Timeline charts. With this latest update, you can view Activity and State plots in real-time, making monitoring and analyzing your simulation data easier and more efficient.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A live Timeline Chart.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation with UI' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

##### Improvement to Server-Simulation with UI

When simulating a Simulation Configuration with a specified UI, you can now open an index UI window that displays all UI elements in separate boxes. An index UI window allows you to easily reorder and resize each individual UI, giving you more control and flexibility in customizing your simulation experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A UI index window where you can reorder and resize all existing UIs.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation with UI' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

#### Triangular Distribution Support

In addition to the widely-used uniform and normal distributions, the new version of Magic Model Analyst /Cameo Simulation Toolkit nowsupports triangular distribution for generating random input values. This new feature offers greater flexibility in model simulation and can help you achieve more accurate simulation results. After applying the «triangular» stereotype, you can easily generate random input values based on the minimum, maximum, and peak values specified for a given Value Property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A triangular distribution chart.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Distribution Extensions' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

#### Behavior Port Support

Magic Model Analyst /Cameo Simulation Toolkit now includes support for Behavior Ports during simulation. If a port is designated as a behavior, all incoming requests will be immediately passed to the owning classifier while ignoring all delegation connectors.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Internal Block Diagram with a Behavior Port.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

#### Watch Pane Improvements

Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2022x Refresh2 introduces several improvements to the Watch Pane:

- You can now export the list of Watch items to a new or existing SelectPropertiesConfig by using the Watch Pane toolbar.
- The Watch Pane can now display the Requirement and Margin columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting the list of Watch items to a SelectPropertiesConfig.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Watch pane' space='CST2022xR2']>]]>

#### Other Improvements

- The new version of Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to create an Internal Transition and set its trigger or effect by simply using the drag-and-drop functionality.
- The Select Port dialog now displays only the Ports that are associated with the context. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Model Analyst Documentation' space='MSI2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742258 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: (2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `272742258`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742258/2022x+Refresh2+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:06.944+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

The 2022x Refresh2 release introduces performance improvements to the client-side as well as general modeling and collaborative modeling enhancements.

The client-side performance enhancements include improved validation performance and decreased table opening time.

For general modeling, this release presents new syncing with Excel options, the ability to temporarily activate or deactivate legends without the diagram lock, and automated package structure creation.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is improved with a new file exchange format .szip, allowing faster transfer of models between OEMs and suppliers, the ability to view Teamwork Cloud data markings in MagicDraw, and other enhancements. Also, Power'By has been updated with standard profiles migration and the ability to cut used project references if the used project is not found.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

- Validation Performance Improvements**Validation Performance Improvements.** Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the **Validate Elements Recursively** action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.

- Table Performance Improvements**Table** Performance Improvements. Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. Now it is 5 times faster.

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the Select Columns dialog's Properties tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png']

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

##### New Sync with Excel OptionsNew Syncing with Excel File Options

Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:

- **Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.** If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool.

- **Color marking modified data upon export.** You can now color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes in the Excel file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.

- **Mapping elements by ID upon export.** From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### *Check user permissions* row is added to the synced Excel file *Constraints in Generic Table* without overwriting the data already existing in the file.

- **Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.** You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the **Sync Plain Text** and **Import Plain Text** check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export without affecting the styling specified in the file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Sync Plain Text**check box in the **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog and the **Import Plain Text** check box in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Manipulate Legends without Diagram LockManipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button, allowingyou to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Temporarily suspending the applied Legend *Status* via **Suspend/Active** toggle button.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Automated Package Structure CreationAutomated Package Structure Creation

You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the**Create Package Structure**action.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The package structure configuration element *SubsystemPackages*defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. 
This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element *Subsystem 3 Design*via the **Create Package Structure**action.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated Package Structure Creation' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

#### CollaborationCollaboration

##### TWCTeamwork Cloud

###### New FileNew File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)

Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!

###### TWC Data MarkingsTeamwork Cloud Data Markings in MagicDraw

Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in **Open Server Project / Manage Project** and **Use Server Project** dialogs in MagicDraw. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.

###### Set as latestEnhanced Set as Latest Functionality

The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.

###### Pre-merge checkPre-merge Check of Project Permissions

We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible. 
**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.

###### Recursive searchRecursive Global Element Usage Search

Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the **Used By in Server Recursively** option for the **Search in Server**action.**Used By in Server Recursively** works similarly to **Used by in Server**, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Used By in Server Recursively action.

###### Other fixesOther fixes/improvements

- Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the **Conflicting Changes** panel.
- **Remove, keep References** operation has been improved to work faster.

##### Power3DEXPERIENCE Platform – Power’By

###### **Standard Profiles**Standard Profiles Migration

To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.

###### Cut referencesCutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found

If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.

#### Upcoming UpdatesUpcoming Updates

Important updates coming with the next major version release:

- OpenJDK version change. Desktop applications ( MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect ) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK 17.0.7+7 version.
- JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. The JavaScript Nashorn language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript*Rhino*instead. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>
- Record Macro functionality discontinuation. The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.

#### **resources**Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Software Architect Documentation' space='MSA2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a571d67-6c72-4e26-ba35-8174d8f06643"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Released on: July 7, 2023</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The 2022x Refresh2 release introduces performance improvements to the client-side as well as general modeling and collaborative modeling enhancements.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The client-side performance enhancements include improved validation performance and decreased table opening time.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For general modeling, this release presents new syncing with Excel options, the ability to temporarily activate or deactivate legends without the diagram lock, and automated package structure creation.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Additionally, collaborative modeling is improved with a new file exchange format .szip, allowing faster transfer of models between OEMs and suppliers, the ability to view Teamwork Cloud data markings in MagicDraw, and other enhancements. Also, Power'By has been updated with standard profiles migration and the ability to cut used project references if the used project is not found.</span></p><p>To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="558d6ae2-3570-4ea4-8214-ed1c8eaabdf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note"
````
