improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5cb0ce0-ea4d-49e5-942e-eb94c9eb9e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client side performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="ee7a2c6e-477e-4a47-948b-aee88216a00a"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2f4fd47-9036-4122-915c-2221c5ede03f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Validation Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Validation Performance Improvements.</strong> Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.<br /><br /></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Performance Improvements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the <strong>Validate Elements Recursively</strong> action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.<br /><br /></h6><ul><li data-uuid="1c8a145d-52b8-4290-8795-56f699bc7c7b"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee1f30b4-276e-465b-8d3a-73b6104f41d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Table Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Table </strong></span><strong>Performance Improvements. </strong>Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Table Performance Improvements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. <span>Now it is 5 times faster.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d616641-bea0-4f1b-afb4-fb3996706237"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="ac0ab59a-9f8c-49c6-85e5-f3e2491de2db">From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog's <strong>Properties </strong>tab.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88793ec1-a4a9-4dbc-9b84-53543c984962"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New Sync with Excel Options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New Syncing with Excel File Options</span></h3><p><span>Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="913b7409-8d5f-4f9a-82e7-d276fffc6f8e"><span> <strong>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.</strong> </span><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.</span> </span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="skipping_rows.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="a756f299-8e8c-4297-88bf-317e783151c7"><span> <strong>Color marking modified data upon export. </strong></span>You can now color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes in the Excel file.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="0c518461-89f8-437e-ae72-5e171bf5e710"><span> <strong>Mapping elements by ID upon export.</strong> </span>From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mapping_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><em>Check user permissions</em> row is added to the synced Excel file <em>Constraints in Generic Table</em> without overwriting the data already existing in the file. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="2a5f597d-9a39-4cfa-ba33-f86a9143cfc9"><span><strong>Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.</strong> You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the <strong>Sync Plain Text</strong> and <strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export without affecting the styling specified in the file.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_plain_text_check_boxes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The <strong>Sync Plain Text </strong>check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog and the <strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog. </span></h6><p><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Syncing with Excel files >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24ac508c-de55-4bdf-98cb-c4dca6796adc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button, allowing</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> you to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Suspend_Mode_for_Applied_Legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Temporarily suspending the applied Legend <em>Status</em> via <strong>Suspend/Active</strong> toggle button.</h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb2b2e4b-cdd9-466f-aebc-a4bb2ee95b3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FKfl9rDorec&amp;t=7s" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Manipulating Legends without Diagram Lock >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f15b693c-102b-4d83-859e-88d806a1a6b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Automated Package Structure Creation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Automated Package Structure Creation</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the<span> </span></span><strong>Create Package Structure</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>action.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Automated Package Structure Creation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The package structure configuration element <em>SubsystemPackages </em>defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. <br />This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element <em>Subsystem 3 Design </em>via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>action. </span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Automated Package Structure Creation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2a3e277-67ff-49eb-8666-39ed7c6d17c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82578d71-581b-42b4-bfdc-d5dcd49ff7cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec7378b7-8301-4eb8-be02-cf9ec9d79654"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New File</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)</span></h4><p>Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!</p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75a73823-35c3-4fd0-a007-d714741b92b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC Data Markings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud Data Markings in MagicDraw</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in <strong>Open Server Project / Manage Project</strong> and <strong>Use Server Project</strong> dialogs in MagicDraw. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_data_markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.</h6><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b246181-664e-4309-91cd-f43601f70e3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Set as latest</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Enhanced Set as Latest Functionality</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_as_latest.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b023512-1cc9-4276-8798-333024967e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Pre-merge check</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Pre-merge Check of Project Permissions</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible.</span><br /><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="per_merge_check.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f4c9a7-e74c-43a8-9ca8-23beb9ac1ee1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Recursive search</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Recursive Global Element Usage Search</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the <strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> option for the <strong>Search in Server </strong>action. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> works similarly to <strong>Used by in Server</strong>, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_by_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Used By in Server Recursively action.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d720d13a-83e8-4b09-9474-2a04e7796ba9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other fixes</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other fixes/improvements</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="7f9bfac4-89d7-4822-a27d-b00503b51c83"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the <strong>Conflicting Changes</strong> panel.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="b4dd86ff-5bed-4196-970c-1d946720e229"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong style="text-align: left;">Remove, keep References</strong> operation has been improved to work faster.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfdd0694-20ca-468f-858e-b8118a0d2206"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Power</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>3DEXPERIENCE <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Platform – Power’By</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11315306-8328-4395-bd48-880be3dc24e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Standard Profiles</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Standard Profiles Migration</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="236"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standard_profiles_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.<br /></span></h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de2f374-10a4-46da-90f5-293c5c454815"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Cut references</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Cutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcccb683-4eb4-41b1-8bde-5ad16b34b792"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Upcoming Updates</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Upcoming Updates</h2><p>Important updates coming with the next major version release:</p><ul><li data-uuid="558895a1-c7a8-4a6e-9ea9-7b445b8435eb"><strong>OpenJDK version change. </strong>Desktop applications (<span>MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect</span>) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK <span>17.0.7+7</span> version. </li><li data-uuid="151e4e04-2ade-4c84-a928-7833429076e1"><strong>JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The JavaScript<span> </span></span><em>Nashorn</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript<span> </span><em>Rhino</em><span> </span>instead. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the deprecated JavaScript Nashorn >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="74070afe-001b-48a8-b194-0b7ed80b5328"><strong>Record Macro functionality discontinuation. </strong>The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.<strong> </strong></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb63ce8f-8c35-4c05-ab0e-c34c56a1bf70"><ac:parameter ac:name="">resources</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="3fd124f9-e8e1-4428-8e62-6999e79a5bb0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSA2022xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Software Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Software Architect 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2ff50d4f-41a8-4e14-a667-ce9739d7661d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="MagicDraw Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742350 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00099: (2022x Refresh2) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `272742350`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742350/2022x+Refresh2+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-27T16:44:49.092+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

The latest release of the 2022x Refresh2 version introduces several notable enhancements. These include the incorporation of UAF Behavior to Structure synchronization, an improved swimlane representative selection dialog, the addition of NAF 4.0 diagrams and tables, and the implementation of background actions logging. Additionally, you can expect improvements to the Gantt Chart and various other enhancements.

This version introduces improvements to MagicGrid projects, as now you can easily create additional MagicGrid package structures for components and subsystems in MagicGrid projects.

The 2022x Refresh2 release introduces performance improvements to both the client-side and Teamwork Cloud-side, as well as general modeling and collaborative modeling enhancements.

The client-side performance enhancements include improved validation performance and decreased table opening time.

For general modeling, this release presents new syncing with Excel options, the ability to temporarily activate or deactivate legends without the diagram lock, and automated package structure creation.

Additionally, collaborative modeling is improved with a technology preview ofa new file exchange format .szip, allowing faster transfer of models between OEMs and suppliers, the ability to view Teamwork Cloud data markings in Magic Software Architect, and other enhancements. Also, Power'By has been updated with standard profiles migration and the ability to cut used project references if the used project is not found.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 3' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 2' space='']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 available

2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### UAF Features

##### UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization

Keeping your Internal Connectivity diagrams consistent with Activities can be tedious, but UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization can simplify the process. With UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization, achieving full synchronization between your structure model and behavior model has never been easier. This feature validates Asset Roles in Internal Connectivity diagrams, checks for the presence of Ports that are typed by compatible Interfaces, and identifies any missing Connectors and Exchanges to be created automatically.

With this technology, coherence between your Activities and Internal Connectivity diagrams will be maintained at any model maturity level. If any inconsistencies arise, the system will detect them and provide you with individual solutions to address each one; thus, you can save valuable time and energy while ensuring the accuracy and consistency of your model.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Swimlane Representative

The Swimlane representative selection dialog has been enhanced. Now when creating swimlanes, you will be prompted to select both property types and properties.

- The available types: Operational Agent / Service / Resource Performer / Organizational Resource
- The available properties: Operational / Service / Resource Roles

Properties contained by the Context and/or Performer of IsCapabaleToPerform relation will be pre-selected by default when:

- Context and/or Is Capable To Perform relations are known,
- Context and/or Performer (which acts as an Activity Context) has structural elements (Operational / Service / Resource Roles).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Framework and Diagramming

###### NAF 4.0 Framework improvements

The NAF 4.0 Framework improvements were implemented in this version by adding the new Taxonomy diagram and tables. Click each diagram or table hyperlink to learn more:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with L1 Node Types Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with P1 Resource Types Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']

###### Background actions logging

Beginning with the 2022x Refresh2 version, the Background Actions Logging Feature will be available. This feature aims to provide more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but cannot be shown in the diagram. The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them from the Notification window to the Containment tree.

The affected UAF diagrams include the following:

- Strategic Phasing Diagram
- Projects Roadmap
- Services Roadmap
- Personnel Evolution
- Resources Evolution
- Personnel Availability Gantt Chart
- Personnel Forecast
- Resources Forecast
- Standards Roadmap

This feature is implemented in UAF and all supported Architecture Framework diagrams.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

700400

###### Gantt Charts improvements

- The Gantt Chart diagrams were enhanced to display bars with triangles when the Start Date is specified and the End Date is empty:
  - Project Roadmap [ATTACHMENT filename='project_roadmap.png']
  - Actual Strategic Phases Gannt Chart [ATTACHMENT filename='actual_strategic_phases.png']
- Adding new Actual Projects and Actual Sub Projects in the Project Roadmap, the End Date is no longer filled out by default. You can always specify the End Date later. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- A new validation rule is added. This rule checks if Actual Projects connected with the Project Sequence relationship have End Dates set according to the specified Project Sequence type/kind . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COR2346' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- The filtering in Gantt Chart diagrams was improved to match how the filtering works in the tables. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2022xR2']>>]]>

##### Usability improvements

- In compliance with the UAF 1.2 specification, the new column Kind is implemented in the Strategic Taxonomy Table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Switching between projects with different Architecture Framework related perspectives has been improved. When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a new message is displayed. In that message, you can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Instance Specification related actions have been improved:
  - When you drag an Instance Specification and drop it on another Instance Specification shape, a slot is assigned by default if the structure between Instance Specification types is defined respectively.
  - When you drag and drop or move the Instance Specification shape inside another Instance Specification, the first action in the shortcut menu is Move Here .
- The Strategic Processes diagram is now suggested by default when you create instances for a Strategic Phase and choose to create a diagram afterward.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Other Enhancements

- Migration to UAF1.2: the migration experience was enhanced. Now you will be informed about cyclic, directly, and indirectly used projects so that you can solve those usages in the correct order and continue the migration successfully. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How to migrate' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- Property Set now groups measurements and a direct measurement name is added to the PropertySet name in the Specification Window.
- The setting of Stream for UAF Parameters or Pins with specified Sync Element has been simplified by adding a Stream command to the shortcut menu.
- The Operational/Resource Role Impact Analysis Map creation from the selected context element - Operational/Resource Role - is now improved.
- The Resource/Service/Operational Exchange item selection window, accessible from the Resources/Services/Operational Connectivity table or Exchange Manager window, has been enhanced. The Conveyed items are now filtered according to the selected Resources/Services/Operational Exchange kind.
- The Problem Domain property creation under Operational Performer and Operational Architecture in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram has been enhanced.
- The Security Process Flow diagram now includes a fix that ensures the creation of Operational and Resource Parameters with the correct stereotype.
- The diagram palette of the Entity Relation Diagram has been simplified by leaving only the Information Model group instead of Conceptual, Logical, and Physical. Also, a Strategic Information button was added.
- The symbol styles for Service Interface and Whole Life Enterprise have been fixed.
- The solver for the COR2012 validation rule was changed from Remove Conveyed Item to Add/Remove Conveyed Item . The updated Add/Remove Conveyed Item solver adds a conveyed item if it is missing or asks to choose which conveyed item should be removed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COR2012' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

#### SysML Features

##### Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid ProjectsAutomated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects

2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the **Create Package Structure** command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the **Create Package Structure**command.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using packages' space='SYSMLP2022xR2']>]]>

#### Client side performance improvementsClient-side Performance Improvements

- Validation Performance Improvements**Validation Performance Improvements.** Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the **Validate Elements Recursively** action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.

- Table Performance Improvements**Table** Performance Improvements. Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. Now it is 5 times faster.

##### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the Select Columns dialog's Properties tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png']

#### miModeling and Infrastructure

##### New Sync with Excel OptionsNew Syncing with Excel File Options

Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:

- **Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.** If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool.

- **Color marking modified data upon export.**You can now color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes and make necessary adjustments in the Excel file based on this information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.

- **Mapping elements by ID upon export.** From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### *Check user permissions* row is added to the synced Excel file *Constraints in Generic Table* without overwriting the data already existing in the file.

- **Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.** You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the **Sync Plain Text**and **Import Plain Text**check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Sync Plain Text**check box in the **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog and the **Import Plain Text** check box in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Manipulate Legends without Diagram LockManipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button, allowingyou to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Temporarily suspending the applied Legend *Status* via **Suspend/Active** toggle button.

To learn more, watch the following video:

700400

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

##### Automated Package Structure CreationAutomated Package Structure Creation

You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the**Create Package Structure**action.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The package structure configuration element *SubsystemPackages*defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. 
This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element *Subsystem 3 Design*via the **Create Package Structure**action.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated Package Structure Creation' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>

#### CollaborationCollaboration

##### TWCTeamwork Cloud

###### New FileNew File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)

Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!

###### TWC Data MarkingsTeamwork Cloud Data Markings in Magic Software Architect

Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in **Open Server Project / Manage Project** and **Use Server Project** dialogs in Magic Software Architect. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.

###### Set as latestEnhanced Set as Latest Functionality

The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.

###### Pre-merge checkPre-merge Check of Project Permissions

We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible. 
**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.

###### Recursive searchRecursive Global Element Usage Search

Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the **Used By in Server Recursively** option for the **Search in Server**action.**Used By in Server Recursively** works similarly to **Used by in Server**, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Used By in Server Recursively action.

###### Other fixesOther fixes/improvements

- Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the **Conflicting Changes** panel.
- **Remove, keep References** operation has been improved to work faster.

##### Power3DEXPERIENCE Platform – Power’By

###### **Standard Profiles**Standard Profiles Migration

To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.

###### Cut referencesCutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found

If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.

#### Upcoming UpdatesUpcoming Updates

Important updates coming with the next major version release:

- OpenJDK version change. Desktop applications ( MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect ) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK 17.0.7+7 version.
- JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. The JavaScript Nashorn language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript*Rhino*instead. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space='MD2022xR2']>]]>
- Record Macro functionality discontinuation. The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation' space='MSOSA2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation' space='CEA2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This feature validates Asset Roles in Internal Connectivity diagrams, checks for the presence of Ports that are typed by compatible Interfaces, and identifies any missing Connectors and Exchanges to be created automatically.</p><p>With this technology, coherence between your Activities and Internal Connectivity diagrams will be maintained at any model maturity level. If any inconsistencies arise, the system will detect them and provide you with individual solutions to address each one; thus, you can save valuable time and energy while ensuring the accuracy and consistency of your model.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1119"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF BtoSSync Reprezentation.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Swimlane Representative</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The Swimlane representative selection dialog has been enhanced. Now when creating swimlanes,  you will be prompted to select both property types and properties.</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="583b0394-c989-46af-847e-9a22b9e0d4f6">The available types: Operational Agent / Service / Resource Performer / Organizational Resource</li><li data-uuid="f3303cc1-7eba-4a97-8526-bf79779b0fd1">The available properties: Operational / Service / Resource Roles</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">Properties contained by the Context and/or Performer of IsCapabaleToPerform relation will be pre-selected by default when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5cc90607-0311-446d-9cd6-46939dc601bf">Context and/or Is Capable To Perform relations are known,</li><li data-uuid="5ada46e3-bf11-47b0-a855-d30e5af6cd32">Context and/or Performer (which acts as an Activity Context) has structural elements (Operational / Service / Resource Roles).</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_Swimlane_Representatives_dialog_property_and_property_types.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Framework and Diagramming</h3><h4 style="text-align: left;">NAF 4.0 Framework improvements</h4><p style="text-align: left;">The NAF 4.0 Framework improvements were implemented in this version by adding the new Taxonomy diagram and tables. Click each diagram or table hyperlink to learn more:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0c01b207-840d-4074-9bff-9cd62b729b72"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Taxonomy Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4d3c27c0-44d8-4727-ac06-4fefe1d4acd4"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li data-uuid="afdc3b37-47b9-4d42-a658-94675e2d28c6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1 Capability Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li data-uuid="22717cb9-b4cc-458b-8aa0-f0dd1bc5c030"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[S1 Service Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9f99a34b-e9e8-42d6-a8da-f345c3907784"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with L1 Node Types Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 Node Types Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e831470f-48fb-42ea-848b-c453c91d4cb0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with P1 Resource Types Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Resource Types Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4 style="text-align: left;">Background actions logging </h4><p>Beginning with the 2022x Refresh2 version, the Background Actions Logging Feature will be available. This feature aims to provide more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but cannot be shown in the diagram. The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them from the Notification window to the Containment tree.</p><p style="text-align: left;"> The affected UAF diagrams include the following:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="dee90bc4-b4c3-4a35-8c94-5b2b23b6cb3e">Strategic Phasing Diagram</li><li data-uuid="b887d8a1-6831-4fc6-93fa-d14ea9142346">Projects Roadmap</li><li data-uuid="a509407f-e524-4198-a195-347aedd9c431">Services Roadmap</li><li data-uuid="ce56006d-bf45-4e28-a00b-97877adda8e1">Personnel Evolution</li><li data-uuid="ad87bb49-f55d-46cc-a191-e95e12b52bd2">Resources Evolution</li><li data-uuid="cff9e8a9-2751-425c-91ea-f0a31638e6f8">Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</li><li data-uuid="7b5ab0fc-fe98-4b3f-bfbf-793041ea7f50">Personnel Forecast</li><li data-uuid="9e708334-9d3b-407a-b9c8-2560624a8ec4">Resources Forecast</li><li data-uuid="68ae2cf0-7166-4e6d-b832-cea5192c764f">Standards Roadmap</li></ul><p>This feature is implemented in UAF and all supported Architecture Framework diagrams.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24bfdb52-4051-41f9-89d9-46154960043f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PvPqofgM5PQ" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Gantt Charts improvements</h4><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="25bdc423-7630-4d74-bb5a-77b3c6267bed">The Gantt Chart diagrams were enhanced to display bars with triangles when the Start Date is specified and the End Date is empty:<ul><li>Project Roadmap<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_roadmap.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Actual Strategic Phases Gannt Chart<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_strategic_phases.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="bf7bfd23-82ac-4003-90a2-ff9ce4a7c14d">Adding new Actual Projects and Actual Sub Projects in the Project Roadmap, the End Date is no longer filled out by default. You can always specify the End Date later. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Roadmap diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="cc21b79f-0a02-45c1-9ef2-e376c7e99f19">A new validation rule is added. This rule checks if Actual Projects connected with the Project Sequence relationship have End Dates set according to the specified Project Sequence type/kind<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="COR2346" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="eb3b98a5-8f8e-46d2-9a52-91969ceb7b66">The filtering in Gantt Chart diagrams was improved to match how the filtering works in the tables. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Usability improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="d8c4a9e1-7ac0-4f64-a4b9-6e71e8d1de5a">In compliance with the UAF 1.2 specification, the new column Kind is implemented in the Strategic Taxonomy Table.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_column_strategic_taxonomy.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="b5bed4f5-ef85-4f4f-9a5d-4d2731561359">Switching between projects with different Architecture Framework related perspectives has been improved. When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a new message is displayed. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In that message, you can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_projects_diff_perspectives.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="f1ab0b11-f1ec-43c8-ba59-6d4584c5b42d">The Instance Specification related actions have been improved:<ul><li>When you drag an Instance Specification and drop it on another Instance Specification shape, a slot is assigned by default if the structure between Instance Specification types is defined respectively.</li><li>When you drag and drop or move the Instance Specification shape inside another Instance Specification, the first action in the shortcut menu is <strong>Move Here</strong>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b2ea8e3f-3085-4dd2-8879-2bacd50f02e2">The Strategic Processes diagram is now suggested by default when you create instances for a Strategic Phase and choose to create a diagram afterward.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_process_for_instances.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="859d2814-0f49-40d1-aa81-b20eafb94ca2"><strong>Migration to UAF1.2:</strong> the migration experience was enhanced. Now you will be informed about cyclic, directly, and indirectly used projects so that you can solve those usages in the correct order and continue the migration successfully. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="How to migrate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about migration>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about migration issues on Teamwork Cloud>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1aa82cd2-37f8-4a6b-ae04-8327421bace9">Property Set now groups measurements and<span> a </span><em>direct</em><span> </span>measurement name is added to the PropertySet name in the Specification Window.</li><li data-uuid="b373a1e1-af55-43ef-bf82-51b910490d0a">The setting of Stream for UAF Parameters or Pins with specified Sync Element has been simplified by adding a Stream command to the shortcut menu.<span> </span></li><li data-uuid="deec3d55-df4f-4244-8126-92ff006bdcdb">The Operational/Resource Role Impact Analysis Map creation from the selected context element - Operational/Resource Role - is now improved.</li><li data-uuid="45306a60-8ba6-4ca5-98a8-f2c759bdd941">The Resource/Service/Operational Exchange item selection window, accessible from the Resources/Services/Operational Connectivity table or Exchange Manager window, has been enhanced. The Conveyed items are now filtered according to the selected Resources/Services/Operational Exchange kind.</li><li data-uuid="596952e7-c992-460f-bcda-5fd23deafbf5">The Problem Domain property creation under Operational Performer and Operational Architecture in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram has been enhanced.</li><li data-uuid="2b3ef9ee-ac22-41ca-89b9-6dab5634ff8f"><span>The Security Process Flow diagram now includes a fix that ensures the creation of Operational and Resource Parameters with the correct stereotype.</span></li><li data-uuid="012f46c7-c8f1-4e1c-a382-52cc283afb7f">The diagram palette of the Entity Relation Diagram has been simplified by leaving only the Information Model group instead of <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Conceptual, Logical, and Physical. Also, a Strategic Information button was added.</span></li><li data-uuid="ade6c3a8-5b1c-4483-9054-4f32fc4db129">The symbol styles for Service Interface and Whole Life Enterprise have been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="58c0e6b9-9b10-49b9-8d99-b44af6b2dde1">The solver for the COR2012 validation rule was changed from <strong>Remove Conveyed Item</strong> to <strong>Add/Remove Conveyed Item</strong>. The updated <strong>Add/Remove Conveyed Item </strong>solver <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">adds a conveyed item if it is missing or asks to choose which conveyed item should be removed.</span><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="COR2012" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><h2>SysML Features</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83f7a371-5896-4ce4-bef8-16936fb15a4b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects </h3><p>2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the <strong>Create Package Structure</strong> command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_Package_Structure.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>command.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR2" ri:content-title="Using packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5cb0ce0-ea4d-49e5-942e-eb94c9eb9e18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Client side performance improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Client-side Performance Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="12d12508-7c57-406c-a878-f28ba1c19726"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2f4fd47-9036-4122-915c-2221c5ede03f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Validation Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Validation Performance Improvements.</strong> Running the validation on the entire model or a selected part is 2-5 times faster, depending on the model, than in 2022x Refresh1.<br /></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Performance Improvements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the time it takes to execute the <strong>Validate Elements Recursively</strong> action on an entire project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2.<br /><br /></h6><ul><li data-uuid="2b9db2ac-7139-4579-acf5-f209ba2b6e31"><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee1f30b4-276e-465b-8d3a-73b6104f41d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Table Performance Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Table </strong></span><strong>Performance Improvements. </strong>Opening a table is now significantly faster than in 2022x Refresh1. Tables with a large number of rows and columns (especially custom columns) will have noticeably decreased opening times.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Table Performance Improvements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart comparing the opening time of a table in a SysML project in 2022x Refresh1 and 2022x Refresh2. <span>Now it is 5 times faster.</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d616641-bea0-4f1b-afb4-fb3996706237"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="405559da-7cc0-4e03-b3a4-2b326dba570a">From now on, when a classifier containing ports is specified in an Instance table, the ports, along with their nested structure, are available for selection in the <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog's <strong>Properties </strong>tab.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ports_In_Select_Columns_Instance_tables.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86c1e37a-6f77-4e5e-aa46-ea5b738c8c41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mi</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Modeling and Infrastructure</h2><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88793ec1-a4a9-4dbc-9b84-53543c984962"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New Sync with Excel Options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New Syncing with Excel File Options</span></h3><p><span>Syncing with Excel files in tables is now improved with additional options:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="5266f7ed-2342-43d5-b5ae-b1a2988cf954"><span> <strong>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import.</strong> </span><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your Excel file contains any purposely created empty rows or columns, you can now skip them instead of stopping the import at the empty row or column, allowing you more control over the data import.</span> </span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="skipping_rows.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All data in the Excel file, including data following the empty row No. 5, is imported to the modeling tool. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="fa650c76-e190-414d-b7fc-5ad3675d5a0b"><span> <strong>Color marking modified data upon export. </strong>You can now </span>color-mark exported table data based on its changes. This allows you to easily review data changes and make necessary adjustments in the Excel file based on this information.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exported table data is color-marked based on its changes. Red indicates removed, blue - updated, and green - added data.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="a6246a2e-329b-4b9c-b806-3029307f5b16"><span> <strong>Mapping elements by ID upon export.</strong> </span>From now on, you can map elements by ID upon export, which allows you to export new additional table data to a synced file without overwriting previously existing content in the file.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mapping_elements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><em>Check user permissions</em> row is added to the synced Excel file <em>Constraints in Generic Table</em> without overwriting the data already existing in the file. </h6><ul><li data-uuid="ea813ee5-8554-4392-b179-ed3145ebea47"><span><strong>Syncing data in Plain Text or HTML upon import/export.</strong> You now have the option to import/export data in HTML (default) or Plain Text via the <strong>Sync Plain Text </strong>and <strong>Import Plain Text </strong>check box options, allowing you to choose the data format for the import/export.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sync_plain_text_check_boxes.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The <strong>Sync Plain Text </strong>check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog and the <strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog. </span></h6><p><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Syncing with Excel files >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24ac508c-de55-4bdf-98cb-c4dca6796adc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now temporarily activate or deactivate a Legend via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button, allowing</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> you to preview a diagram or table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Suspend_Mode_for_Applied_Legends.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Temporarily suspending the applied Legend <em>Status</em> via <strong>Suspend/Active</strong> toggle button.</h6><p>To learn more, watch the following video:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dd5e88f-47c8-4d11-9366-72c5754bfefe"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FKfl9rDorec&amp;ab_channel=CATIAMBSE" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Manipulating Legends without Diagram Lock >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f15b693c-102b-4d83-859e-88d806a1a6b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Automated Package Structure Creation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>Automated Package Structure Creation</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now easily create additional package structures you may require in your model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting an existing package structure. Now you can do that efficiently by defining your package structure configurations and then creating those structures via the<span> </span></span><strong>Create Package Structure</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>action.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Automated Package Structure Creation.png" /></ac:image></span></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The package structure configuration element <em>SubsystemPackages </em>defines the subsystems package structure for a Block element. <br />This subsystems package structure is created for the Block element <em>Subsystem 3 Design </em>via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>action. </span></h6><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Automated Package Structure Creation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2a3e277-67ff-49eb-8666-39ed7c6d17c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Collaboration</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Collaboration</h2><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82578d71-581b-42b4-bfdc-d5dcd49ff7cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec7378b7-8301-4eb8-be02-cf9ec9d79654"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New File</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New File Exchange Format for Server Projects (Technology Preview)</span></h4><p>Until now, .mdzip files were used for swapping projects in disconnected environments; however, this format is not ideal as there are issues with slow performance and project structural changes. We wanted to solve both problems to ensure faster project export and import while retaining the same project structure. Therefore, we present a technology preview of a new file exchange format: .szip. This format can be used for project export with or without used projects, adding a project as a new server project, and updating a project from a local project. We encourage our users to try out this format, which allows faster model transfer. Do not hesitate to share your feedback with us!</p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75a73823-35c3-4fd0-a007-d714741b92b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">TWC Data Markings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Teamwork Cloud Data Markings in Magic Software Architect</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Teamwork Cloud data markings are now shown in <strong>Open Server Project / Manage Project</strong> and <strong>Use Server Project</strong> dialogs in Magic Software Architect. Additionally, a check is performed to ensure that users are not trying to open or use a project with a higher access level than they are assigned, in which case a notification is displayed.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_data_markings.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Opening a server project with Teamwork Cloud data markings shown.</h6><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b246181-664e-4309-91cd-f43601f70e3a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Set as latest</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Enhanced Set as Latest Functionality</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Set as Latest functionality was updated and now allows users to set a specific version as the latest in another branch. Therefore, it is now possible to quickly set a particular version of the branch to be the latest in the trunk without time-consuming merging processes.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_as_latest.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">New Select Branch... option of the Set as Latest functionality.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b023512-1cc9-4276-8798-333024967e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Pre-merge check</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Pre-merge Check of Project Permissions</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">We introduced the ability to check permissions before merging to avoid project merge delays due to delays in the delivery of error messages about insufficient permissions. The Check Permissions button is now available in the Merge Projects dialog, allowing users to see if the merge will be possible.</span><br /><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="per_merge_check.png" /></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Merge Projects dialog with the new Check permissions button.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f4c9a7-e74c-43a8-9ca8-23beb9ac1ee1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Recursive search</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Recursive Global Element Usage Search</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Previously, it was only possible to search for element usages individually, which is not an optimal solution in many cases. With 2022x Refresh2, we are introducing the <strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> option for the <strong>Search in Server </strong>action. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Used By in Server Recursively</strong> works similarly to <strong>Used by in Server</strong>, except it searches for usages of the selected element and all its child elements.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_by_recursively.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Used By in Server Recursively action.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d720d13a-83e8-4b09-9474-2a04e7796ba9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other fixes</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other fixes/improvements</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="df7a7f6e-cc4c-4d09-b945-1f3c1651e079"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Relations (including connectors) are now represented in more detail in the conflicting changes tree of the <strong>Conflicting Changes</strong> panel.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="c76b7ae5-1011-4c8e-b084-b1c57c3fa3c3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong style="text-align: left;">Remove, keep References</strong> operation has been improved to work faster.</span></li></ul><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfdd0694-20ca-468f-858e-b8118a0d2206"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Power</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>3DEXPERIENCE <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Platform – Power’By</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11315306-8328-4395-bd48-880be3dc24e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Standard Profiles</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>Standard Profiles Migration</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To successfully migrate from 2022x Refresh1 to 2022x Refresh2, Power’By users need to upgrade their standard profiles. To help them accomplish this, we have provided a dedicated filter that allows users to select standard profiles and migrate them in bulk.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="236"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standard_profiles_migration.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Selecting Standard Profiles in project migration dialog.<br /></span></h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2de2f374-10a4-46da-90f5-293c5c454815"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Cut references</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Cutting References If a Used Project Is Not Found</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If users try to open a main project and its used project or projects are not found, it is possible to remove the used project or projects and cut all references. This way, users can still work on the main project.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcccb683-4eb4-41b1-8bde-5ad16b34b792"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Upcoming Updates</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Upcoming Updates</h2><p>Important updates coming with the next major version release:</p><ul><li data-uuid="53c99be2-9fd3-476c-939c-c362ce3e7302"><strong>OpenJDK version change. </strong>Desktop applications (<span>MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect</span>) and Teamwork Cloud will only run using OpenJDK <span>17.0.7+7</span> version. </li><li data-uuid="5f1d6c9b-45e7-4072-b5ab-7263521ce87d"><strong>JavaScript Nashorn scripting removal. </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The JavaScript<span> </span></span><em>Nashorn</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>language usage is deprecated with the intention of removing it from the next major version. Please use JavaScript<span> </span><em>Rhino</em><span> </span>instead. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the deprecated JavaScript Nashorn >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="dbb0aa5d-b8a3-4ca7-a105-2a62076a0b51"><strong>Record Macro functionality discontinuation. </strong>The Record Macro functionality will be discontinued in the next major version release.<strong> </strong></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="7150de3c-2e77-4334-b246-1ef399a22f7d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MSOSA2022xR2" ri:content-title="Magic Systems of Systems Architect Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Systems of Systems Architect 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1c34cd23-ebcf-4fa4-b4ef-113c9197b6a1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CEA2022xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo Enterprise Architecture Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
