<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272742888 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: (2022x Refresh2) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `272742888`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272742888/2022x+Refresh2+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:37.557+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.

##### Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects

2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the **Create Package Structure** command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the **Create Package Structure**command.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using packages' space='SYSMLP2022xR2']>]]>

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: July 7, 2023</span></h6><p><br /></p><p>SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1 introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. These include an option to select which diagrams containing elements that realize Item Flows to update so they show the newly created flows. Also, a quick way to show/hide flowing items in a diagram, see the element type representation in the Item Flow Direction selection in the Create/Edit Item Flow wizard and auto-removal of not realized Item Flows. In addition, you can now perform the Requirements verification analysis in the Instance table.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects</h3><p>2022x Refresh2 introduces a means to easily create a MagicGrid-based package structure for a block that represents a logical subsystem or component within the solution domain model. Previously, this could have been achieved by copy-pasting the package structure of an existing subsystem or component; however, now you can use the <strong>Create Package Structure</strong> command, providing you with a fast and easy approach.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_Package_Structure.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) SysML v1 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a MagicGrid-based package structure via the <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>command.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR2" ri:content-title="Using packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Automated Package Structure Creation in MagicGrid Projects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Documentation</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="1c180d69-cebe-490e-b7e5-663cfdea3b06"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272743005 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00102: (2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin

- page_id: `272743005`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272743005/2022x+Refresh2+UAF+1.2+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T13:13:50.991+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x Refresh2) 2022x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 7, 2023

The latest release of the 2022x Refresh2 version introduces several notable enhancements. These include the incorporation of UAF Behavior to Structure synchronization, an improved swimlane representative selection dialog, the addition of NAF 4.0 diagrams and tables, and the implementation of background actions logging. Additionally, you can expect improvements to the Gantt Chart and various other enhancements.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2022xR2'].For further information, check the.

#### UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization

Keeping your Internal Connectivity diagrams consistent with Activities can be tedious, but UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization can simplify the process. With UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization, achieving full synchronization between your structure model and behavior model has never been easier. This feature validates Asset Roles in Internal Connectivity diagrams, checks for the presence of Ports that are typed by compatible Interfaces, and identifies any missing Connectors and Exchanges to be created automatically.

With this technology, coherence between your Activities and Internal Connectivity diagrams will be maintained at any model maturity level. If any inconsistencies arise, the system will detect them and provide you with individual solutions to address each one; thus, you can save valuable time and energy while ensuring the accuracy and consistency of your model.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Swimlane Representative

The Swimlane representative selection dialog has been enhanced. Now when creating swimlanes, you will be prompted to select both property types and properties.

- The available types: Operational Agent / Service / Resource Performer / Organizational Resource
- The available properties: Operational / Service / Resource Roles

Properties contained by the Context and/or Performer of IsCapabaleToPerform relation will be pre-selected by default when:

- Context and/or Is Capable To Perform relations are known,
- Context and/or Performer (which acts as an Activity Context) has structural elements (Operational / Service / Resource Roles).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Framework and Diagramming

##### NAF 4.0 Framework improvements

The NAF 4.0 Framework improvements were implemented in this version by adding the new Taxonomy diagram and tables. Click each diagram or table hyperlink to learn more:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with L1 Node Types Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with P1 Resource Types Table' space='UAF12P2022xR2']

###### Background actions logging

Beginning with the 2022x Refresh2 version, the Background Actions Logging Feature will be available. This feature aims to provide more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but cannot be shown in the diagram. The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them from the Notification window to the Containment tree.

The affected UAF diagrams include the following:

- Strategic Phasing Diagram
- Projects Roadmap
- Services Roadmap
- Personnel Evolution
- Resources Evolution
- Personnel Availability Gantt Chart
- Personnel Forecast
- Resources Forecast
- Standards Roadmap

This feature is implemented in UAF and all supported Architecture Framework diagrams.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

700400

##### Gantt Charts improvements

- The Gantt Chart diagrams were enhanced to display bars with triangles when the Start Date is specified and the End Date is empty:
  - Project Roadmap [ATTACHMENT filename='project_roadmap.png']
  - Actual Strategic Phases Gannt Chart [ATTACHMENT filename='actual_strategic_phases.png']
- Adding new Actual Projects and Actual Sub Projects in the Project Roadmap, the End Date is no longer filled out by default. You can always specify the End Date later. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- A new validation rule is added. This rule checks if Actual Projects connected with the Project Sequence relationship have End Dates set according to the specified Project Sequence type/kind . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COR2346' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- The filtering in Gantt Chart diagrams was improved to match how the filtering works in the tables. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2022xR2']>>]]>

#### Usability improvements

- In compliance with the UAF 1.2 specification, the new column Kind is implemented in the Strategic Taxonomy Table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Switching between projects with different Architecture Framework related perspectives has been improved. When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a new message is displayed. In that message, you can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Instance Specification related actions have been improved:
  - When you drag an Instance Specification and drop it on another Instance Specification shape, a slot is assigned by default if the structure between Instance Specification types is defined respectively.
  - When you drag and drop or move the Instance Specification shape inside another Instance Specification, the first action in the shortcut menu is Move Here .
- The Strategic Processes diagram is now suggested by default when you create instances for a Strategic Phase and choose to create a diagram afterward.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Other Enhancements

- Migration to UAF1.2: the migration experience was enhanced. Now you will be informed about cyclic, directly, and indirectly used projects so that you can solve those usages in the correct order and continue the migration successfully. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How to migrate' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>
- Property Set now groups measurements and a direct measurement name is added to the PropertySet name in the Specification Window.
- The setting of Stream for UAF Parameters or Pins with specified Sync Element has been simplified by adding a Stream command to the shortcut menu.
- The Operational/Resource Role Impact Analysis Map creation from the selected context element - Operational/Resource Role - is now improved.
- The Resource/Service/Operational Exchange item selection window, accessible from the Resources/Services/Operational Connectivity table or Exchange Manager window, has been enhanced. The Conveyed items are now filtered according to the selected Resources/Services/Operational Exchange kind.
- The Problem Domain property creation under Operational Performer and Operational Architecture in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram has been enhanced.
- The Security Process Flow diagram now includes a fix that ensures the creation of Operational and Resource Parameters with the correct stereotype.
- The diagram palette of the Entity Relation Diagram has been simplified by leaving only the Information Model group instead of Conceptual, Logical, and Physical. Also, a Strategic Information button was added.
- The symbol styles for Service Interface and Whole Life Enterprise have been fixed.
- The solver for the COR2012 validation rule was changed from Remove Conveyed Item to Add/Remove Conveyed Item . The updated Add/Remove Conveyed Item solver adds a conveyed item if it is missing or asks to choose which conveyed item should be removed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='COR2012' space='UAF12P2022xR2']>>]]>

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12P2022xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: July 7, 2023</span></h6><p><br /></p><p>The latest release of the 2022x Refresh2 version introduces several notable enhancements. These include the incorporation of UAF Behavior to Structure synchronization, an improved swimlane representative selection dialog, the addition of NAF 4.0 diagrams and tables, and the implementation of background actions logging. Additionally, you can expect improvements to the Gantt Chart and various other enhancements.</p><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2022xR2" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For further information, check the </span><ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[product documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization</h2><p>Keeping your Internal Connectivity diagrams consistent with Activities can be tedious, but UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization can simplify the process. With UAF Behavior to Structure Synchronization, achieving full synchronization between your structure model and behavior model has never been easier. This feature validates Asset Roles in Internal Connectivity diagrams, checks for the presence of Ports that are typed by compatible Interfaces, and identifies any missing Connectors and Exchanges to be created automatically.</p><p>With this technology, coherence between your Activities and Internal Connectivity diagrams will be maintained at any model maturity level. If any inconsistencies arise, the system will detect them and provide you with individual solutions to address each one; thus, you can save valuable time and energy while ensuring the accuracy and consistency of your model.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1119"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF BtoSSync Reprezentation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Swimlane Representative</h2><p style="text-align: left;">The Swimlane representative selection dialog has been enhanced. Now when creating swimlanes,  you will be prompted to select both property types and properties.</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="c197abe2-6c3b-46b4-9eb7-9e09e3cfb8b8">The available types: Operational Agent / Service / Resource Performer / Organizational Resource</li><li data-uuid="85ceab3f-c996-4c1a-8541-cabc45a09519">The available properties: Operational / Service / Resource Roles</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">Properties contained by the Context and/or Performer of IsCapabaleToPerform relation will be pre-selected by default when:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2d6648bd-4e98-444a-82c7-b6e0f233d5c5">Context and/or Is Capable To Perform relations are known,</li><li data-uuid="2d6fac1d-cac1-49b7-9f0e-15cb3bfc487d">Context and/or Performer (which acts as an Activity Context) has structural elements (Operational / Service / Resource Roles).</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_Swimlane_Representatives_dialog_property_and_property_types.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Framework and Diagramming</h2><h3 style="text-align: left;">NAF 4.0 Framework improvements</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The NAF 4.0 Framework improvements were implemented in this version by adding the new Taxonomy diagram and tables. Click each diagram or table hyperlink to learn more:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b2946981-e9a3-4e7a-aebf-56e4e83f7903"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Taxonomy Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="5793bcec-7bac-444e-b7aa-517d3f607b03"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li data-uuid="49729961-844a-44f7-bc67-3610b19302b0"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1 Capability Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li data-uuid="e5925e1b-a136-47ac-b8a1-468c99ae22f6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[S1 Service Taxonomy Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e98c3ab8-4c32-451b-bf83-e947f4e2859e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with L1 Node Types Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 Node Types Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="6d6d805b-4b14-4b14-9c06-8ce26996dd21"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with P1 Resource Types Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Resource Types Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4 style="text-align: left;">Background actions logging </h4><p>Beginning with the 2022x Refresh2 version, the Background Actions Logging Feature will be available. This feature aims to provide more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but cannot be shown in the diagram. The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them from the Notification window to the Containment tree.</p><p style="text-align: left;"> The affected UAF diagrams include the following:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="265447b6-3575-4b77-9683-adeb6c87a667">Strategic Phasing Diagram</li><li data-uuid="5bd25121-edcf-4e66-a78f-6fcf06cd97bc">Projects Roadmap</li><li data-uuid="4ac66320-77b5-4975-ab68-49e03a392403">Services Roadmap</li><li data-uuid="6dcaa261-b14c-4def-917b-916965311224">Personnel Evolution</li><li data-uuid="7ac4724f-3677-476d-83e3-638a0a708a3e">Resources Evolution</li><li data-uuid="efe7fcd8-40df-4f40-81fe-df133404ef03">Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</li><li data-uuid="fe0cc1e6-d40f-446b-9b63-919d0b8e812a">Personnel Forecast</li><li data-uuid="5ca0cbd9-ad12-4cb4-b753-7465faa6f66c">Resources Forecast</li><li data-uuid="39c54ccd-41c0-4a79-badf-2273ee1294a1">Standards Roadmap</li></ul><p>This feature is implemented in UAF and all supported Architecture Framework diagrams.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="230c7bff-d4f8-451b-9001-44f86b9a70ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PvPqofgM5PQ&amp;ab_channel=CATIAMBSE" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Gantt Charts improvements</h3><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="10e6b1e6-b914-46f4-a4d6-f325c4bc245a">The Gantt Chart diagrams were enhanced to display bars with triangles when the Start Date is specified and the End Date is empty:<ul><li>Project Roadmap<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_roadmap.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Actual Strategic Phases Gannt Chart<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_strategic_phases.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="108dd5ef-5711-4f17-a4c6-8d157073ece6">Adding new Actual Projects and Actual Sub Projects in the Project Roadmap, the End Date is no longer filled out by default. You can always specify the End Date later. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Roadmap diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="b74afd54-0a53-474a-bf91-21c041ab9d0d">A new validation rule is added. This rule checks if Actual Projects connected with the Project Sequence relationship have End Dates set according to the specified Project Sequence type/kind<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="COR2346" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bf3e93b2-3d3f-4fc5-b1c0-0f5285a7396e">The filtering in Gantt Chart diagrams was improved to match how the filtering works in the tables. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h2>Usability improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="dce9e54a-6284-4cca-bb6b-5832747dea82">In compliance with the UAF 1.2 specification, the new column Kind is implemented in the Strategic Taxonomy Table.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_column_strategic_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="a4d4e956-8b1f-4048-b623-469eb9c5aa5e">Switching between projects with different Architecture Framework related perspectives has been improved. When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a new message is displayed. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In that message, you can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_projects_diff_perspectives.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="26e7ed3f-8d7d-4913-a3e4-e3029f9b33ce">The Instance Specification related actions have been improved:<ul><li>When you drag an Instance Specification and drop it on another Instance Specification shape, a slot is assigned by default if the structure between Instance Specification types is defined respectively.</li><li>When you drag and drop or move the Instance Specification shape inside another Instance Specification, the first action in the shortcut menu is <strong>Move Here</strong>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0199414e-52a2-4eab-806f-87e58bc74708">The Strategic Processes diagram is now suggested by default when you create instances for a Strategic Phase and choose to create a diagram afterward.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 80.0px;text-align: left;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_process_for_instances.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x Refresh2) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Other Enhancements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="c304f76a-b393-44d7-b213-993b1db052eb"><strong>Migration to UAF1.2:</strong> the migration experience was enhanced. Now you will be informed about cyclic, directly, and indirectly used projects so that you can solve those usages in the correct order and continue the migration successfully. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="How to migrate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about migration>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about migration issues on Teamwork Cloud>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2f339beb-b364-4563-b6bc-0ff958fdefa7">Property Set now groups measurements and<span> a </span><em>direct</em><span> </span>measurement name is added to the PropertySet name in the Specification Window.</li><li data-uuid="6cfd2dfe-e1f0-426e-bfb5-27728646e009">The setting of Stream for UAF Parameters or Pins with specified Sync Element has been simplified by adding a Stream command to the shortcut menu.<span> </span></li><li data-uuid="02bfc645-5866-43a4-b0b9-aec138293ec1">The Operational/Resource Role Impact Analysis Map creation from the selected context element - Operational/Resource Role - is now improved.</li><li data-uuid="4e8828ab-8d9d-4eda-9cba-61c87f161f85">The Resource/Service/Operational Exchange item selection window, accessible from the Resources/Services/Operational Connectivity table or Exchange Manager window, has been enhanced. The Conveyed items are now filtered according to the selected Resources/Services/Operational Exchange kind.</li><li data-uuid="6be55adf-ce32-4b81-929a-d4f36914a4a1">The Problem Domain property creation under Operational Performer and Operational Architecture in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram has been enhanced.</li><li data-uuid="ca4f088f-0955-4275-bc2c-0a46550bdb12"><span>The Security Process Flow diagram now includes a fix that ensures the creation of Operational and Resource Parameters with the correct stereotype.</span></li><li data-uuid="f3a7e1d5-c499-4627-b256-b90e41eee7e3">The diagram palette of the Entity Relation Diagram has been simplified by leaving only the Information Model group instead of <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Conceptual, Logical, and Physical. Also, a Strategic Information button was added.</span></li><li data-uuid="a6a8a223-87ca-4eec-900b-0e3a4b4af2fd">The symbol styles for Service Interface and Whole Life Enterprise have been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="60ed9c65-8a65-471a-a1b5-58f1fad54581">The solver for the COR2012 validation rule was changed from <strong>Remove Conveyed Item</strong> to <strong>Add/Remove Conveyed Item</strong>. The updated <strong>Add/Remove Conveyed Item </strong>solver <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">adds a conveyed item if it is missing or asks to choose which conveyed item should be removed.</span><span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="COR2012" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="ee569ad8-2ef2-4406-baea-555701bab907"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022xR2" ri:content-title="UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.2 Plugin 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624997 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00104: (2022x) 2022x Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `261624997`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624997/2022x+2022x+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:13.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News > (2022x) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Teamwork Cloud / Magic Collaboration Studio

###### Released on: September 2, 2022

2022x Hot Fix 1 is now available for Teamwork Cloud / Magic Collaboration Studio with several bug fixes and performance improvements.

##### Project migration and other performance improvements

Branch listing in UI, merge commit, client-side project migration, and other performance improvements.

##### Resource Usage Map

Inconsistent used profile versions are no longer returned after migration to a new product version.

##### REST API

Role endpoint now returns the *containGlobalOperationsOnly*attribute upon calling */osmc/admin/roles/<roleId>*.

##### Other

- Global Element Usage Search result paging issue was addressed.
- The issue of branch-related dialogs failing to load fully with *BranchNotFoundException*has been fixed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2 style="text-align: left;">Teamwork Cloud / Magic Collaboration Studio</h2><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: September 2, 2022</h6><p><br /></p><p>2022x Hot Fix 1 is now available for Teamwork Cloud / Magic Collaboration Studio with several bug fixes and performance improvements.</p><h3>Project migration and other performance improvements</h3><p>Branch listing in UI, merge commit, client-side project migration, and other performance improvements.</p><h3><span>Resource Usage Map</span></h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Inconsistent used profile versions are no longer returned after migration to a new product version.</span></p><h3>REST API</h3><p>Role endpoint now returns the <em>containGlobalOperationsOnly </em>attribute upon calling <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">/osmc/admin/roles/&lt;roleId&gt;</span></em>.</p><h3><span>Other</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="82f85126-2ebb-4710-a9c3-c817fd931394"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Global Element Usage Search result paging issue was addressed.</span></li><li data-uuid="62a51a22-935d-4fd7-aafe-c3f082e9d038"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The issue of branch-related dialogs failing to load fully with <em>BranchNotFoundException </em>has been fixed.</span></p></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624771 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00106: (2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `261624771`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624771/2022x+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:00.566+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

In version 2022x, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud brings a number of new functionalities to make your work with documents even smoother. We have introduced several document contextualization improvements, such as automatic image numbering and a quicker way to reorder newly created paragraphs. In addition, you will be able to navigate large tables a lot easier due to multiple table usability enhancements, embed diagrams from Cameo Collaborator documents into web pages and documents, and monitor long-running tasks with the help of the task manager.

##### Document Contextualization Enhancements

We are happy to announce that Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x brings several improvements to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='CC4TWC2021xR2'] we introduced in previous versions:

- When you add images to a Cameo Collaborator document, they are automatically numbered and an image caption is created based on the file name to make your document more presentable. In Addition, you can modify the image number and caption at any time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Auto-numbering images added to a Cameo Collaborator document.

- Now you can quickly reorder newly created paragraphs and images in a section by cutting and pasting them to a new location.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reordering paragraphs and images in a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Improved Table Usability

If once in a while you find yourself getting frustrated when trying to read a large table in a Cameo Collaborator document, we have some very good news. Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x comes with significant user experience improvements when it comes to navigating tables:

- Now the heading row stays visible when you scroll down a table.
- You can resize table columns by simply hovering the mouse pointer between two columns until the resizing icon appears.
- The horizontal scroll bar is always at hand regardless of what table section you are viewing.
- The text is aligned to the top left corner of a table cell for better readability.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Usability improvements in tables.

##### Embedding Dynamic Diagrams

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces a new exciting functionality allowing you to embed diagrams from Cameo Collaborator into web pages or documents. When copying a diagram link, you can select to get the URL designed to create an inline frame and easily embed it into any content supporting the iFrame component. Furthermore, such a URL is dynamic and always displays the latest version of an embedded diagram even after a Cameo Collaborator document is updated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Embedding a diagram from Cameo Collaborator into a Confluence page.

##### Task Manager

The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud has a Task Manager which you can use to monitor the status of long-running tasks, such as exporting documents to PDF or HTML. It will definitely come in handy when you need to manage several document exports at once as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='CC4TWC2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"> Released on: July 1, 2022</h6><p><br /></p><p>In version 2022x, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud brings a number of new functionalities to make your work with documents even smoother. We have introduced several document contextualization improvements, such as automatic image numbering and a quicker way to reorder newly created paragraphs. In addition, you will be able to navigate large tables a lot easier due to multiple table usability enhancements, embed diagrams from Cameo Collaborator documents into web pages and documents, and monitor long-running tasks with the help of the task manager.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Document Contextualization Enhancements</h3><p>We are happy to announce that Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x brings several improvements to <ac:link ac:anchor="Enriching Cameo Collaborator Documents"><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2021xR2" ri:content-title="2021x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[document contextualization features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> we introduced in previous versions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="62a5c6a3-d068-46ae-9ceb-882900075a04">When you add images to a Cameo Collaborator document, they are automatically numbered and an image caption is created based on the file name to make your document more presentable. In Addition, you can modify the image number and caption at any time.<br /><br /></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_caption.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Auto-numbering images added to a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="474bc47b-88e7-4faa-b5ca-878235da8033">Now you can quickly reorder newly created paragraphs and images in a section by cutting and pasting them to a new location.<br /><br /></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_pragraph_location.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Reordering paragraphs and images in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><h3>Improved Table Usability</h3><p>If once in a while you find yourself getting frustrated when trying to read a large table in a Cameo Collaborator document, we have some very good news. Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x comes with significant user experience improvements when it comes to navigating tables:</p><ul><li data-uuid="699a3dc5-be67-460c-a04f-441432e7f64d">Now the heading row stays visible when you scroll down a table.</li><li data-uuid="3a9ce434-a92a-4067-9dda-dd2f9324f6b5">You can resize table columns by simply hovering the mouse pointer between two columns until the resizing icon appears.</li><li data-uuid="eaf0c528-0dc0-492b-bdb7-cad401aeba08">The horizontal scroll bar is always at hand regardless of what table section you are viewing.</li><li data-uuid="5f814817-4875-4f83-a9b5-b3362c161827">The text is aligned to the top left corner of a table cell for better readability. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_improvements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Usability improvements in tables.</h6><h3>Embedding Dynamic Diagrams</h3><p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces a new exciting functionality allowing you to embed diagrams from Cameo Collaborator into web pages or documents. When copying a diagram link, you can select to get the URL designed to create an inline frame and easily embed it into any content supporting the iFrame component. Furthermore, such a URL is dynamic and always displays the latest version of an embedded diagram even after a Cameo Collaborator document is updated.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="embedding_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Embedding a diagram from Cameo Collaborator into a Confluence page.</h6><h3>Task Manager</h3><p>The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud has a Task Manager which you can use to monitor the status of long-running tasks, such as exporting documents to PDF or HTML. It will definitely come in handy when you need to manage several document exports at once as shown in the figure below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="task_manager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="b0cd1d48-2066-4579-aec8-9dbb7ae0a88b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CC4TWC2022x" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624814 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00107: (2022x) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `261624814`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624814/2022x+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:02.660+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

Cameo DataHub release brings a number of improvements to both general functionalities and various integrations. In version 2022x, you will be able to change node types for existing maps, edit data source names, benefit from a new Server Certificates Management tool, and more.

The ENOVIA Requirements integration introduces a unified authentication approach and support for custom requirements and attributes.

The DOORS Next Generation integration now supports global configuration, allows reusing DHLinks in multiple data sources, and enables you to create data sources for the selected baselines.

#### General Cameo DataHub Improvements

##### Changing Node Types for Existing Maps

This new capability allows you to refactor the selected element’s type in the Scheme Map manager. Now, when a data mapping scheme is defined and used in the synchronization process, it is possible to update the mapped element’s type to a new type defined on the model or data source side. Once a new element type is selected, the mapping can be migrated to include updated element types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Schema Map Migration.

##### Certificate Management System

With the release of version 2022x, we have consolidated self-signed certificate management for all secure network connections made by a modeling tool. Now a new Server Certificates Management tool handles all secure connections supported by the client. The tool allows users to add or remove self-signed certificates without running complex commands via the command-line interface. You can simply use the Environment Options dialog in your modeling tool to:

- See the list of all currently added self-signed certificates and their details.
- Add new certificates or certificate storage trusted by the current user or by all users.
- Manage all added certificates.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing secure connections.

##### Editable Data Source Names

The Cameo DataHub Explorer panel has been enhanced with a new capability that enables editing the Data Source name in the Cameo DataHub explorer panel. Now, you can rename the connected Data Source for better identification. The edited name is used in place of a regular name across all Cameo DataHub user interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editable data source name.

##### Filtering Conflict Dashboard

The Conflict Dashboard has been improved to display the change-related data in a more informative manner. When the Check for DHLink Change operation is initiated, the Conflict Dashboard displays the exact stereotype names for links. Additionally, you can filter out changes by the source and target data types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Conflict Dashboard.

##### Unified Authentication Approach for Platform-related Applications

Now, all platform-related applications can benefit from the unified and standardized authentication process introduced in version 2022x. This means that the applications listed under the 3DEXPERIENCE menu reuse the same authentication session once logged in.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Logging in to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.

#### Improvements to ENOVIA Requirements Integration (TRM)

##### Traceability Management Application (TRM) Has Been Renamed

The name of the data source for the requirement management application (TRM) on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform has been renamed to “ENOVIA Requirements" (ENOVIA REQS). This way the name of the data source in the Cameo DataHub user interface is the same as the name of the related application on the platform side. In addition, the ENOVIA Requirements data source can now be found in the 3DEXPERIENCE menu of your modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TRM name changed to ENOVIA Requirements

##### Support of Customized Requirements and Attributes

ENOVIA Requirements connector enables you to retrieve requirements and attributes customized by the IP Classification application. Now, custom requirements and attributes are available for mapping and synchronization in Datahub. Requirement Attribute in TRM may be customized to contain multiple values. DataHub can now synchronize them to the model.

#### Improvements to DOORS Next Generation Integration

##### Global Configuration Support

DOORS Next Generation connector now supports global configuration. When the global configuration management is enabled in DOORS Next Generation, you can add all RM domain-related objects identified on the selected global configuration to the Cameo DataHub Explorer panel as separate data sources.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a global configuration data source.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Global configuration data Source.

##### Reusing DHLink Synchronization Links Across Data Sources

DataHub 2022x brings a new capability that enables you to reuse DHTrace synchronization links across streams and baselines of the same DOORS Next Generation component. The **Copy DHTrace from other data source** command allows you to copy links from one baseline or stream to another baseline or stream. The DHTrace links on the original baseline or stream are marked as Excluded. In addition, you can include links back into the synchronization process.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Copying DHLink from one data source to another.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### DHLink copied from another data source.

##### DOORS Next Generation Baseline Support

DOORS Next Generation connector now supports DOORS Next Generation baselines. You can use the **Create Baseline as Data Source** command to display the list of baselines available in DOORS Next Generation for the selected component. Once you select a baseline, the data source for the selected baseline is added to the Cameo DataHub panel, provided that the DOORS Next Generation data source is connected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a data source for the selected baseline.

#### Discontinued Integrations

As of version 2022x, CameoDataHub no longer supports HP Application Lifecycle Management (ALM).

#### Documentation

- Cameo DataHub 2022x

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c56b327-fe27-4089-8113-c912c4c28ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: July 1, 2022</h6><p><br /></p><p>Cameo DataHub release brings a number of improvements to both general functionalities and various integrations. This means that the applications listed under the 3DEXPERIENCE menu reuse the same authentication session once logged in.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="TRMLogin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo DataHub" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Logging in to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.</h6><h2>Improvements to ENOVIA Requirements Integration (TRM)</h2><h3>Traceability Management Application (TRM) Has Been Renamed</h3><p>The name of the data source for the requirement management application (TRM) on the 3DEXPERIENCE platform has been renamed to “ENOVIA Requirements&quot; (ENOVIA REQS). This way the name of the data source in the Cameo DataHub user interface is the same as the name of the related application on the platform side. In addition, the ENOVIA Requirements data source can now be found in the 3DEXPERIENCE menu of your modeling tool.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="TRMDatasource.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Cameo DataHub" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">TRM name changed to ENOVIA Requirements</h6><h3><br />Support of Customized Requirements and Attributes</h3><p>ENOVIA Requirements connector enables you to retrieve requirements and attributes customized by the IP Classification application. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624987 space=VN version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00109: (2022x) Data Markings and Classification Plugin

- page_id: `261624987`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624987/2022x+Data+Markings+and+Classification+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:12.517+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

The 2022x version introduces a new product: **Data Markings and Classification Plugin**. This plugin is designed for system and software engineers, enterprise architects, business analysts, and others who work with classified or sensitive data. It provides compatibility with the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification. Using the **Data Markings and Classification Plugin**, you can specifically mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification or sensitivity. The plugin allows you to:

- Use predefined DoD data markings and/or create custom ones. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_use_dod.png']
- Access data markings via diagram (also symbol, table row, etc.) and element shortcut menus, as well as their specification windows. [ATTACHMENT filename='access_data_markings.png']
- Add data markings to various elements (relationships, blocks, packages, ports, operations, properties, comments, etc.) of your project. [ATTACHMENT filename='diagrams_elements_and_containment_tree.png']

To see it in action, watch the following demo.

>]]>

##### Documentation

-

This plugin is designed for system and software engineers, enterprise architects, business analysts, and others who work with classified or sensitive data. It provides compatibility with the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification. Using the <strong>Data Markings and Classification Plugin</strong>, you can specifically mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or sensitivity</span>. The plugin allows you to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d8f56942-2330-4999-8dd6-95f7906b2454"><p>Use predefined DoD data markings and/or create custom ones.</p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_use_dod.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="384f78f6-bf26-48d6-89de-4ef0d5158926"><p>Access data markings via diagram (also symbol, table row, etc.) and element shortcut menus, as well as their specification windows.</p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="access_data_markings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="eb02aad5-f318-4839-beb0-98642da8d99f"><p>Add data markings to various elements (relationships, blocks, packages, ports, operations, properties, comments, etc.) of your project.</p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagrams_elements_and_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>To see it in action, watch the following demo.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9254dedc-340b-4721-8fc3-d81aa39624b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OBvX40zgbqc&amp;t=1s" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the Data Markings and Classification Plugin >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="f38cd4b0-9fd9-402d-9a27-4a416769718d"><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624963 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00111: (2022x) Magic Alf Analyst / Alf Plugin

- page_id: `261624963`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624963/2022x+Magic+Alf+Analyst+Alf+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:09.563+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

##### Helper Functions

The following *Helper Functions* have been added to the Alf model library to provide certain Action Language Helper capabilities that are not natively available as part of the Alf language.

- InState – Checks if a particular object is in a certain state.
- WasInState – Checks if a particular object has ever visited a certain state.
- GetCurrentTime – Gets the current simulation time.
- GetTimeUnit – Gets the name of the configured simulation time unit.

Check the product compatibility [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Compatibility' space='PC'].

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Alf Analyst Documentation' space='MAF2022x']

library to provide certain Action Language Helper capabilities that are not natively available as part of the Alf language.</p><ul><li data-uuid="b4268106-07c2-443b-8e5f-a7e4741a1cb1">InState – Checks if a particular object is in a certain state.</li><li data-uuid="655fbcd8-4bcd-43eb-8d62-ee09ecd0b221">WasInState – Checks if a particular object has ever visited a certain state.</li><li data-uuid="c7abd832-0c43-4d56-b16c-efeb77eafee9">GetCurrentTime – Gets the current simulation time.</li><li data-uuid="baac43eb-0780-4dd7-a898-1c2a6da1775d">GetTimeUnit – Gets the name of the configured simulation time unit.</li></ul><p>Check the product compatibility <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PC" ri:content-title="Product Compatibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Documentation</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="32db1bea-a13b-4de1-82ef-c45ec2b3cf2b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MAF2022x" ri:content-title="Magic Alf Analyst Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Alf Analyst 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="83fdc02e-c255-4f94-851e-15c00edcd62c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="ALFP2022x" ri:content-title="Alf Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Alf Plugin 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624697 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00112: (2022x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `261624697`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624697/2022x+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:13:58.295+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

The 2022x release includes fresh and exciting features, such as newly introduced data markings and powerful reporting capabilities. Together with other usability changes, these new features allow for easier installation, more powerful access control, and better insights into what’s happening with your data.

In version 2022x, the Collaborator application brings a number of new functionalities as well. We have introduced several document contextualization improvements, such as automatic image numbering and a quicker way to reorder newly created paragraphs. In addition, you will be able to navigate large tables a lot easier due to multiple table usability enhancements, embed diagrams from Collaborator documents into web pages and documents, and monitor long-running tasks with the help of the task manager.

#### Data_markingsData Markings

In addition to the Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) mechanism, you can now apply even stricter access rules using **[Data markings](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022x/Managing+data+markings+in+TWC+Admin)**. This feature allows you to mark resources, categories, users, and user groups with custom-defined hierarchical access levels. You can employ these levels as additional security layers when listing or accessing resources on the server. After enabling data markings in the **Settings** app, you can set the clearance levels of users and user groups in the **Users** app and set the classification levels of resources and categories in the **Resources** app. This allows you to restrict access to certain resources, categories, users, and user groups.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Reporting Capabilities

In order to have better insights into what’s happening with Teamwork Cloud data, we are introducing the new [**Reports** app](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022x/Reports+application). It enables audit-oriented generation of exportable reports on user, resource activity, permission changes, and much more with just a couple of clicks.The new**Reports**app allows you to track, record, and export the information related to Teamwork Cloud usage. This app is available directly through the Teamwork Cloud Web Application Platform or the app bar menu:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reports app with sample report .

#### OSLC DialogsOSLC Selection Dialogs

As an OSLC data Provider, Teamwork Cloud extends its supported [OSLC Service set](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2022x/OSLC+API) by introducing delegated selection dialogs. From now on, any OSLC Consumer tool can open up and browse Teamwork Cloud model content in a web-based manner and link to model elements of interest following standard OSLC protocols.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Teamwork Cloud OSLC Selection Dialog

#### OtherOther

- AuthServer installation : in comparison to earlier versions, the AuthServer installation has changed. Now, the Authentication server is under the WebAppPlatform and thus no longer runs as a separate service. The AuthServer installation and upgrade procedures, as well as related parameters and their locations, are modified. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication server' space=''] page.
- Cassandra 4.0.x : TWCloud 2022x supports the newest major release of the Apache Cassandra database. This allows enjoying all of the benefits of Cassandra‘s performance and stability improvements and adds Java 11 support.
- Containers : we are providing instructions and sample scripts on how to build Cassandra and TeamworkCloud containers from the TeamworkCloudSuite deliverable. These containers can be run on any orchestration platform together with WebApps to ensure easy TWCloud deployment.
- Global Element Usage Indexing and Search is out of Technology Preview! After multiple iterations, tweaking the performance and usability of this feature, we have reached a production-ready maturity level.

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### Document Contextualization Enhancements

We are happy to announce that Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x brings several improvements to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2021x Refresh2 Version News' space='CC4TWC2021xR2'] we introduced in previous versions:

- When you add images to a Cameo Collaborator document, they are automatically numbered and an image caption is created based on the file name to make your document more presentable. In Addition, you can modify the image number and caption at any time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Auto-numbering images added to a Cameo Collaborator document.

- Now you can quickly reorder newly created paragraphs and images in a section by cutting and pasting them to a new location.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reordering paragraphs and images in a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Improved Table Usability

If once in a while you find yourself getting frustrated when trying to read a large table in a Cameo Collaborator document, we have some very good news. Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2022x comes with significant user experience improvements when it comes to navigating tables:

- Now the heading row stays visible when you scroll down a table.
- You can resize table columns by simply hovering the mouse pointer between two columns until the resizing icon appears.
- The horizontal scroll bar is always at hand regardless of what table section you are viewing.
- The text is aligned to the top left corner of a table cell for better readability.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Usability improvements in tables.

##### Embedding Dynamic Diagrams

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces a new exciting functionality allowing you to embed diagrams from Cameo Collaborator into web pages or documents. When copying a diagram link, you can select to get the URL designed to create an inline frame and easily embed it into any content supporting the iFrame component. Furthermore, such a URL is dynamic and always displays the latest version of an embedded diagram even after a Cameo Collaborator document is updated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Embedding a diagram from Cameo Collaborator into a Confluence page.

##### Task Manager

The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud has a Task Manager which you can use to monitor the status of long-running tasks, such as exporting documents to PDF or HTML. It will definitely come in handy when you need to manage several document exports at once as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2022x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624812 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00113: (2022x) Magic Concept Modeler / Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `261624812`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624812/2022x+Magic+Concept+Modeler+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:02.286+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 1, 2022

##### OWL import improvements

- Added support for DisjointUnionOf axioms.
- Added support for multiple property chains for a super property.
- Added support for an OWL object property assertion when its corresponding UML property is owned by an association.
- The prefix of an imported ontology is now saved into the 'namespacePrefix' tagged value of the corresponding Concept Model.
- Added support for reimporting an individual.

##### OWL export improvements

- An unnamed UML «Property Chain» Property that subsets another named property is exported as an OWL object-property chain.

##### Bug fixes

- Importing a necessary and sufficient self restriction no longer creates an unnecessary anonymous class.
- Exporting a link between instance specifications now works when a slot has an association-owned property as its defining feature.
- Exporting a concept model element IRI with leading or trailing white space no longer generates an invalid IRI.
- Indirectly importing a property chain is no longer ignored.
- An imported property without a domain is now owned by «Anything».
- A complete generalization set with super class belonging to another concept model now correctly exports the covering axiom.
- Importing a necessary and sufficient restriction on a class that has no other equivalence relationship no longer creates an unnecessary anonymous class.
- The generated IRI for an instance no longer starts with an upper case letter.
- Importing a DatatypeRestriction no longer generates an error message.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Concept Modeler Documentation' space='MCM2022x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeler Documentation' space='CCMP2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624540 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00114: (2022x) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `261624540`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624540/2022x+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:13:53.703+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
