<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261624967 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00120: (2022x) UAF 1.2 Plugin

- page_id: `261624967`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261624967/2022x+UAF+1.2+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T19:14:09.991+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2022x) 2022x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 1, 2022x

In the 2022x release, we continued implementing the UAF 1.2 standard changes, making the UAF 1.2 Profile fully supported in UAF, UAF Enterprise Architect, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. Therefore UAF 1.1 is no longer supported. Moreover, New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available. Finally, we continued developing the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial but adapted to the UAF 1.2 profile.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='NMDOC'].For further information, check the.

##### Methods and Standards

Beginning with this version, the UAF 1.2 Plugin is available for use in production. The UAF 1.2 Profile is fully supported in UAF, UAF Enterprise Architect, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. The following migration will be **necessary**:

- UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2' space='UAF12P2021xR2']>]]>
- UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2' space='UAF12P2022x']>]]>
- UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2' space='UAF12P2022x']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2' space='UAF12P2022x']>]]>

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**IMPORTANT!** The UAF 1.1 Plugin is no longer available.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Evolution of standards

##### UAF 1.2 support

Together with the UAF 1.2 Profile, the following changes (such as new aspects*, viewpoints**, diagrams, and tables) are implemented:

- Interaction Scenarios Aspect is renamed to Sequences Aspect.
- New Architecture Management Viewpoint:
  - New Architecture Management diagram.
  - Dictionary is a part of Architecture Management Viewpoint.
- New Motivation Aspect:
  - New Strategic Motivation diagram.
  - Requirements view specification is a part of Motivation Aspect for operational, personnel, resources viewpoints.
  - New Security Controls diagram.
- Improvements in Strategic Viewpoint:
  - Support of Value Streams.
  - Introduced Strategic Constraints, new Strategic Constraint table in UAF Framework, and new C8 Planning Assumption table in NAF 4.0 Framework.
  - Introduced Strategic Exchanges.
  - New Strategic Processes diagram.
- Improvements in Services Viewpoint:
  - Introduced Service Contract constraints.
  - Introduced Service Exchanges.
  - Service Association stereotype is introduced and applied to associations between Services.
  - Resource Service introduced to model technical services, such as web services.
- Improvements in Security Viewpoint:
  - Risks as a cross-cutting construct bringing new Risks diagram available in most viewpoints, under Parameters Aspect.
  - New Security Constraints table.
- Implementation Matrix enhanced and adapted to UAF 1.2 Profile.
- Support Personnel Traceability.
  - Functions to Operational Activities and Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Project Mapping matrices are available in Personnel Traceability View Specification.
- Improvements in Information Aspect:
  - Dictionary is a part of Information Aspect.
  - Introduced Strategic Information element and new Strategic Information diagram.

***Aspects** were previously known as model kinds.

****Viewpoints** were previously known as domains.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 grid. 
(Click the image to enlarge)

##### Library Support

New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available:

- UJTL (20220215)
- DISR (20211110)

You can choose the library version when creating a new project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using libraries' space='UAF12P2022x']>]]>

##### ArchiMate Import (Technology preview)

In this version, we continued developing the import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but adapted to the UAF 1.2 profile. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing from ArchiMate' space='UAF12P2022x']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12P2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

no longer available.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cea-evo-19-22x - plugin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Evolution of standards</span></h6><h3>UAF 1.2 support</h3><p>Together with the UAF 1.2 Profile, the following changes (such as new aspects*, viewpoints**, diagrams, and tables) are implemented:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9dd80260-18a4-4a33-afde-c4f1a180f6d8">Interaction Scenarios Aspect is renamed to Sequences Aspect.</li><li data-uuid="a70122f4-6ca3-4026-a205-4cbd2bf1ea2d">New Architecture Management Viewpoint:<ul><li>New Architecture Management diagram.</li><li>Dictionary is a part of Architecture Management Viewpoint.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a0368878-6974-4304-911c-434b5c294991">New Motivation Aspect:<ul><li>New Strategic Motivation diagram.</li><li>Requirements view specification is a part of Motivation Aspect for operational, personnel, resources viewpoints.</li><li>New Security Controls diagram.</li></ul></li><li Framework, and new C8 Planning Assumption table in NAF 4.0 Framework.</li><li>Introduced Strategic Exchanges.</li><li>New Strategic Processes diagram.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="769a461c-a798-4aaa-8454-d17944bf6be8">Improvements in Services Viewpoint:<ul><li>Introduced Service Contract constraints.</li><li>Introduced Service Exchanges.</li><li>Service Association stereotype is introduced and applied to associations between Services.</li><li>Resource Service introduced to model technical services, such as web services. </li></ul></li><li data-uuid="caa9375e-e74d-42c9-9fc4-f727e3d1013f">Improvements in Security Viewpoint:<br /><ul><li>Risks as a cross-cutting construct bringing new Risks diagram available in most viewpoints, under Parameters Aspect.</li><li>New Security Constraints table.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="cce52ad8-c004-4270-83aa-c647e49a6db9">Implementation Matrix enhanced and adapted to UAF 1.2 Profile.</li><li data-uuid="923c5d13-f6b4-48f4-9858-1cd53906bcec">Support Personnel Traceability.<ul><li>Functions to Operational Activities and Actual Responsible  Resources to Actual Project Mapping matrices are available in Personnel Traceability View Specification.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="671ded6f-1550-47b6-819d-020ca7785997">Improvements in Information Aspect:<ul><li>Dictionary is a part of Information Aspect.</li><li>Introduced Strategic Information element and new Strategic Information diagram.</li></ul></li></ul><hr /><p><strong>*Aspects</strong> were previously known as model kinds.</p><p><strong>**Viewpoints</strong> were previously known as domains.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">UAF 1.2 grid.</span><br style="text-align: center;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">(Click the image to enlarge)</span></h6><h3>Library Support</h3><p>New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available:</p><ul><li data-uuid="dd6511e0-728c-4a2a-a314-0d5288fa9d34">UJTL (20220215)</li><li data-uuid="5da8b35f-33b3-47bc-b276-8ec10c216415">DISR (20211110)</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can choose the library version when creating a new project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about using libraries >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>ArchiMate Import (Technology preview)</h3><p>In this version, we continued developing the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">import of the ArchiMate model from the open exchange file into the UAF project. The imported element mapping is still only partial, but adapted to the UAF 1.2 profile. The mapping of ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 approach is based on the NATO Architecture Framework. </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022x" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1040"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 horizontal-white-background.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2022x) UAF 1.2 Plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="ee569ad8-2ef2-4406-baea-555701bab907"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2022x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.2 Plugin 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744742 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00122: (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `272744742`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744742/2024x+Refresh1+2024x+Refresh1+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:44:42.050+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News > (2024x Refresh1) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: December 6, 2024

2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh1) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where an element is pasted to the current owner if the containment path on a diagram is pasted too has been resolved. Now, you can copy-paste an element to a different owner, allowing you to update the containment path.
- The issue where magnifying a relation map using a Mac trackpad throws an exception has been resolved.
- The modeling tool no longer gets unresponsive when a modeling tool-specific window is placed on a second monitor and the monitor is disconnected.
- The issue where a validation failure is not reported if the error message has an incorrect script has been resolved.
- The screen recording dialog is no longer shown upon launching the modeling tool on macOS.
- The issue of the DSLS Mac native library not being signed has been resolved.
- The modeling tool no longer freezes upon clicking the ‘Add New’ button in a Generic Table.
- The issue where updated table rows are not highlighted after syncing Excel files has been resolved.
- Excel file texts are no longer imported with incorrect fonts.
- Excel files exported from Generic tables now have consistent sheet names.
- The expanded tables are no longer displayed as collapsed in Diagram Overview shapes after reloading the project.
- Copy-pasted symbols are no longer affected by the symbol style changes made to the original symbol after copying.
- The issue of pasting HTML text in the Advanced HTML Editor not displaying the pasted text in the TextBox or Note has been resolved.
- Additional content is no longer added after Excel import when the ‘Sync Plain Text’ option is disabled.

###### Collaboration

- 
  - The problem where in certain cases duplicated references are created from the «Customization» stereotype to stereotyped elements has been resolved.
  - The issue where in certain cases an exception occurs when initiating the project usages table has been fixed.
  - The problem where an exception occurs after trying to change the version of a used project has been fixed.

###### Collaboration Powered by **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

- 
  - The issue where the name of a duplicated project is changed to the original project name has been fixed.
  - The Requirements Table no longer get corrupted after committing a project.
  - The problem where the project commit fails after saving a project offline or restoring an auto-saved project has been fixed.
  - The issue where the project commit fails after importing a used project that has other usages has been resolved.
  - Several issues causing the package export to a new server project to fail has been resolved.
  - The problem where you cannot commit a project after importing a used project with DSL elements has been fixed.
  - Elements no longer lose their constraints after exporting the package with the constraints to a new server project.
  - The issue where in certain cases the name of a used project is changed after the project commit has been fixed.
  - The problem where an exception occurs when trying to migrate profiles has been fixed.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML

- The port symbol property’s ‘Position of Labels’ value ‘Inside Enclosing Shape’ now works as expected for nested ports.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue where the default value of a constraint parameter is not passed to another constraint parameter has been fixed.
- The problem where in certain cases signals with redefined properties are not sent during simulation has been resolved.
- The problem where in certain scenarios it is impossible to add a property value in the variables tree has been fixed.
- Server-side simulation no longer needs an additional license to work.
- The issue where you cannot add a breakpoint to a port from the Containment tree has been resolved.

##### Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.2

- The order of added Capability Provision in the Strategic Phasing chart's details panel has been improved to bring more clarity.
- The issue where Strategic Phasing Gantt Capability Provision bar is not updated after changing the time periods in the Capability Provision area has been fixed.

##### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Fixed a cross-cluster resource synchronization failure that would occur when the authentication token changed.
- Performance issues caused by creating model elements via the Teamwork Cloud REST API, which resulted in large multi-value property lists, have been resolved.

##### Cameo DataHub

- The migration issues that occurred while accessing synchronized data from previous versions have been fixed to ensure data consistency.

##### Systems Cybersecurity Designer

- An issue that prevented the simultaneous deselection of multiple Threat Types in the Select Threat Type dialog has been fixed.
- An issue where the dropdown list for core parameters in Attack Potential-Based Attack Path failed to appear when reopening the project has been fixed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[vulnerability fixes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in our modeling tools and server products.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. ## PAGE 00124: (2024x Refresh1) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes

- page_id: `272744744`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744744/2024x+Refresh1+Addressed+vulnerabilities+and+component+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:44:42.589+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News > (2024x Refresh1) Hot Fixes > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open Source components have been updated, as listed below, to address known software vulnerabilities. Legal Notices will be updated to reflect these, and other changes, at the next scheduled regular release.

##### Teamwork Cloud/Magic Collaboration Studio

| Library | Old version | New version | CVEs addressed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Eclipse EMF common | 2.30.0 | 2.31.0 |  |
| Eclipse EMF ecore | 2.36.0 | 2.37.0 |  |
| Eclipse EMF xmi | 2.37.0 | 2.38.0 |  |
| Jetty | 9.4.54.v20240208 | 9.4.56.v20240826 |  |
| Netty | 4.1.108.Final | 4.1.114.Final | CVE-2024-29025 |
| ElasticSearch | 7.17.20 | 7.17.24 | CVE-2024-23450 |

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| jfreechart | 1.5.3 | 1.5.5 |  |
| Jetty | 9.4.54.v20240208 | 9.4.56.v20240826 | CVE-2024-6763, CVE-2024-8184 |

##### WebApps

| commons-io | 2.16.0 | 2.17.0 | - |
| java-support | 8.4.0 | 8.4.2 | CVE-2024-22262CVE-2024-22259CVE-2024-22243CVE-2023-6378 |
| Micrometer | 1.12.5 | 1.12.9 | - |
| OpenSAML | 4.3.0 | 4.3.2 | CVE-2024-22262CVE-2024-22259CVE-2024-22243CVE-2023-44483 |
| Spring Expression Language (SpEL) | 6.1.6 | 6.1.13 | - |
| Spring Transaction | 6.1.6 | 6.1.13 | - |
| Spring Security | 6.2.4 | 6.3.3 | CVE-2024-38809 |
| RoaringBitmap | 1.0.5 | 1.0.6 | - |
| XMLBeans | 5.2.0 | 5.2.1 | - |
| XML APIs | - | 1.4.01 | - |
| Byte Buddy | 1.14.12 | 1.14.19 |  |
| bcpkix-jdk18on, bcprov-jdk18on, bcutil-jdk18on | 1.77 | 1.78.1 | CVE-2024-29857CVE-2024-30171CVE-2024-30172CVE-2024-34447 |
| Jakarta Activation API | 2.1.2 | 2.1.3 |  |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744668 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: (2024x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `272744668`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744668/2024x+Refresh1+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:43:37.205+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 5, 2024

Version 2024x Refresh1 ofCameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces major enhancements in commenting, PDF export, change tracking, and model hardening. Most notable improvements include the ability to create custom comment templates and a significant increase in PDF generation performance, as well as model hardening capabilities, including opening a document without publishing the project and linking Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements. For added convenience, we also introduced new document editing features, such as persisting commenting/editing mode, the ability to add a video, and a one-click saving option in Rich Text editor. But that‘s not all; we made other huge usability improvements, such as advanced comment customization options, support for diagram and table comparison, and the ability to open historical document versions.

#### Commenting

Cameo Collaborator now lets you customize comments by creating different comment types, such as issue, question, or comment, and adding as many additional input fields as necessary for each type. These extensions are essential to satisfy the specific needs of your project.

##### Ability to Customize Comments

This version includes a new ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates' space='MCS2024xR1'] through a Profile Diagram. Previously limited to modifying only the title, body, and urgency of comments, you now have the option to tailor additional input fields and properties to suit your specific needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing a comment template with a Profile Diagram.

##### Comment Table

We enhanced the comment review process in models by automatically generating a hierarchical comment table under the package that represents the document. This table is created when the very first comment is added and lists all comments, along with their priority and resolution status.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing Cameo Collaborator comments table in the modeling tool.

#### Improved PDF Export

##### Significant Increase in Performance

The performance of the PDF export functionality has greatly improved, resulting in much faster export times, as well as the ability to handle considerably larger documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Password Protection for PDFs

For added security of sensitive project information, you can now protect PDFs with a password when generating a file for export.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Protecting a generated Cameo Collaborator PDF file with a password.

##### Text Search in Diagrams

To help you manage project data more efficiently and locate specific information more quickly, we introduce the ability to search for text within diagrams in exported PDF documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for text within a diagram of an exported Cameo Collaborator document PDF file.

##### Exporting Section Titles as Bookmarks

Cameo Collaborator document section titles are now exported to PDFs as bookmarks, allowing you to navigate through the document with ease.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Section titles displayed as PDF document bookmarks.

#### Change Tracking

In response to customer feedback and critical requirements, we introduce major improvements to change tracking capabilities. You can now easily track changes in documents by opening historical versions and view what edits were made by which user at a specific time. Additionally, you can compare diagrams and tables across different versions,allowing you to understand how the model evolved, who was involved in the process, and how it led to important requirements and architectural decisions.

###### Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening historical version document' space='MCS2024xR1'] of Cameo Collaborator documents and open them in a read-only mode. This allows you to monitor content changes and make informed decisions, helping to review and improve project workflows.

Additionally, you can share a link to a specific document version. This is useful when a model has changes significant to system engineers, who can share the relevant version with others for review.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing or linking a historical version of a document.

##### Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions

We implemented the ability to easily compare diagrams and tables across different versions of a document. This allows you to effortlessly track the evolution of your content and gain valuable insights into how your elements have evolved over time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparing diagrams in different Cameo Collaborator document versions using Fade mode.

#### Model Hardening

In the initial phases of systems engineering, informal ideas often arise, necessitating lightweight capturing methods. Recognizing this need, Cameo Collaborator nowenables non-modelers to easily suggest project changes in a wiki form, which engineers can then use to build models. They can input requirements or other data which modelers can then convert into SysML/UAF models.

##### Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing

To help non-modelers share ideas more quickly and efficiently, we introduce the ability to open a Cameo Collaborator document without publishing the associated project first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a document of an unpublished project with changes reflected in the modeling tool.

##### Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements

We now allow linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to model element properties of numerical or textual type. This allows you to view up-to-date information and edit a Cameo Collaborator document without the need to manipulate the actual model in a modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements.

#### User Experience Improvements

##### Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode

When you enter commenting or editing mode, it will no longer close automatically after creating or editing a comment. Instead, the mode will persist until you manually close it or choose to discard/commit your changes to the server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting mode in Cameo Collaborator remains active when managing comments.

##### One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form

Now you can save your edits with just one click while editing values in Rich Text Editing forms, eliminating the need to minimize the form. Introducing the Save button in the toolbar simplifies the workflow, enhancing user experience by reducing the steps required to save changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Clicking Save to save the comment and exit Rich Text editing mode.

##### Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document

You can now enrich your Cameo Collaborator documents with dynamic multimedia content by adding a video instead of a still image. This enhances communication and collaboration between teams and creates a more engaging and comprehensive experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Video in a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Legend Support in Non-editable Documents

Addressing customer feedback, we introduce the ability to display table legends in non-editable Cameo Collaborator documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing a corresponding table with its legend in the modeling tool and Cameo Collaborator.

#### otherOther Fixes/Improvements

- It is no longer necessary to specify a Cameo Collaborator URL in modeling tool Environment options, as the URL is retrieved automatically.
- Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.
- You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.
- We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.
- Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.
- SLF4J was removed and Log4j is now used for logging .

#### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='TWCloud2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the URL is retrieved automatically.</li><li data-uuid="f9a28609-2769-420f-b5e0-c63ce8640dd3">Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.</li><li data-uuid="4892b043-9708-4ec5-9433-4bd166953f2c">You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.</li><li data-uuid="02946331-9da4-4b22-b36c-0819a6d91903">We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.</li><li data-uuid="82c0a6aa-5335-4e19-b507-f8c8df603a30">Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.</li><li data-uuid="86b67add-dc31-4627-962c-89a09af3808c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">SLF4J was removed and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Log4j is now used for logging</span></span>.</li></ul><h2><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Documentation</span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="b0cd1d48-2066-4579-aec8-9dbb7ae0a88b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744746 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00126: (2024x Refresh1) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272744746`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744746/2024x+Refresh1+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:44:43.479+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: July 5, 2024

In version 2024x Refresh1, Cameo DataHub presents a number of new features and enhancements. This release introduces various enhancements, security and technical improvements, as well as bug and minor fixes.

### Jama Connect®

With 2024x Refresh1 version, we introduce a new connector to support integration. The new driver JAMA Connect has been added to the list of available drivers in the Add Data Source dialog. To learn more about the new connector, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Sources for JAMA Connect' space='CDH2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The new JAMA Connect connector is available in the Add Data Source dialog.

### Environment Options

The **Save HTML images from the data source in the project as attached files**option in the Environment Options dialog is updated now. The option is now set to *true*by default. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Save HTML images from the data source in the project as the attached files option set to *true*in the Environment dialog.

### ENOVIA Requirements

ENOVIA Requirements driver now supports the two-way synchronization for the images found on mapped attribute values.

### Other Improvements

- OSLC selection dialog of the OSLC drivers V2 and V3 is enhanced with an additional view mode parameter. This enhancement presents the OSLC API provider‘s response in compact view on the OSLC delegated UIs.
- The VBS mode argument needed to launch IBM Doors is set to be used by default.
- Cameo DataHub now supports the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform version 2024x FD03 for both cloud and on-premise.
- Cameo DataHub migrated to the 3DS platform Relations API V2 to resolve the issue related to moving synchronized items from one specification to another.
- The listing of specifications on Spec selecting UI is migrated from DB queries to Indexed API to improve performance.
- Cameo DataHub now supports the 7.37.2 version of the jxBrowser.

### Security improvements

- Third-party software libraries used in the Cameo DataHub plugin codebase are updated to their most recent available versions.

### Bug Fixes

- The Maximize/Restore button presentation is fixed to display the button‘s label correctly.
- The Minimize/Restore button functionality is fixed to restore the affected panel to its original size correctly.
- The Regression introduced in the previous version of the Cameo DataHub, impacting the customization of IBM Doors data types to support more flexible mapping scenarios, is now fixed. To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing new node type for DOORS' space='CDH2024xR1'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The new driver JAMA Connect has been added to the list of available drivers in the Add Data Source dialog. This enhancement presents the OSLC API provider‘s response in compact view on the OSLC delegated UIs.</span></li><li><span>The VBS mode argument needed to launch IBM Doors is set to be used by default.</span></li><li><span>Cameo DataHub now supports the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform version 2024x FD03 for both cloud and on-premise.</span></li><li><span>Cameo DataHub migrated to the 3DS platform Relations API V2 to resolve the issue related to moving synchronized items from one specification to another. </span></li><li><span>The listing of specifications on Spec selecting UI is migrated from DB queries to Indexed API to improve performance. </span></li><li><span>Cameo DataHub now supports the 7.37.2 version of the jxBrowser.</span></li></ul><h1>Security improvements</h1><ul><li><span>Third-party software libraries used in the Cameo DataHub plugin codebase are updated to their most recent available versions.</span></li></ul><h1>Bug Fixes</h1><ul><li><span>The Maximize/Restore button presentation is fixed to display the button‘s label correctly.</span></li><li><span>The Minimize/Restore button functionality is fixed to restore the affected panel to its original size correctly.</span></li><li><span>The Regression introduced in the previous version of the Cameo DataHub, impacting the customization of IBM Doors data types to support more flexible mapping scenarios, is now fixed. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744559 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00131: (2024x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272744559`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744559/2024x+Refresh1+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:42:35.304+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: July 5, 2024

Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh1 further improves your user experience with added branch managing capabilities and enhanced branch visualization. The ability to set the DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin, added support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, a new available report type, and other improvements greatly contribute to increased ease of use and functionality.

Version 2024x Refresh1 ofCameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces major enhancements in commenting, PDF export, change tracking, and model hardening. Most notable improvements include the ability to create custom comment templates and a significant increase in PDF generation performance, as well as model hardening capabilities, including opening a document without publishing the project and linking Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements. For added convenience, we also introduced new document editing features, such as persisting commenting/editing mode, the ability to add a video, and a one-click saving option in Rich Text editor. But that‘s not all; we made other huge usability improvements, such as advanced comment customization options, support for diagram and table comparison, and the ability to open historical document versions.

#### NOTE: 2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 Version News' space='CATIA']>]]>

****

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
- 
- 
- 

****

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 
  -

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
  -

  - 
- 

### Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin

#### Enhanced Branch Management

###### Managing Branches in Resources Application

To streamline branch management and offer you greater control and convenience, we introduce the Branches pane in the resource details view. This significant new feature of the Resources application allows you to efficiently [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing resource branches' space='MCS2024xR1'], including archiving, renaming, and deleting them, directly from the web user interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing resource branches in Resources application.

###### Improved Visual Branch Representation

2024x Refresh1 includes some subtle yet impactful enhancements to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing history' space='MCS2024xR1']. Archived branches are now clearly distinguishable with a greyed-out tag for easier navigation and organization. Additionally, you can now adjust column sizes effortlessly using a convenient column size slider, providing greater flexibility and customization within the interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual branch representation improvements.

#### Setting DSLS License in Teamwork Cloud Admin

We’re happy to announce two exciting updates relating to license management in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

First, you can now utilize DSLS licenses seamlessly within the Settings application, simplifying the process of setting and managing licenses. Additionally, we‘re introducing support for triad licenses, enabling you to configure up to three DSLS server addresses for a robust server failover mechanism.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

#### Added Support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect

In this release, we expanded our authentication capabilities to include OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect clients alongside OAuth 1.0a, facilitating secure, consent-driven access while safeguarding user credentials. Users can now authorize client apps to access resources without compromising their credentials, ensuring enhanced privacy and security. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='MCS2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting OAuth 2.0 client in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

#### Application Deployment through Kubernetes

We are introducing a new unified deployment strategy for Magic Collaboration Studio, Web Application Platform, and Cassandra through Kubernetes. With Kubernetes deployment, users can seamlessly manage and scale their applications with greater flexibility and consistency.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Other Fixes/Improvements

- Resource Synchronization now supports a secured connection (TLS).
- Task manager is now accessible from any application and you can download Task manager reports.
- A new type of report is available: the Locked resources report, displaying resources that have locked elements.
- Now you only need the Resource Manager role to manage branch level permissions instead of the Manage User Permissions permission, which was included solely in the Security Manager role.
- From now on Cassandra-related properties from Magic Collaboration Studio configuration files apply to Authserver as well, so it is no longer necessary to configure Cassandra-related properties in the authserver.properties file.

### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

#### Commenting

Cameo Collaborator now lets you customize comments by creating different comment types, such as issue, question, or comment, and adding as many additional input fields as necessary for each type. These extensions are essential to satisfy the specific needs of your project.

###### Ability to Customize Comments

This version includes a new ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates' space='MCS2024xR1'] through a Profile Diagram. Previously limited to modifying only the title, body, and urgency of comments, you now have the option to tailor additional input fields and properties to suit your specific needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing a comment template with a Profile Diagram.

###### Comment Table

We enhanced the comment review process in models by automatically generating a hierarchical comment table under the package that represents the document. This table is created when the very first comment is added and lists all comments, along with their priority and resolution status.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing Cameo Collaborator comments table in the modeling tool.

#### Improved PDF Export

###### Significant Increase in Performance

The performance of the PDF export functionality has greatly improved, resulting in much faster export times, as well as the ability to handle considerably larger documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Password Protection for PDFs

For added security of sensitive project information, you can now protect PDFs with a password when generating a file for export.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Protecting a generated Cameo Collaborator PDF file with a password.

###### Text Search in Diagrams

To help you manage project data more efficiently and locate specific information more quickly, we introduce the ability to search for text within diagrams in exported PDF documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for text within a diagram of an exported Cameo Collaborator document PDF file.

###### Exporting Section Titles as Bookmarks

Cameo Collaborator document section titles are now exported to PDFs as bookmarks, allowing you to navigate through the document with ease.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Section titles displayed as PDF document bookmarks.

#### Change Tracking

In response to customer feedback and critical requirements, we introduce major improvements to change tracking capabilities. You can now easily track changes in documents by opening historical versions and view what edits were made by which user at a specific time. Additionally, you can compare diagrams and tables across different versions,allowing you to understand how the model evolved, who was involved in the process, and how it led to important requirements and architectural decisions.

###### Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening historical version document' space='MCS2024xR1'] of Cameo Collaborator documents and open them in a read-only mode. This allows you to monitor content changes and make informed decisions, helping to review and improve project workflows.

Additionally, you can share a link to a specific document version. This is useful when a model has changes significant to system engineers, who can share the relevant version with others for review.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing or linking a historical version of a document.

###### Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions

We implemented the ability to easily compare diagrams and tables across different versions of a document. This allows you to effortlessly track the evolution of your content and gain valuable insights into how your elements have evolved over time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparing diagrams in different Cameo Collaborator document versions using Fade mode.

#### Model Hardening

In the initial phases of systems engineering, informal ideas often arise, necessitating lightweight capturing methods. Recognizing this need, Cameo Collaborator nowenables non-modelers to easily suggest project changes in a wiki form, which engineers can then use to build models. They can input requirements or other data which modelers can then convert into SysML/UAF models.

###### Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing

To help non-modelers share ideas more quickly and efficiently, we introduce the ability to open a Cameo Collaborator document without publishing the associated project first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a document of an unpublished project with changes reflected in the modeling tool.

###### Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements

We now allow linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to model element properties of numerical or textual type. This allows you to view up-to-date information and edit a Cameo Collaborator document without the need to manipulate the actual model in a modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements.

#### User Experience Improvements

###### Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode

When you enter commenting or editing mode, it will no longer close automatically after creating or editing a comment. Instead, the mode will persist until you manually close it or choose to discard/commit your changes to the server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting mode in Cameo Collaborator remains active when managing comments.

###### One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form

Now you can save your edits with just one click while editing values in Rich Text Editing forms, eliminating the need to minimize the form. Introducing the Save button in the toolbar simplifies the workflow, enhancing user experience by reducing the steps required to save changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Clicking Save to save the comment and exit Rich Text editing mode.

###### Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document

You can now enrich your Cameo Collaborator documents with dynamic multimedia content by adding a video instead of a still image. This enhances communication and collaboration between teams and creates a more engaging and comprehensive experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Video in a Cameo Collaborator document.

###### Legend Support in Non-editable Documents

Addressing customer feedback, we introduce the ability to display table legends in non-editable Cameo Collaborator documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing a corresponding table with its legend in the modeling tool and Cameo Collaborator.

#### otherOther Fixes/Improvements

- It is no longer necessary to specify a Cameo Collaborator URL in modeling tool Environment options, as the URL is retrieved automatically.
- Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.
- You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.
- We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.
- Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.
- SLF4J was removed and Log4j is now used for logging .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8bb10b8-912b-4eec-96f6-7e84fb5ee2d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h1 style="text-align: center;">Magic Collaboration Studio</h1><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Released on: July 5, 2024</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh1 further improves your user experience with added branch managing capabilities and enhanced branch visualization. The ability to set the DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin, added support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, a new available report type, and other improvements greatly contribute to increased ease of use and functionality.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(52,53,65);">Version 2024x <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Refresh1 of </span>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces major enhancements in commenting, PDF export, change tracking, and model hardening. Most notable improvements include the ability to create custom comment templates and a significant increase in PDF generation performance, as well as model hardening capabilities, including opening a document without publishing the project and linking Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements. For added convenience, we also introduced new document editing features, such as persisting commenting/editing mode, the ability to add a video, and a one-click saving option in Rich Text editor. But that‘s not all; we made other huge usability improvements, such as advanced comment customization options, support for diagram and table comparison, and the ability to open historical document versions.  </span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="569e25e5-7042-4e37-bc33-e76dbcdf057e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products</span> </span> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CATIA" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><hr /><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Enhanced Branch Management" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing Branches in Resources Application" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Improved Visual Branch Representation"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Improved visual branch representation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Setting DSLS License in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Added Support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Application Deployment through Kubernetes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Application Deployment Through Kubernetes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Fixes/Improvements" /></li></ul><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Commenting" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Ability to Customize Comments" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Comment Table" /></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Improved PDF Export" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Significant Increase in Performance" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Password Protection for PDFs" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Text Search in Diagrams" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting Section Titles as Bookmarks" /></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Change Tracking" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions" /></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Model Hardening" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements" /></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="User Experience Improvements" /><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Legend Support in Non-editable Documents" /></li></ul></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="other"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Other Fixes/Improvements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><hr /><p><br /></p><h1>Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin</h1><h2>Enhanced Branch Management</h2><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Managing Branches in Resources Application</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To streamline branch management and offer you greater control and convenience, we introduce the Branches pane in the resource details view. This significant new feature of the Resources application allows you to efficiently <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing resource branches" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage resource branches]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, including archiving, renaming, and deleting them, directly from the web user interface.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="managing_resource_branches.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Managing resource branches in Resources application.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>Improved Visual Branch Representation</span></h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">2024x Refresh1 includes some subtle yet impactful enhancements to the <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating full-screen history view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing history" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[visual branch representation window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Archived branches are now clearly distinguishable with a greyed-out tag for easier navigation and organization. Additionally, you can now adjust column sizes effortlessly using a convenient column size slider, providing greater flexibility and customization within the interface.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_branch_representation_features.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> Visual branch representation improvements.</h6><h2>Setting DSLS License in Teamwork Cloud Admin</h2><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">We’re happy to announce two exciting updates relating to license management in Teamwork Cloud Admin.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">First, you can now utilize DSLS licenses seamlessly within the Settings application, simplifying the process of setting and managing licenses. Additionally, we‘re introducing support for triad licenses, enabling you to configure up to three DSLS server addresses for a robust server failover mechanism.</span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="flexnet_and_dsls_license_tabs.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(122,134,154);">Setting DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin.</span></h6><h2>Added Support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect</h2><p>In this release, we expanded our authentication capabilities to include OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect clients alongside OAuth 1.0a, facilitating secure, consent-driven access while safeguarding user credentials. Users can now authorize client apps to access resources without compromising their credentials, ensuring enhanced privacy and security. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing OAuth client keys" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="oauth2.0.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting OAuth 2.0 client in Teamwork Cloud Admin.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Application Deployment through Kubernetes</h2><p>We are introducing a new unified deployment strategy for Magic Collaboration Studio, Web Application Platform, and Cassandra through Kubernetes. With Kubernetes deployment, users can seamlessly manage and scale their applications with greater flexibility and consistency.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="kubernetes_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Other Fixes/Improvements</h2><ul><li>Resource Synchronization now supports a secured connection (TLS).</li><li><span>Task manager is now accessible from any application and you can download Task manager reports.</span></li><li>A new type of report is available: the Locked resources report, displaying resources that have locked elements.</li><li>Now you only need the Resource Manager role to manage branch level permissions instead of the Manage User Permissions permission, which was included solely in the Security Manager role.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">From now on Cassandra-related properties from Magic Collaboration Studio configuration files apply to Authserver as well, </span>so it is no longer necessary to configure Cassandra-related properties in the <em>authserver.properties </em>file.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h1>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h1><h2>Commenting</h2><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Cameo Collaborator now lets you customize comments by creating different comment types, such as issue, question, or comment, and adding as many additional input fields as necessary for each type. These extensions are essential to satisfy the specific needs of your project.</span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Ability to Customize Comments</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">This version includes a new ability to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customize comment attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> through a Profile Diagram. Previously limited to modifying only the title, body, and urgency of comments, you now have the option to tailor additional input fields and properties to suit your specific needs.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_comment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Customizing a comment template with a Profile Diagram.</h6><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>Comment Table</span></h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">We enhanced the comment review process in models by automatically generating a hierarchical comment table under the package that represents the document. This table is created when the very first comment is added and lists all comments, along with their priority and resolution status.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_table.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Viewing Cameo Collaborator comments table in the modeling tool.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2>Improved PDF Export</h2><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Significant Increase in Performance</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">The performance of the PDF export functionality has greatly improved, resulting in much faster export times, as well as the ability to handle considerably larger documents. </p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="675" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="marketing.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Password Protection for PDFs</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">For added security of sensitive project information, you can now protect PDFs with a password when generating a file for export.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="pdf_password_protection.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Protecting a generated Cameo Collaborator PDF file with a password. </h6><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Text Search in Diagrams</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">To help you manage project data more efficiently and locate specific information more quickly, we introduce the ability to search for text within diagrams in exported PDF documents.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="searching_diagram_text.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Searching for text within a diagram of an exported Cameo Collaborator document PDF file.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Exporting Section Titles as Bookmarks</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Cameo Collaborator document section titles are now exported to PDFs as bookmarks, allowing you to navigate through the document with ease.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="section_name_bookmark.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Section titles displayed as PDF document bookmarks.</h6><h2>Change Tracking</h2><p>In response to customer feedback and critical requirements, we introduce major improvements to change tracking capabilities. You can now easily track changes in documents by opening historical versions and view what edits were made by which user at a specific time. <span>Additionally, you can compare diagrams and tables <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">across different versions, </span><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">allowing you to understand how the model evolved, who was involved in the process, and how it led to important requirements and architectural decisions.</span></span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening historical version document" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[access historical versions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of Cameo Collaborator documents and open them in a read-only mode. This allows you to monitor content changes and make informed decisions, helping to review and improve project workflows.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Additionally, you can share a link to a specific document version. This is useful when a model has changes significant to system engineers, who can share the relevant version with others for review. </p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewing_historical_document_version.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Viewing or linking a historical version of a document.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">We implemented the ability to easily compare diagrams and tables across different versions of a document. This allows you to effortlessly track the evolution of your content and gain valuable insights into how your elements have evolved over time. </span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="926" ac:width="1080"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_fade.gif" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparing diagrams in different Cameo Collaborator document versions using Fade mode.</h6><h2>Model Hardening</h2><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">In the initial phases of systems engineering, informal ideas often arise, necessitating lightweight capturing methods. Recognizing this need, Cameo Collaborator now </span><span>enables non-modelers to easily suggest project changes in a wiki form, which engineers can then use to build models. They can input requirements or other data which modelers can then convert into SysML/UAF models.</span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">To help non-modelers share ideas more quickly and efficiently, we introduce the ability to open a Cameo Collaborator document without publishing the associated project first.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="opening_document_without_publishing.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Opening a document of an unpublished project with changes reflected in the modeling tool.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">We now allow linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to model element properties of numerical or textual type. This allows you to view up-to-date information and edit a Cameo Collaborator document without the need to manipulate the actual model in a modeling tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="linking_elements_to_paragraphs.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements.</h6><h2>User Experience Improvements</h2><h4>Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">When you enter commenting or editing mode, it will no longer close automatically after creating or editing a comment. Instead, the mode will persist until you manually close it or choose to discard/commit your changes to the server.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="persisting_commenting_mode.gif" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Commenting mode in Cameo Collaborator remains active when managing comments.</h6><h4>One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form</h4><p><span>Now you can save your edits with just one click while editing values in Rich Text Editing forms, eliminating the need to minimize the form. Introducing the Save button in the toolbar simplifies the workflow, enhancing user experience by reducing the steps required to save changes.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_rich_text_editing.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Clicking Save to save the comment and exit Rich Text editing mode.</span></h6><h4>Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document</h4><p><span>You can now enrich your Cameo Collaborator documents with dynamic multimedia content by adding a video instead of a still image. This enhances communication and collaboration between teams and creates a more engaging and comprehensive experience.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="video.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Video in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4>Legend Support in Non-editable Documents</h4><p><span>Addressing customer feedback, we introduce the ability to display table legends in non-editable Cameo Collaborator documents.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_with_legend.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Viewing a corresponding table with its legend in the modeling tool and Cameo Collaborator.</h6><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5b14424-bfd8-4e27-b396-618461111e40"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Fixes/Improvements</h2><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li>It is no longer necessary to specify a Cameo Collaborator URL in modeling tool Environment options, as the URL is retrieved automatically.</li><li>Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.</li><li>You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.</li><li>We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.</li><li>Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">SLF4J was removed and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Log4j is now used for logging</span></span>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

Check the product compatibility [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Compatibility' space='PC'].

<h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: July 5, 2024</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The version was released for compatibility purposes only. It does not contain new capabilities, only minor bug fixes.</span></p><p>Check the product compatibility <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PC" ri:content-title="Product Compatibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="31f9ea6c-4b3e-413f-a0ea-68508f88e997"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM2024xR1" ri:content-title="Magic Concept Modeler Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Concept Modeler 2024x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9c90e515-9555-4143-b92e-22ec01d96a24"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCMP2024xR1" ri:content-title="Concept Modeler Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Concept Modeler Plugin 2024x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744339 space=VN version=5 -->
## PAGE 00133: (2024x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `272744339`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744339/2024x+Refresh1+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-05-19T13:22:38.749+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer

Released on: July 5, 2024

SysML features introduce several improvements to Item Flow management. You can now navigate from a selected Item Flow in the Containment tree to the diagrams where it is realized, specify Conveyed Items from the Diagram Palette, and hide arrow notations on realizing elements when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified. Conveyed Items are now represented with icons in the 'Create / Edit Item Flow' dialog. Item Flow creation between Activity Parameter Nodes and Actions has been enhanced. Two new validation rules highlight Item Flows without Conveyed Items. The 'Preview Affected Diagrams' dialog further enhances the Item Flow management experience by presenting all affected diagrams.

Furthermore, the 2024x Refresh1 release contains new validation solvers for the 'Invalid Connector' validation rule, allowing for an easy connector redefinition. The new release also introduces the MagicGrid perspective.

Simulation features introduce several significant enhancements. One of the key updates is the ability toanimate behavioral diagrams during server-side simulationby embedding theminto HTML-generated user interfaces. The latest product version also supports model data export to the SSP file format,allowing you transfer models between different simulation tools, such as Dymola. Furthermore, you can now load and play external simulation results from CSV files using the Results Player.Additionally, a new project option allows you to display runtime values on Port labels during simulation, providing immediate insights into model behavior. The new release also supports Matlab integration for Apple Silicon,allows enabling and disabling simulation annotations in diagrams, offers improvements to the ALH.callOperation method and built-in math functions, and more.

Client-side performance enhancements include optimized efficiency for exporting matrices and tables in SVG format. For general modeling, this release improves Information Flow management. You can now easily navigate from a selected Information Flow in the Containment tree to the diagrams where it is realized and specify Conveyed Information directly in the diagram. You can choose to hide arrow notations on realizing elements when the Conveyed Information is hidden or unspecified. Conveyed Information is now represented with icons in the 'New Conveyed Information' dialog. New validation rules highlight Information Flows that do not have any Conveyed Information.

Additional enhancements include the improved samples access and navigation in the modeling tool. Also, a new 'Letter Spacing Reduction in SVG' environment option is introduced to control the spacing between letters when exporting diagram images in SVG format.

Additionally, collaborative modeling brings several enhancements tothe Model Patch functionality, which is now out of the technology preview phase. The Model Patch UI has been improved with a new panel that identifies and explains issues related to model patch application. Project commit processes are now more efficient with a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) queue system for simultaneous commits. Finally, you can save time by skipping the migration of archived branches when migrating Teamwork Cloud projects and more.

Collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform introduces significant improvements as well. You can now move Teamwork Cloud projects to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform and then update these projects using the .szip file format. In addition, the History and Edit Branches dialogs now indicate if and from which iteration a project was merged.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024xR1']. For further information, check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2024xR1'].

#### NOTE: 2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 available

2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 Version News' space='CATIA']>]]>
