objectives. Those represent 'how' an adversary achieves a given step of an attack, by performing a specific action. <span>When one of those techniques is used as a step of an Attack Path, the recommended requirements for that technique will be automatically proposed by the <strong>Recommend Control</strong> command on any Threat Scenario associated to that Attack Path. To learn more, refer to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SCD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Libraries" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enterprise Technique.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>MITRE ATT&amp;CK Enterprise Technique Library.</span></h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ICS Technique.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">MITRE ATT&amp;CK ICS Technique Library.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="NIST Control.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">NIST Control Library.</span></h6><h2><span>Customizable Threat Types</span></h2><p style="text-align: left;"><span>In addition to the STRIDE classification for Threat Types, UNECE classification is now directly available in the tool. </span><span>Moreover, these </span><span>Threat Types</span><span> are now customizable by users. A custom hierarchy of threat types can be created, and will be automatically used in the selection dialog of threat type, in addition to STRIDE and UNECE. To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SCD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Threat  scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Threat scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nesting Effect.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Select Threat Type dialog showing customized threat types in the custom folder along with STRIDE and UNECE classification.</span></h6><h2>Assets creation</h2><p>New commands have been added to simplify Assets creation, and to ease the selection of Asset’s Underlying Elements. To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SCD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Asset" /></ac:link>.</p><p>From a SysML Block Definition Diagram or from a SysML Internal Block Diagram, you can now directly:</p><ul><li data-uuid="dd922a67-df9c-4589-8707-3f133081f0bb">Create new Assets.</li><li data-uuid="9755791c-04b9-454c-a4d9-70640bb5e7e0">Add an Underlying Element to an existing Asset.</li></ul><p>From an Item Table, you can now select a set of Members and:</p><ul><li data-uuid="cf0ee68b-d651-4dde-896f-12ace3f390b1">Create a new Asset with those Members as Underlying Element.</li><li data-uuid="f41c1f51-b667-4e93-8662-20a3d22ab565">Add those Members as Underlying Elements of an existing Asset.</li></ul><h2>Reports</h2><p>It is now possible to export your ISO 21434 project using Report Wizard capability, with a provided TARA Report Template. To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SCD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Generating  Cybersecurity Reports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Cybersecurity Reports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span> </span></p><h2>Automatic generation of Cybersecurity Goals</h2><p>In the TARA table, you may want to auto-generate the cybersecurity goals associated with a given Threat Scenario, without needing to create them one by one. <span>We provide a dedicated command, </span><strong>Generate /Synchronize Cybersecurity goals, </strong><span>in the TARA table for the same purpose. When the command runs on a Threat Scenario, it will create a set of Cybersecurity goals, and associate those goals with the Threat Scenario. The names of the auto-generated Cybersecurity Goals will be: “</span><span>[Asset Name] of the [Item Name] shall be protected against [Threat type]</span><span>”.</span></p><h2>Other improvements</h2><h3>Final Item with Cybersecurity Concept</h3><p>ISO21424 project now provides an additional table, which gathers all Items, with associated Assets, Goals and Requirements.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Functional Cybersecurity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Functional Cybersecurity Concept table with the item and its associated assets, goals and requirements.</h6><h3>ISO 21434 &amp; 26262 convergence</h3><p>Safety goals defined in a HARA analysis can be reused in a TARA analysis, and a validation rule validates that the ASIL Value &amp; Safety Risk<em> </em>Value are aligned.</p><h3>Enhanced Recommend control command</h3><p>The<strong> Recommend control</strong> command now recursively searches for recommended requirements associated to Attack Path Steps. <span>For example, if an </span>Attack Path<span> has a step, which is another </span>Attack Path<span>, then the steps of the second </span>Attack Path<span> will be considered by this </span>Recommend control<span> command.</span></p><h2><span>Documentation</span></h2><ul><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SCD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Systems Cybersecurity Designer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Systems Cybersecurity Designer 2024x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744939 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: (2024x Refresh1) UAF 1.2 Plugin

- page_id: `272744939`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744939/2024x+Refresh1+UAF+1.2+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T15:45:01.974+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh1) 2024x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### UAF 1.2 Plugin

Released on: July 5, 2024

Beginning with version 2024x Refresh1, significant enhancements have been made to various diagramming tools and tables. The **Security Connectivity** and **Security Role-based Connectivity** tables have been restructured into dedicated diagrams for better clarity on Operational and Resource viewpoints. The **Strategic Driver Map** now visually connects Drivers, Enterprise Goals, and Strategic Phases, while the new **Strategic Drivers Table** organizes elements, such as Drivers, Challenges, Opportunities, and Capabilities, in a structured format. The **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog has been introduced for easier management and preview of affected diagrams, with new options for updating diagram elements. Enhancements to **Gantt Charts** include better project and milestone management and display options for projects. Measurement management has been improved with features for reusing default values and auto-populating measurements in Property Sets. Additionally, model conversion between frameworks and new validation rules have been implemented to streamline processes and ensure data integrity.

To download the latest version, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR1/Downloading+installation+files).For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR1).

- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
  - 

- 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

### Diagraming

##### Security Connectivity and Role-Based Connectivity Tables

Beginning with version 2024x Refresh1, the Security Connectivity and Security Role-based Connectivity tables have been redesigned into dedicated diagrams focusing on the Operational and Resource viewpoints:

- Security Connectivity (Resource) Table
- Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table
- Security Connectivity (Operational) Table
- Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Strategic Driver Map

This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Strategic Driver Table

The new Strategic Drivers Table allows you to assemble Drivers, Challenges, Opportunities, and Capabilities in a tabular form in strict order: the Driver presenting Challenges, the Challenges motivating Opportunities, and the Opportunities impacting Capabilities.

In this table, you can also define relationships between Drivers and Challenges, Challenges and Opportunities, Opportunities and Capabilities. This table captures the Driver, Challenge, Opportunity, and Capability elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

600400

##### Affected Diagrams Preview Dialog

To enhance the exchange creation and management experience in Resource, Service, and Operational viewpoints, we have introduced the **Preview Affected Diagrams** dialog. This dialog conveniently presents all affected diagrams, allowing you to preview them individually. Furthermore, the affected elements, where the created Exchanges will be realized, are prominently marked in the diagram preview pane.

During the preview process, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for diagrams in which you wish to display the Conveyed Items of the selected exchange.

The new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR1']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams**controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified exchanges. Set the option to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of exchanges whenever a new exchange is created or an existing one is modified.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Report templates

- The new report templates have been implemented for Service Connectivity Table and Service Role-based Connectivity Table. The reports are available through the Report Wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A new Strategic Driver Table also makes a new report available. You can create the report through the Report Wizard, directly from the table toolbar, or using a quick report option from the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Gantt Chart Enhancements

##### Project Roadmap Enhancements

- The new Actual Project Assignment wizard will help you add new Actual Projects to the chart more conveniently. With this wizard, you will:
  - Have the ability to reuse existing Projects as created Actual Project types [ATTACHMENT filename='actual_project_assignmnet_wizard.png']
  - Control Actual Projects hierarchy creation based on the existing Project structure.
- Two additional steps were added to the Actual Project Milestone Creation wizard. With these steps, you can specify the Actual Project Milestone Version Released and Version Withdrawn properties during milestone creation. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- The new**Display Actual Projects as Grouping Elements**option (located in the Gantt chart options area) is introduced. This option controls how the Actual Project is displayed: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Turn the option on to display the Actual Project only as a grouping element.
  - Turn the option off to display the grouping Actual Project bars with Actual Project Milestones and completion progress.

##### Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Enhancements

- You can now choose whether you want to show or hide selected Capability's Provisions in the Capability Provisions area in the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart. [ATTACHMENT filename='show_hide_capability_provisions_area_strategic_phasing.png']
- Now, when creating a Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, you can choose to add Capabilities and Resources from the used projects. These elements will be displayed in gray to indicate that they come from a used project.

##### Other

- The Gantt charts were enhanced to indicate when an element is used from another project and/or is read-only, with the element name displayed in gray to signify this status.

### Exchange Management

- The functionality when reconnecting connectors between elements was improved, offering options for managing exchanges. Now, the modeling tool checks the flow properties of source and target ports (if defined), recommending suitable exchanges for realization. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>
- You can now specify a Conveyed Item directly in the diagram, picking the needed Conveyed Item element from the diagram palette and dragging it on to the exchange. [ATTACHMENT filename='set_conveyed_item_palette_personnel.png']
- Now the Conveyed Items in the Realized Exchange wizards are represented with icons to help you identify the Conveyed Items.
- With this version, when you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements as well. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing**[Project option](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR1/Setting+project+options)controls the arrow visibility.
- You can now easily navigate from the selected exchange in Containment tree to the diagrams where that exchange is realized. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Measurement Management Enhancements

With this version, we have enhanced measurements management for Property Sets and Measurable Elements.

- Before the 2024xR1 release, you had the ability to define actual values for all measurements within Measurement Sets or Property Sets. Now, with the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you can effortlessly reuse default actual values to define the entire structure of Actual Property Sets. This enhancement includes the implementation of the Default value button, conveniently accessible within the Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Actual Measurements' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Prior to the 2024xR1 release, automatic population of measurements was limited to Measurable Elements only, with no capability for Property Sets. With the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you now have the ability to automatically populate measurements on Property Sets as well. Measurements are created for elements with the **Applies for** tag values (stereotyped with «DoDAFProperties») applied, and the newly created measurements are typed by the elements with the «DoDAFProperties» stereotype applied. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Before the 2024x R1 release, reusing and synchronization of Measurement structures was not feasible in UAF. With the release of the 2024x R1, you can now leverage this functionality for Property Set usages, including Assets and Roles, as well as Measurement Sets and Measurements. This enhancement enables the creation of an Actual Property Set structure suggested based on the structure of the Property Set typing the Measurable Element, with default values also suggested.

Existing Actual Property Sets of the Measurable Element type are recommended, and this allows you to review and adjust them as needed. Additionally, the newly created Actual Property Set is applied as a default value of the Measurable Element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Model Conversion Between Frameworks Improvements

The project conversion between different Enterprise Architecture Frameworks has undergone some improvements and now adds more clarity. The framework packages are seamlessly mapped during conversion, with package names adjusted accordingly. Custom-named packages remain unchanged, while multiple packages with identical names are merged into one.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### Validation

- The COR2185 validation rule was improved to synchronize Measurement Sets of the validated element, located in the Measurement Set property, with Measurement Sets of the associated element: extending beyond Measurable Elements to include Property sets that own the measurements typed by Measurement Sets.
- New completeness validation rules (COM2028, COM2029, COM2030) have been implemented for Operational, Resource, and Service viewpoints to highlight exchanges that are not realized by any realizing element.
- The new correctness validation rule COR2184was implemented to check whether the Actual Project Start and End dates enclose the dates of its owned Actual Projects.
- The COR2196 correctness validation rule was implemented to check whether the date of the Actual Project Milestone does not precede the date of its referenced Actual Project Milestones.

### Discontinued Features

- The PES export functionality is no longer available.

### Other Enhancements

- One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Operational/Resource/Resource Service/Service/ Interface, which has the same features as its original Interface except that its Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with conjugated Interfaces' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]>
- You can now create a Definition and Comment for any UAF element in their**Specification**window>**Documentation/Comment**property group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- The new Project option **Show Diagram Abbreviations**has beenintroduced. Set this option to true to show diagram abbreviation in the Containment tree. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- With the new Project Option **Show default documentation for UAF Packages**, you canchoose to show the default UAF Packagedocumentation. Turning the option on will display the documentationin:
  - The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property
  - The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the Model Browser. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Show default documentation for UAF packages' space='UAF12P2024xR1']>>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='default_package_documentation.png']
- The Strategic Motivation Table has been improved. Now when you click the **Add New** or **Add Nested** button, the SysML Requirements are collapsed and you can focus only on UAF requirements - Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective.
- The creation process of UAF Instance Specifications was enhanced by enabling the creation of slots when one instance specification is dragged and dropped onto another from the diagram, diagram palette, or Containment tree when the structure of Instance Specification types is predefined.
- The role-based connectivity diagrams have been enhanced by adding a Context column to show the context of the element realizing the exchange .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can create the report through the Report Wizard, directly from the table toolbar, or using a quick report option from the Containment tree.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_driver_table_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h1><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="425295e2-fe50-4c03-bcaa-d4ce5e8c0393">Gantt Chart Enhancements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h1><h3 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Project Roadmap Enhancements</h3><ul><li><span>The new Actual Project Assignment wizard will help you add new Actual Projects to the chart more conveniently. </span><span>With this wizard, you will:</span><ul><li>Have the ability to reuse existing Projects as created Actual Project types<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_assignmnet_wizard.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Control Actual Projects hierarchy creation based on the existing Project structure. </li></ul></li><li><p style="text-align: left;">Two additional steps were added to the Actual Project Milestone Creation wizard. With these steps, you can specify the Actual Project Milestone Version Released and Version Withdrawn properties during milestone creation.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The new<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Display Actual Projects as Grouping Elements</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>option (located in the Gantt chart options area) is introduced. This option controls how the Actual Project is displayed:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Turn the option on to display the Actual Project only as a grouping element.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Turn the option off to display the grouping Actual Project bars with Actual Project Milestones and completion progress.</span></li></ul><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about customizing Gantt chart >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_as_grouping_element.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ul><h3 style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Enhancements</span></h3><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can now choose whether you want to show or hide selected Capability's Provisions in the Capability Provisions area in the </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f2e1a337-26d0-4f6e-8147-a18526735125">Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart.</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_hide_capability_provisions_area_strategic_phasing.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Now, when creating a Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, you can choose to add Capabilities and Resources from the used projects. These elements will be displayed in gray to indicate that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e9a86650-1dd8-4f55-abad-e0c69ca2d6d9">they</ac:inline-comment-marker> come from a used project. </li></ul><h3 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Other</h3><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Gantt charts were enhanced to indicate when an element is used from another project and/or is read-only, with the element name displayed in gray to signify this status. </span></li></ul><p><br /></p><h1>Exchange Management</h1><ul><li><p>The functionality when reconnecting connectors between elements was improved, offering options for managing exchanges. Now, the modeling tool checks the flow properties of source and target ports (if defined), recommending suitable exchanges for realization. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </p></li><li>You can now specify a Conveyed Item directly in the diagram, picking the needed Conveyed Item element from the diagram palette and dragging it on to the exchange. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette_personnel.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Now the Conveyed Items in the Realized Exchange wizards are represented with icons to help you identify the Conveyed Items. </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">With this version, when you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose to hide the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>arrow notation on the realizing elements as well. The<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong><span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR1/Setting+project+options">Project option</a><span> </span>controls the arrow visibility. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now easily navigate from the selected exchange in Containment tree <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">to the diagrams where that exchange is realized. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Navigation to realizing elements in symbolic diagrams.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><h1><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d55023bf-ae6c-41b1-b535-e1c30ab9630e">Measurement Management Enhancements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h1><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">With this version, we have enhanced measurements management for Property Sets and Measurable Elements.</span></p><ul><li style="text-align: left;">Before the 2024xR1 release, you had the ability to define actual values for all measurements within Measurement Sets or Property Sets. Now, with the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you can effortlessly reuse default actual values to define the entire structure of Actual Property Sets. This enhancement includes the implementation of the <strong>Default value</strong> button, conveniently accessible within the Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Prior to the 2024xR1 release, automatic population of measurements was limited to Measurable Elements only, with no capability for Property Sets. With the introduction of the 2024xR1 release, you now have the ability to automatically populate measurements on Property Sets as well. Measurements are created for elements with the <strong>Applies for</strong> tag values (stereotyped with «DoDAFProperties») applied, and the newly created measurements are typed by the elements with the «DoDAFProperties» stereotype applied. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_applyFor_tag.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><ul><li>Before the 2024x R1 release, reusing and synchronization of Measurement structures was not feasible in UAF. With the release of the 2024x R1, you can now leverage this functionality for Property Set usages, including Assets and Roles, as well as Measurement Sets and Measurements. This enhancement enables the creation of an Actual Property Set structure suggested based on the structure of the Property Set typing the Measurable Element, with default values also suggested.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Existing Actual Property Sets of the Measurable Element type are recommended, and this allows you to review and adjust them as needed. Additionally, the newly created Actual Property Set is applied as a default value of the Measurable Element.</span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reusing measurements structure owned by roles type.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h1>Model Conversion Between Frameworks Improvements</h1><p>The project conversion between different Enterprise Architecture Frameworks has undergone some improvements and now adds more clarity. The framework packages are seamlessly mapped during conversion, with package names adjusted accordingly. Custom-named packages remain unchanged, while multiple packages with identical names are merged into one.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_frameworks_wn.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h1>Validation</h1><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The COR2185 validation rule was improved to synchronize Measurement Sets of the validated element, located in the Measurement Set property, with Measurement Sets of the associated element: extending beyond Measurable Elements to include Property sets that own the measurements typed by Measurement Sets. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New completeness validation rules (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">COM2028, COM2029, COM2030) have been implemented for Operational, Resource, and Service viewpoints to highlight exchanges that are not realized by any realizing element. </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The new correctness validation rule <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">COR2184 </span>was implemented to check whether the Actual Project Start and End dates enclose the dates of its owned <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Actual Projects. </span></span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The COR2196 correctness validation rule was implemented to check whether the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a7cd7f86-4c77-45ad-beb5-754b7039fccd">date</ac:inline-comment-marker> of the Actual Project Milestone does not precede the date of its referenced Actual Project Milestones. </span></li></ul><p><br /></p><h1>Discontinued Features</h1><ul><li><span>The PES export functionality is no longer available. </span></li></ul><p><br /></p><h1>Other Enhancements</h1><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">One more way to model conjugated ports is to create the Conjugated Operational/Resource/Resource Service/Service/ Interface, which has the same features as its original Interface except that its Flow Properties are reversed (conjugated). </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with conjugated Interfaces" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now create a Definition and Comment for any UAF element in their </span><strong>Specification<span> </span></strong>window<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>&gt; </span><strong>Documentation/Comment</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition_creation.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The new Project option <strong style="text-align: left;">Show Diagram Abbreviations </strong>has been<span> introduced. Set this option to true to show diagram abbreviation in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5cf373e3-512c-43a2-88d4-7cf632a318bd">Containment tree</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_abbreviations_sample.png" /></ac:image></span></span></li><li><p><span>With the new Project Option <strong style="text-align: left;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</strong>, y</span>ou <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">can</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">choose to show the default UAF Package</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">documentation. Turning the option on will display the documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="07ad3d02-a54c-4104-b8eb-db8fb7ebb4ac">in:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li>The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property</li><li>The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the<span> </span>Model Browser.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_package_documentation.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The Strategic Motivation Table has been improved. Now when you click the <strong>Add New</strong> or <strong>Add Nested</strong> button, the SysML Requirements are collapsed and you can focus only on UAF requirements - Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective. </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The creation process of UAF Instance Specifications was enhanced by enabling the creation of slots when one instance specification is dragged and dropped onto another from the diagram, diagram palette, or Containment tree <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="00bf4a89-3c01-4f93-b81c-058fe8451b8c">when</ac:inline-comment-marker> the structure of Instance Specification types is predefined.</span></span></li><li>The role-based connectivity diagrams have been enhanced by adding a <span>Context column to show the context of the element realizing the exchange</span>.  </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272745696 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00142: (2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `272745696`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272745696/2024x+Refresh2+2024x+Refresh2+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:03:44.336+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Version News > (2024x Refresh2) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: May 2, 2025

2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh2) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where an association was not shown when dropping a property on a diagram, despite the associated class already being displayed, has been resolved.
- The issue where the Project Merge Core 2024x Refresh2 plugin was not loading in projects has been resolved.
- The issue where the installed modeling tool’s icon was not displayed in the Windows Programs and Features panel has been resolved.
- The issue where the Classifier Behavior property was set to a copy-pasted element that did not have that property has been fixed.
- The issue where an IllegalArgumentException was thrown has been fixed.
- The issue where diagram tag values were displayed on a diagram by default has been fixed.
- The issue where exporting a diagram as SVG resulted in corrupted icons has been fixed.

###### Collaboration

- The problem where element changes were not highlighted during model merge or comparison has been resolved.
- The issue causing delays when opening large projects has been fixed.
- Several standard profile migration issues have been fixed.
- The issue preventing you from changing a used project version has been fixed.

###### Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

- The issue where an exception occurs when adding certain standard profiles to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform has been resolved.
- Several standard profile migration issues have been fixed .
- The problem preventing standard profiles from appearing in the Use Server Project dialog has been fixed.
- The error occurring when creating new server projects from certain templates after standard profile migration has been resolved
- The issue where you are unable to create a Business Motivation Diagram in a BPMN2 project has been fixed.
- The problem preventing opening a project after standard profile migration has been fixed.
- The error that occurs after changing a used project version has been resolved.
- Exporting packages to new server projects now works as expected.
- The issue where committing a project could result in an error under certain conditions has been fixed.

###### Report Wizard

- The issue where the local template did not open when the attached template was extracted has been fixed.
- The issue where the attached template did not open has been fixed.
- The issue where the attached template did not open has been fixed.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML

- The issue where a model inconsistency was detected after refactoring a part property and deleting an association that owns it in a server project has been resolved.
- The issue where a type was not assigned to a part property while the Type Selection mode was enabled has been resolved.
- The issue where the requirement ID changed after setting the verify method has been fixed.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- Issue where the users could not instantiate SIMULIA templates on the Cloud platform because user had access to several platforms has been fixed.
- Issue when an error message was incorrectly displayed that no licenses are available while using SIMULIA Integration has been fixed.
- Issue where the State flow (Simulink States) Export did not work correctly, when user region was set using non-Latin characters has been fixed.
- Issue where dropping the FMU block from the Containment Tree created wrong URL has been fixed.
- Issue where the MATLAB integrate failed when its installation directory had a custom name has been fixed.
- Issue where the State Machine did not exit the Nested state using a Trigger when Parent state has a do activity has been fixed.
- Issue where the wrong hyperlink was published in the swagger documentation has been fixed.

##### MagicCollaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Optimized retrieval of logged-in users' data in the Users application.
- Improved synchronization to disallow projects with the same local ID from being synchronized to the same server.
- An issue breaking client and server compatibility has been resolved.
- Fixed an issue occurring with .szip exports with a large amount of used projects.
- Fixed memory leak in Document Exporter.

##### Cameo DataHub

- Issue related to synchronization of requirement items with a multi-valued string type attribute set to no value has been fixed.
- Issue related to verification of the ENOVIA Requirements data source URL, that enables connection to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform configured to use 3dspace instead of 3DSpace has been fixed.
- Fixed a failing use case where already synchronized items were moved from one location to another and then synchronized again. This fix applies only to platforms older than R23XFD03.
- Multiples issues related to displaying customized attributes in the DataHub Explorer Panel, mapping them, and synchronizing them have been fixed.
- Enabled synchronization of JAMA Connect items with properties marked as mandatory and read-only for the current user.
- Enabled synchronization of single- and multi-value selection list properties.
- Added support for hieroglyph characters (Japanese, Chinese, etc.).
- Other small bug-fixes and visual improvements.

##### Systems Cybersecurity Designer

- Issue where the internal error occurred while generating Attach path in Attack graph in between Attack path step & Failure mode/Malfunctioning Behavior has been fixed.
- Issue where the library versions displayed in TARA report were incorrect has been fixed.
- Issue where the user was not able to drop the Safety/Cybersecurity goal in value field of Cybersecurity Goal of a Threat scenario has been fixed.
- Issue where the user could assign invalid enumeration literals to any property has been fixed.

**Using SCD plugin in Teamwork Cloud environment:**

- Issue where the notification message was displayed for all control elements, when only one element was removed has been fixed.
- Issue where the project related notifications were not generated in Project tab of Notification window has been fixed.
- Issue where the duplicate messages were displayed in notification window has been fixed.
- Issue where the Risk Treatment Decision property (dependency relation locked by another user) failed to refresh automatically when modified has been fixed.
- Issue where the notification messages were inconsistent when the CAL value of Cybersecurity Goal was modified has been fixed.

##### Cameo Safety and Reliability Analyzer

- Issue where an internal error occurred when setting ASIL value in an ISO 26262 project table has been fixed.
- Issue where a custom derived property named ASIL did not display any values, while ISO 26262 plugin was installed has been fixed.

##### CATIA Software Producer

- The issues regarding the drag and drop functionlity have been resolved.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2 style="text-align: left;">CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools</h2><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: May 2, 2025</h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2024x Refresh2) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[vulnerability fixes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in our modeling tools and server products.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw</h3><ul><li data-uuid="99c96305-a1a0-4528-9183-68623b0e4867"><span>The issue where an association was not shown when dropping a property on a diagram, despite the associated class already being displayed, has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="7d9e85df-ff42-4666-8cb9-e632984b6227"><span>The issue where the Project Merge Core 2024x Refresh2 plugin was not loading in projects has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="a0a4b3e7-eee5-4cee-ae08-24692010aebf"><span>The issue where the installed modeling tool’s icon was not displayed in the Windows Programs and Features panel has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="a267d141-55b1-4171-a9a1-10089e155bc8"><span>The issue where the Classifier Behavior property was set to a copy-pasted element that did not have that property has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="2cb79597-d4a6-442f-8a76-7f0e55caacb6"><span>The issue where an </span><span>IllegalArgumentException</span><span> was thrown has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="62489bdc-7dcf-412c-a22e-233e78907b39"><span>The issue where diagram tag values were displayed on a diagram by default has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="25adfd0c-b8fb-4e1e-bc22-480d5e9399b7">The issue where exporting a diagram as SVG resulted in corrupted icons has been fixed. </li></ul><h4>Collaboration</h4><ul><li data-uuid="743acb59-e57a-427d-9a8f-c9f8c1173835">The problem where element changes were not highlighted during model merge or comparison has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="c9be24d0-8a70-4852-80e9-19c5a2a151fd">The issue causing delays when opening large projects has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="1370a7aa-56c9-4f94-b5ec-17a1e12c44cb"><span>Several standard profile migration issues have been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="4c02f9ae-96a1-4f0d-a474-05ea7eb1e08f">The issue preventing you from changing a used project version has been fixed.</li></ul><h4>Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</h4><ul><li data-uuid="dd48208a-f98a-49c7-8cf6-da5f0c7f4bf2">The issue where an exception occurs when adding certain standard profiles to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="79d281dd-38f9-4e26-a2bf-f0405a65ef81"><span>Several standard profile migration issues have been fixed</span>.</li><li data-uuid="8e7398ae-8c8a-4d66-8894-49c9fdae14c6">The problem preventing standard profiles from appearing in the <strong>Use Server Project</strong> dialog has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="ffcee89e-cc07-4e84-bee9-188dc9d603a1">The error occurring when creating new server projects from certain templates after standard profile migration has been resolved</li><li data-uuid="3a82e287-2cae-48e7-a6d9-3e5c0fb0c0e7">The issue where you are unable to create a Business Motivation Diagram in a BPMN2 project has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="09ca315e-8df2-4bf2-9f42-81f80e5463e9">The problem preventing opening a project after standard profile migration has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="1a2afca4-7b41-40fe-a6af-176663e761d8">The error that occurs after changing a used project version has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="1c3653e2-cad1-4d5c-b834-4bc4170d557e">Exporting packages to new server projects now works as expected.</li><li data-uuid="9fd4d43f-2fc1-4e40-9cba-e958db73865e">The issue where committing a project could result in an error under certain conditions has been fixed.</li></ul><h4>Report Wizard</h4><ul><li data-uuid="eb3b1003-c866-4c46-a038-afb1143d5e77">The issue where the local template did not open when the attached template was extracted has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="de7b3d57-8c2a-401b-bc4f-9d5fed4798b0">The issue where the attached template did not open has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="0aa7bd4e-56d0-468f-b65c-9b726ded8166">The issue where the attached template did not open has been fixed.</li></ul><h3>Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML</h3><ul><li data-uuid="0023b2c0-dd64-44e2-b0f6-56a51528a832"><span>The issue where a model inconsistency was detected after refactoring a part property and deleting an association that owns it in a server project has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="e5e27a6f-860f-42b2-9f29-280694897e4a"><span>The issue where a type was not assigned to a part property while the Type Selection mode was enabled has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="b17ce440-f869-4661-bfe9-50e3855c7b16">The issue where the requirement ID changed after setting the verify method has been fixed. </li></ul><h3>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit</h3><ul><li data-uuid="caf60614-ab34-473c-93ee-cf8e89b1f540">Issue where the users could not instantiate SIMULIA templates on the Cloud platform because user had access to several platforms has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="d1848746-a9ac-49c2-911f-e90b75c7aa2a">Issue when an error message was incorrectly displayed that no licenses are available while using SIMULIA Integration has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="257e39f3-987b-49ac-b351-47e7aaff08b6">Issue where the State flow (Simulink States) Export did not work correctly, when user region was set using non-Latin characters has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="1d4df7c6-8877-4203-b9cd-ed24ad9b3f24">Issue where dropping the FMU block from the Containment Tree created wrong URL has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="2a63cf26-6ae8-43a3-8da1-e0d51502339c">Issue where the MATLAB integrate  failed when its installation directory had a custom name has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="fb877fb3-cdc9-47c9-ab0d-2b960792a196">Issue where the State Machine did not exit the Nested state using a Trigger when Parent state has a do activity has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="37d180ab-304d-40c6-bb48-019d51ef610d">Issue where the wrong hyperlink was published in the swagger documentation has been fixed.</li></ul><h3><span>Magic </span><span>Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="5a157583-3d58-4f81-9920-169e7888e54d">Optimized retrieval of logged-in users' data in the Users application.</li><li data-uuid="b5dbc2b5-88f1-498f-af46-262cb783194c">Improved synchronization to disallow projects with the same local ID from being synchronized to the same server.</li><li data-uuid="1d27e3dd-084f-4490-884d-0122c9ac7f96">An issue breaking client and server compatibility has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="f0daeb3a-c770-439d-af4e-ee91e780eb21">Fixed an issue occurring with<em> .szip</em> exports with a large amount of used projects.</li><li data-uuid="26ad3fb6-2dd8-41e8-9eaa-f68d34c81277"><p>Fixed memory leak in Document Exporter.</p></li></ul><h3>Cameo DataHub</h3><ul><li data-uuid="89ffcc16-c797-496e-a2af-26c4104f7448">Issue related to synchronization of requirement items with a multi-valued string type attribute set to no value has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="f0964724-b557-47b9-ad86-e6725cef0152">Issue related to verification of the ENOVIA Requirements data source URL, that enables connection to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform configured to use 3dspace instead of 3DSpace has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="7612e429-a30b-4ad2-8de9-29325ff3048f">Fixed a failing use case where already synchronized items were moved from one location to another and then synchronized again. This fix applies only to platforms older than R23XFD03.</li><li data-uuid="0b3b8d33-879b-41e6-8f1b-21b080f8361a">Multiples issues related to displaying customized attributes in the DataHub Explorer Panel, mapping them, and synchronizing them have been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="ee84e3df-f5ef-44b1-ae44-afe2bc11903e">Enabled synchronization of JAMA Connect items with properties marked as mandatory and read-only for the current user.</li><li data-uuid="6b5e9b04-dc30-4e75-a3e1-19bf30c50c1a">Enabled synchronization of single- and multi-value selection list properties.</li><li data-uuid="2647234d-18c6-4f1e-808f-702fe104e65f">Added support for hieroglyph characters (Japanese, Chinese, etc.).</li><li data-uuid="9b8e5f7e-26bc-4386-8623-10a61f8a018a">Other small bug-fixes and visual improvements.</li></ul><h3>Systems Cybersecurity Designer</h3><ul><li data-uuid="2874fcae-0d56-4332-80fb-06a04d889176">Issue where the internal error occurred while generating Attach path in Attack graph in between Attack path step &amp; Failure mode/Malfunctioning Behavior has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="22cdc4c4-4287-4d5e-9ecb-9f0afc8b6e23">Issue where the library versions displayed in TARA report were incorrect has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="129eb2af-1382-4cf6-bdaf-98fec831462e">Issue where the user was not able to drop the Safety/Cybersecurity goal in value field of Cybersecurity Goal of a Threat scenario has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="998c0532-2f85-4a1c-93cb-b8f88bf30860">Issue where the user could assign invalid enumeration literals to any property has been fixed.</li></ul><p><strong>Using SCD plugin in Teamwork Cloud environment:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="644088ba-a1e7-486d-89a4-bf969f620d86"><span>Issue where the notification message was displayed for all control elements, when only one element was removed has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="170e1e9e-92ff-4126-b86d-7edb59632a4c"><span>Issue where the project related notifications were not generated in Project tab of Notification window has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="87b04e04-c428-4a9e-a1d9-3adfdb1023fe"><span>Issue where the duplicate messages were displayed in notification window has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="c42dd1ce-32eb-4d10-99f7-c223651d25f4"><span>Issue where the Risk Treatment Decision property (dependency relation locked by another user) failed to refresh automatically when modified has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="5ef2343b-9ca2-42f8-86e9-192c8e12179f"><span>Issue where the notification messages were inconsistent when the CAL value of Cybersecurity Goal was modified has been fixed.</span></li></ul><h3>Cameo Safety and Reliability Analyzer</h3><ul><li data-uuid="d7c51818-af8d-4eb1-a2f0-cf2754e5d4b2">Issue where an internal error occurred when setting ASIL value in an ISO 26262 project table has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="bc46a3ae-e5eb-471c-831f-dd42ba576e7f">Issue where a custom derived property named ASIL did not display any values, while ISO 26262 plugin was installed has been fixed.</li></ul><h3>CATIA Software Producer</h3><ul><li data-uuid="12c70525-a70d-4021-a6c8-499c7a7df334">The issues regarding the drag and drop functionlity have been resolved.</li></ul>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on November 8, 2024

#### TIP: Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Get started by selecting a product below and its version in the site header.

##### Hot Fix for version 2024x Refresh2

- 2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1 was released on May 2, 2025, for the CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272745620 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00145: (2024x Refresh2) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `272745620`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272745620/2024x+Refresh2+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:02:35.973+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 8, 2024

Cameo Collaborator is now integrated with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, enabling you to publish and view Cameo Collaborator documents directly on the platform. You can now include content from one document to another, making it easier to enrich your documents. Additionally, you can now link paragraphs to model element properties directly within Cameo Collaborator.

##### Cameo Collaborator Integration with the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We are excited to announce that with the release of version 2024x Refresh2, Cameo Collaborator is integrated with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. This enhancement expands Cameo Collaborator capabilities, allowing you to not only work with documents on Teamwork Cloud, but also publish and view them on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

##### Composing Documents from Other Documents' Content

We are excited to announce a powerful feature that provides a new way to augment Cameo Collaborator documents. You can now include content, such as diagrams and tables, from one document into another. This allows you, for example, to compose a document entirely from the content of several different documents. Depending on the selected option, the included content will be updated when the source document changes or will stay the same as it was at the time of inclusion..

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Inserting content for another document.

##### New Way to Link Paragraphs to Model Elements

In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator, we introduced the ability to link paragraphs to numerical and textual model element properties. This release adds further improvements to this functionality, allowing you to link paragraphs not only in a modeling tool but in Cameo Collaborator as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of linking a paragraph to the requirement text.

##### Other Improvements

Editable Cameo Collaborator documents are often enriched by adding images. Now, when exporting these documents to PDF or HTML formats, all attached images are included in the generated files.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='TWCloud2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272745700 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00146: (2024x Refresh2) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272745700`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272745700/2024x+Refresh2+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:03:45.439+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: November 8, 2024

In version 2024x Refresh2, Cameo DataHubintroduces numerous connector specific enhancements. Also, the release introduces various bug fixes and general enhancements.

- 
- 

- 
- 

### Enhancements

##### IBM Doors Next Generation connector

**Enhanced Backlink Support**: DataHub now fully supports the backlink between IBM DOORS NG and TWC. The OSLC resource link is now automatically retrieved from the TWC OSLC API. When the model is committed to TWC and data is synchronized between DOORS NG and the model, the **Satisfy By**link hover now provides a preview of the Collaborator resource. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Business Use Case flow with DNG' space='CDH2022xR2'].

##### JAMA Connect connector

The JAMA Connect requirements management application connector introduces several new features:

- Related Element Selection : Users can now select related elements from either the modeling application or JAMA Connect. When a synchronized item is selected in a model or on the DataHub Explorer panel, initiating the Select the related item command will highlight the corresponding element on the opposite side. This enhancement simplifies navigation between synchronized parts of the model and JAMA Connect project.
- Diagram Synchronization : You can now synchronize diagrams from JAMA Connect to the model. Diagrams are converted into images and embedded into the Text property of the selected model element. Note that synchronization is currently only supported from JAMA Connect to the model. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Synchronization' space='CDH2024xR2'] .
- Streamlined GUI : The DataHub GUI has been now enhanced to disable unsupported commands when using the JAMA Connect connector, resulting in a clearer and more user-friendly interface.

### Bug Fixes

##### JAMA Connect

- UTF-8 Synchronization Error : Fixed an issue where a UTF-8 related error occurred during data synchronization from the model to JAMA Connect in specific cases.
- Custom Item Synchronization : Resolved issues with the synchronization of custom JAMA Connect items not functioning correctly in certain scenarios.
- Link Display Issues : Fixed an issue where links between items synchronized from the model to JAMA Connect project were not displayed correctly until the project was reloaded.
- Authentication Panel : Resolved an issue that disabled the JAMA Connect data source login panel inputs when switching between available authentication methods.

##### ENOVIA Requirements

- Data Loss Prevention : Fixed an issue causing partial data loss during synchronization from the ENOVIA Requirements application to the model due to complex HTML-formatted data.
- Empty Requirement Specification : Resolved an issue preventing the creation of empty Requirement Specifications on the ENOVIA Requirements application side based on an empty package from a model. You can now synchronize an empty package and add necessary items later, either in the model or the ENOVIA Requirements application.
- 3DS Platform Support : This release now supports the 3DS 2025x platform, deployed both on the cloud and on-premise.

### Other Enhancements

- **Improved GUI for Non-UML Projects**: The DataHub GUI now automatically disables its menus when a non-UML project is open.
- Various small GUI enhancements and bug fixes have been implemented to improve overall user experience and application stability.

The OSLC resource link is now automatically retrieved from the TWC OSLC API. When the model is committed to TWC and data is synchronized between DOORS NG and the model, the <strong>Satisfy By </strong>link hover now provides a preview of the Collaborator resource. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH2022xR2" ri:content-title="Business Use Case flow with DNG" /></ac:link>.</p><h3><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">JAMA Connect connector</span></h3><p>The JAMA Connect requirements management application connector introduces several new features:</p><ul><li><strong>Related Element Selection</strong>: Users can now select related elements from either the modeling application or JAMA Connect. When a synchronized item is selected in a model or on the DataHub Explorer panel, initiating the <strong>Select the related item </strong>command will highlight the corresponding element on the opposite side. This enhancement simplifies navigation between synchronized parts of the model and JAMA Connect project.</li><li><strong>Diagram Synchronization</strong>: You can now synchronize diagrams from JAMA Connect to the model. Diagrams are converted into images and embedded into the <strong>Text </strong>property of the selected model element. Note that synchronization is currently only supported from JAMA Connect to the model. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Synchronization" ri:space-key="CDH2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</li><li><strong>Streamlined GUI</strong>: The DataHub GUI has been now enhanced to disable unsupported commands when using the JAMA Connect connector, resulting in a clearer and more user-friendly interface.</li></ul><h1>Bug Fixes</h1><h3>JAMA Connect</h3><ul><li><strong>UTF-8 Synchronization Error</strong>: Fixed an issue where a UTF-8 related error occurred during data synchronization from the model to JAMA Connect in specific cases.</li><li><strong>Custom Item Synchronization</strong>: Resolved issues with the synchronization of custom JAMA Connect items not functioning correctly in certain scenarios.</li><li><strong>Link Display Issues</strong>: Fixed an issue where links between items synchronized from the model to JAMA Connect project were not displayed correctly until the project was reloaded.</li><li><strong>Authentication Panel</strong>: Resolved an issue that disabled the JAMA Connect data source login panel inputs when switching between available authentication methods.</li></ul><h3>ENOVIA Requirements</h3><ul><li><strong>Data Loss Prevention</strong>: Fixed an issue causing partial data loss during synchronization from the ENOVIA Requirements application to the model due to complex HTML-formatted data.</li><li><strong>Empty Requirement Specification</strong>: Resolved an issue preventing the creation of empty Requirement Specifications on the ENOVIA Requirements application side based on an empty package from a model. You can now synchronize an empty package and add necessary items later, either in the model or the ENOVIA Requirements application.</li><li><strong>3DS Platform Support</strong>: This release now supports the 3DS 2025x platform, deployed both on the cloud and on-premise.</li></ul><h1>Other Enhancements</h1><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Improved GUI for Non-UML Projects</strong>: The DataHub GUI now automatically disables its menus when a non-UML project is open.</span></li><li>Various small GUI enhancements and bug fixes have been implemented to improve overall user experience and application stability.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272745524 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00151: (2024x Refresh2) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272745524`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272745524/2024x+Refresh2+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:02:30.599+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh2) 2024x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: November 8, 2024

The latest Magic Collaboration Studio release introduces branch creation directly from the web UI, simplifying project management without needing a modeling tool. New webhook support for specific project branches adds flexibility in tracking model changes, and users can now generate personal access tokens for secure, password-free CLI authentication. Additionally, conditional user groups offer dynamic access control based on SAML IdP attributes, ensuring user criteria compliance on every login.

Cameo Collaborator is now integrated with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, enabling you to publish and view Cameo Collaborator documents directly on the platform. You can now include content from one document to another, making it easier to enrich your documents. Additionally, you can now link paragraphs to model element properties directly within Cameo Collaborator.

****

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

****

- 
- 
- 
- 

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Creating Branches via Web UI

We are excited to announce a powerful new feature: the ability to create project branches directly from the web user interface! This eliminates the need to have access to a modeling tool, making it easier and faster than ever to manage your projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the resource history dialog to create project branches.

##### Webhooks for Project Branches

You can now create webhooks for the project trunk as well as for its specific branches. This enhancement allows more flexibility and control when monitoring model change events.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a branch for webhook to listen to.

##### Personal Access Token

You can now use the My Account application to generate a personal access token, providing a secure and convenient way to authenticate command-line tools. With this token, you can authenticate without needing to enter your username and password, enhancing both security and ease of use.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a personal access token in the My Account application.

##### Conditional User Groups

In certain security-related authentication scenarios it is necessary for user access rights to be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 introduces the ability to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values whenever you log in.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of enabling and defining a conditional user group.

##### Other

Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 comes with improved visual branch representation for mobile devices.

#### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

##### Cameo Collaborator Integration with the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We are excited to announce that with the release of version 2024x Refresh2, Cameo Collaborator is integrated with the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. This enhancement expands Cameo Collaborator capabilities, allowing you to not only work with documents on Teamwork Cloud, but also publish and view them on the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

##### Composing Documents from Other Documents' Content

We are excited to announce a powerful feature that provides a new way to augment Cameo Collaborator documents. You can now include content, such as diagrams and tables, from one document into another. This allows you, for example, to compose a document entirely from the content of several different documents. Depending on the selected option, the included content will be updated when the source document changes orwill stay the same as it was at the time of inclusion..

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Inserting content for another document.

##### New Way to Link Paragraphs to Model Elements

In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator, we introduced the ability to link paragraphs to numerical and textual model element properties. This release adds further improvements to this functionality, allowing you to link paragraphs not only in a modeling tool but in Cameo Collaborator as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of linking a paragraph to the requirement text.

##### Other Improvements

Editable Cameo Collaborator documents are often enriched by adding images. Now, when exporting these documents to PDF or HTML formats, all attached images are included in the generated files.

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio' space='MCS2024xR2']

**News of earlier versions**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh1 Version News' space='MCS2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Version News' space='MCS2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh2 Version News' space='MCS2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh1 Version News' space='MCS2022xR1']

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8bb10b8-912b-4eec-96f6-7e84fb5ee2d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p><h1 style="text-align: center;">Magic Collaboration Studio</h1><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Released on: November 8, 2024</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>The latest Magic Collaboration Studio release introduces branch creation directly from the web UI, simplifying project management without needing a modeling tool. New webhook support for specific project branches adds flexibility in tracking model changes, and users can now generate personal access tokens for secure, password-free CLI authentication. Additionally, conditional user groups offer dynamic access control based on SAML IdP attributes, ensuring user criteria compliance on every login.</p><p>Cameo Collaborator is now integrated with the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, enabling you to publish and view Cameo Collaborator documents directly on the platform. You can now include content from one document to another, making it easier to enrich your documents. Additionally, you can now link paragraphs to model element properties directly within Cameo Collaborator.</p><p><br /></p><hr /><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Teamwork Cloud" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Branches via Web UI" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Webhooks for Project Branches" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Personal Access Token" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Conditional User Groups" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other" /></li></ul><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Cameo Collaborator Integration with the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying Content from Another Document"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying Content from Another Document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="New Way to Link Paragraphs to Model Elements" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements" /></li></ul><hr /><p><br /></p><h2>Teamwork Cloud</h2><h3>Creating Branches via Web UI</h3><p>We are excited to announce a powerful new feature: the ability to create project branches directly from the web user interface! This eliminates the need to have access to a modeling tool, making it easier and faster than ever to manage your projects.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_branches_via_web_ui.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the resource history dialog to create project branches.</h6><h3>Webhooks for Project Branches</h3><p>You can now create webhooks for the project trunk as well as for its specific branches. This enhancement allows more flexibility and control when monitoring model change events.</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_branch_scope.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting a branch for webhook to listen to.</h6><h3>Personal Access Token</h3><p>You can now use the My Account application to generate a personal access token, providing a secure and convenient way to authenticate command-line tools. With this token, you can authenticate without needing to enter your username and password, enhancing both security and ease of use.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personal_access_token.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Generating a personal access token in the My Account application.</h6><h3>Conditional User Groups</h3><p>In certain security-related authentication scenarios it is necessary for user access rights to be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 introduces the ability to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values whenever you log in.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="conditional_user_groups.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of enabling and defining a conditional user group.</h6><h3>Other</h3><p>Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 comes with improved visual branch representation for mobile devices.</p><p><br /></p><h2>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h2><h3>Cameo Collaborator Integration with the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="52f62463-d469-405c-8423-5218a463a096">We are excited to announce that with the release of version 2024x Refresh2, Cameo Collaborator is integrated with the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="52f62463-d469-405c-8423-5218a463a096">3D</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="52f62463-d469-405c-8423-5218a463a096">EXPERIENCE platform. This enhancement expands Cameo Collaborator capabilities, allowing you to not only work with documents on Teamwork Cloud, but also publish and view them on the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="52f62463-d469-405c-8423-5218a463a096">3D</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="52f62463-d469-405c-8423-5218a463a096">EXPERIENCE platform.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="824d74ea-5e31-470d-a83b-83a161a0775d">Composing Documents from Other Documents' Content </ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a4ea05f7-a451-47fc-bc53-06baaf00e83b">We are excited to announce a powerful feature that provides a new way to augment Cameo Collaborator documents. You can now include content, such as diagrams and tables, from one document into another. This allows you, for example, to compose a document entirely from the content of several different documents. Depending on the selected option, the included content will be updated when the source document changes or </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a4ea05f7-a451-47fc-bc53-06baaf00e83b">will stay the same as it was at the time of inclusion..</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inserting_content_from_another_document.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Inserting content for another document.</h6><h3>New Way to Link Paragraphs to Model Elements</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator, we introduced the ability to link paragraphs to numerical and textual model element properties. This release adds further improvements to this functionality, allowing you to link paragraphs not only in a modeling tool but in Cameo Collaborator as well.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="linking_paragraph_to_element_property.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of linking a paragraph to the requirement text.</h6><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6aa63d05-2a7d-4a4c-a243-bcf566ac2636">Other Improvements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2bc89124-3b61-4107-9f55-a80bd7829097">Editable Cameo Collaborator documents are often enriched by adding images</ac:inline-comment-marker>.
